Title: Data Annotator for AI Models (English)
Description:
Location: Remote working in the following US States: CA, CO, DE, FL, GA, IL, IN, KS, MA, MN, NC, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NY, OK, OR, PA, TN, TX, WA, WI

Work Schedule: Full-time, Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Pacific Standard Time)

Experience: Previous experience in data annotation or content review is a plus

Education: Bachelor’s degree required

Contract duration: End of September, 2026

Compensation: 40 USD/hour




Does this sound like you?

Are you looking for remote, work-from-home jobs? Are you interested in helping to improve the reliability of today’s AI models? If yes, then this opportunity is for you!




What we are looking for!

RWS Group is looking for USA-based Data Annotators to annotate, evaluate and curate text, video and geographic data. This information will be used to train and improve generative AI and machine learning models.

This role requires close collaboration with RWS Project Managers and client stakeholders through regular video meetings to ensure alignment, quality, and continuous improvement.




Responsibilities:

- Annotate data accurately and consistently according to predefined guidelines in the required language.

- Perform basic research as needed to ensure accurate annotation.

- Provide feedback regarding observed patterns in the annotated data and contribute to continuous improvement of the data annotation process.

- Maintain high attention to detail and quality throughout the annotation process.

- Continuous learning and adaptability; willingness to provide feedback and ask questions.

- Training will be provided post-hire.

- Collaborate closely with RWS Project Managers and client stakeholders through regular video calls to discuss guidelines, quality expectations, and project updates.




Work benefits:

- Work from home, full time.

- Work-life balance — maintain your lifestyle while you work.

- Timely payments made directly to your bank account.

- Laptop will be provided.




Job Requirements

- Native-level proficiency in English. Additional language knowledge is a plus.

- Ability to work full time, from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Pacific Standard Time).

- Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience required.

- Familiarity with Excel or Google Sheets, including basic formulas.

- Good general communication skills.

- Detail-oriented with the ability to understand and follow instructions.

- Ability to meet deadlines.

- Responsible, reliable, and communicative.

- Must successfully pass a client exam and interview with the hiring team as part of the selection process.

- A personal laptop/desktop to access RWS systems.




Preferred Qualifications:

- Familiarity with the Appen Annotation Platform (ADAP) and machine learning principles.

- A broad range of interests with specific areas of expertise is a plus.

- Experience in a professional work environment.

- Previous experience with data annotation tasks.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Inventory Visibility and Controls (Hybrid, Seattle)
Description:
Job Description

The Inventory Visibility & Controls team (IV&C) was established to strengthen enterprise-wide inventory transparency, traceability, and controls, scaling data, analytics, and AI-powered insights that enable proactive decision-making, prevent loss and unsellable inventory, and support foundational capabilities such as RFID and inventory lifecycle management.

We are hiring a Senior Data Analyst for Inventory Visibility & Controls within Inventory Operations. In this role, you will help scale enterprise inventory visibility and controls by translating complex business questions into decision-ready insights and durable analytics products that strengthen traceability across the product lifecycle. In addition to delivering insights, this role will play a key part in advancing automation‑first and AI‑enabled analytics by partnering with product and engineering teams to scale real‑time visibility, anomaly detection, and human‑in‑the‑loop resolution workflows, embedding insights directly into tools that drive action—not just awareness. You will partner closely with business leaders and cross‑functional teams across program management, product, engineering, and analytics to define requirements, acquire and transform data, and deliver dashboards, analyses, and narratives that enable faster anomaly detection and resolution, reduce risk, and unlock unintentionally unsellable inventory. Additionally, you’ll contribute to a culture of data-driven decision-making through strong storytelling, stakeholder engagement, and data literacy—flexing across priorities as needed to support the team’s highest-impact visibility and controls outcomes. This role is ideal for a senior analyst who thrives at the intersection of data, operations, and controls and wants to reduce manual investigation, accelerate time to resolution, and help shift IV&C from reactive reporting to proactive, automated, and AI‑assisted inventory controls at enterprise scale.

A day in the life…


Own and lead the delivery of high‑impact, production‑ready data products that support enterprise inventory visibility, anomaly detection, and controls across the inventory lifecycle—including complex analyses, scalable dashboards, and executive‑level insights—across ongoing and ambiguous, high‑stakes problem spaces.
Translate loosely defined business questions into clear analytical strategies, independently designing end‑to‑end approaches that identify the right data, methods, trade‑offs, and success measures.
Partner with product, data science, and engineering teams to shape AI‑enabled analytics, including anomaly detection, prioritization, and recommendation experiences that support faster, more consistent inventory issue resolution.
Contribute to human‑in‑the‑loop AI workflows, ensuring analytical logic is explainable, auditable, and aligned with inventory control and financial integrity standards as automation scales.
Design and maintain trusted analytical assets by driving standards for data modeling, metric definitions, data quality, and governance—ensuring insights are durable, explainable, and aligned to one source of truth.
Lead analytical storytelling for complex and conflicting findings, connecting data across domains to provide context, highlight risks and opportunities, and influence decisions at multiple leadership levels.
Anticipate downstream impacts and stakeholder reactions, effectively landing insights that may challenge assumptions while maintaining trust and momentum toward action.
Mentor and elevate junior analysts, contributing to shared best practices, technical standards, and team learning to raise the quality and consistency of analytical work.
Continuously identify opportunities to improve analytics tooling, architecture, and processes, with a focus on automation, AI‑assisted insights, and scalable self‑service experiences that move the organization from reactive reporting to proactive control.


You own this if you have…


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Engineering); advanced degree or relevant certification strongly preferred.
5+ years of professional experience delivering advanced analytics, data products, and business insights in complex, cross‑functional environments.
Expert‑level SQL skills, with experience writing clean, repeatable, production‑ready code and operating in production analytics environments including version control, deployment standards, and data quality monitoring.
Advanced experience acquiring, transforming, joining, and blending data across multiple internal and external sources, with deep understanding of data structures, quality considerations, and trade‑offs.
Expert knowledge of at least one programming language (e.g., Python, R) to support analysis, modeling, automation, and production workflows; working knowledge of additional languages and tools.
Proven ability to design and execute end‑to‑end analytical workflows, applying advanced statistical, analytical, and (where applicable) predictive techniques to solve ambiguous business problems.
Experience designing or supporting automated analytics, monitoring, or alerting solutions, with exposure to AI‑assisted or conversational analytics in enterprise self‑service or investigation workflows.
Exceptional analytical storytelling and communication skills, with a track record of influencing decisions by clearly articulating insights, risks, opportunities, and trade‑offs to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Advanced data visualization expertise (e.g., Looker or similar tools), including the ability to design cohesive dashboard suites, elevate visualization standards, and deliver intuitive, self‑service experiences at scale.
Strong business acumen with the ability to deeply understand domain‑specific processes, metrics, and systems, and proactively identify where analytics can drive the greatest impact.
Demonstrated ability to build trusted partnerships with senior stakeholders, operate as a strategic thought partner, and independently drive work forward in highly ambiguous environments.
Experience with data governance, metric standardization, and production analytics best practices strongly preferred.
Experience with inventory, merchandising, supply chain, or operational data and KPIs is a plus.


Pay Range Details

The pay range(s) below has been provided in compliance with state specific laws. Pay ranges may be different for other locations.

Pay offers are dependent on the location, as well as job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.

$121,000.00 - $199,000.00 Annual

We’ve got you covered…

Benefits

Our employees are our most important asset and that’s reflected in our benefits. Nordstrom is proud to offer a variety of benefits to support employees and their families, including:


Medical/Vision, Dental, Retirement and Paid Time Away
Life Insurance and Disability
Merchandise Discount and EAP Resources


This position may be eligible for performance-based incentives/bonuses. Benefits include 401k, medical/vision/dental/life/disability insurance options, PTO accruals, Holidays, and more. Eligibility requirements may apply based on location, job level, classification, and length of employment. Learn more in the Nordstrom Benefits Overview by copying and pasting the following URL into your browser: https://careers.nordstrom.com/pdfs/Ben_Overview_17-19.pdf

A few more important points...

The job posting highlights the most critical responsibilities and requirements of the job. It’s not all-inclusive. There may be additional duties, responsibilities and qualifications for this job.

For Los Angeles or San Francisco applicants: Nordstrom is required to inform you that we conduct background checks after conditional offer and consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with legal requirements per Los Angeles, Cal. Muni. Code 189.04 and the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. For additional state and location specific notices, please refer to the Legal Notices document within the FAQ section of the Nordstrom Careers site.

Applicants with disabilities who require assistance or accommodation should contact the nearest Nordstrom location, which can be identified at www.nordstrom.com.

Please be mindful that there may be legal notices and requirements related to this job posting that are specific to your state. Review the Career Site FAQ’s for relevant information and guidelines.

Current Nordstrom employees: To apply, log into Workday, click the Careers button and then click Find Jobs.

Nordstrom keeps job postings open for at least one day after the posting date.

Title: BI Analyst II
Description:
Purpose Brands, the parent company of Orangetheory, Anytime Fitness, Waxing the City, and The Bar Method, is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst II, to join its team. This is a great position for someone who is looking to expand their career, and join a company with a fun, fast-paced and inspirational culture.

As part of our Analytics team, this data and analytics professional analyzes business questions, assesses the impact of business initiatives. They think critically, are mathematically savvy, know how to query data, and apply analytical methods to derive insights. They work with our data engineers to augment datasets to enable dashboarding, reports, and operationalizing insights. They partner with business stakeholders to help them make use of analysis results and deploy data-informed decision-making processes.

Purpose/Impact: (Duties & Essential Functions)

Reporting & Analytics


Analyze business questions and communicate findings through briefs, presentations, reports or other media. Clarify and document the questions, how findings may be used, and the analysis approach.
Conduct impact analyses, leading to a deeper understanding of business performance, the member experience and fitness results. Guidance on approach provided by senior analyst or BI Manager.
Develop simple deterministic models that project outcomes based on observed relationships, e.g., break-even projections, simple systems models.
Develop reports and dashboards for an entire problem space, such as website performance, lead conversion or studio portfolio analysis. These are product features that are supported and evolved over time as opposed to one-off analyses.
Develop internal and external documentation and training on Analytics products, such as dashboards, reports, KPI's, source data collection and transformation, data lineage and suggested use cases.
Develop end-user applications with low-code tools to surface actionable analytics insights, collect data or execute analytics driven workflows/actions.
Work with data engineers to augment existing datasets with metrics and categorizations that make it easier to answer common business and analysis questions and publish appropriate reports.
Build the company knowledge base of data-informed insights, documenting findings and promoting and communicating those findings through appropriate internal channels.


Performance Expectations


Able to develop or collaborate with a data engineer on the design (metrics, attributes) of “last mile” datasets for analyzing common questions in a specific business domain.
Well versed in most of the functionality our BI platforms offer and able to make efficient use of them.
Uses a toolkit of analytic methods, e.g., variance analysis, sampling, establishing correlations, methods of analyzing causality to answer business questions with data.
Able to ask questions to fully understand the context and business question asked and how answering it will make a difference. Occasionally still needs help from senior analyst or manager to frame the problem.
Chooses or proposes a path of inquiry that is logical and coherent.
Follows applicable COE (Center of Excellence) tenets and methodologies when analyzing business questions or developing reports and dashboards.
Makes accurate inferences from the data most of the time. Still needs some help from more senior analysts or manager to validate conclusions.
Presents findings in an easy-to-understand way for business stakeholders, using COE practices.
Shares and documents approach and findings, making sure the rest of the team has visibility and can build on them.
Is recognized by business stakeholders as contributing to their decision-making processes or improving their operations.
Regularly reviews work with more senior analysts, seeks to learn and incorporates learnings into work products.


Background and Strengths


BS/BA in computer science, statistics, mathematics, business administration, economics, or related field with a focus on logic and quantitative analysis.
3-5 years of experience in reporting and analysis. Experience with implementing, maintaining or developing reporting in Power BI, Tableau or comparable platforms is a plus.
Basic understanding of statistics (sampling, statistical significance).
Proficiency in querying and manipulating data using SQL. Know how to use subqueries, window and analytic functions, regular expressions.
Experience with Python, R or similar languages.


Strengths


Well organized. Thinks about how to do the work, then acts. Seeks to contextualize the work.
Demonstrates understanding of which analytic methods to use to answer certain types of questions.
Good interpersonal skills, seeks to understand others’ viewpoints, motivations, ways of thinking.
Communicates clearly and knows best practices for presentations.
Shows curiosity about the industry and can explain how our brands are differentiated.


Salary Range: $90k - $115k

What’s in it for you?

We offer a competitive salary along with exceptional benefits such as:


Medical, Dental and Vision Coverage
Hybrid Work Environment
Life and Disability Insurance
Unlimited Time off + Paid Holidays
Flexible Friday's between Memorial Day and Labor Day
401(K) Savings Plan Matching at 4%
10 Coaching and Therapy sessions
Mental Health Benefits
Brand Discounts & Reimbursements
In-house workout facilities
Professional Development Opportunities
Team Building, Employee Engagement Activities & so much more


WORK SCHEDULE

Purpose Brands LLC, Currently Observe The Following Hybrid Work Model For Employees At Our Boca Raton (FL), Woodbury (MN), And Seattle (WA) Offices


Remote optional: Fridays
On-site days: Mondays, Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays


DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION STATEMENT

Purpose Brands is committed to encouraging, facilitating, and upholding an environment centered on diversity, equity, and inclusion across every facet of the Purpose Brands. We will work to create a sustainable culture that supports a healthy space for learning and growing, valuing, and empowering every employee, inspiring a diverse franchise network, and uplifting the members and communities we serve.

EEO STATEMENT

Purpose Brands provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal, or local law. Discrimination of any type will not be tolerated.

Title: Analyst Performance Analytics - Dedicated
Description:
What You Need To Know

Shape a remarkable future with us. Build a career working for an industry leader that truly invests in their people – and equips them with leading technology, continuous learning, and the ability to bring their best selves to work. As the preeminent wine and spirits distributor, Southern Glazer’s isn't just one of Forbes’ Top Private Companies; it's a family-owned business with deep roots dating back to 1933.

Southern Glazer’s offers a competitive compensation package with expected first year total earnings of $61,500-81,500 / year including bonus. In determining a final offer, the company will evaluate a specific candidate's education, skills and experience and will make an offer appropriately.

Southern Glazer’s is proud of its well-earned positive reputation, continually achieving accolades for our outstanding workplace culture. We take pride in creating a culture where our people are valued, supported, and provided opportunities for growth and belonging.

As a full-time employee, you can choose from a wide-ranging menu of our Top Shelf Benefits, including comprehensive medical and prescription drug coverage, dental and vision plans, tax-saving Flexible Spending Accounts, disability coverage, life insurance plans, and a 401(k) plan. We also offer tuition assistance, a wellness program, parental leave, vacation accrual, paid sick leave, and more.

By joining Southern Glazer’s, you would be part of a team that values excellence, innovation, and community. This is more than just a job – it's an opportunity to build the future of beverage distribution and grow with a company that truly cares about its people.

Overview

Supports a variety of activities including, but not limited to, incentives, SPP Objectives, and programs.

Primary Responsibilities


Log incentive parameters by meeting with trade partners or suppliers to establish and document the specifics of incentive programs, including brands, target audience, performance metrics, and time periods
Review and approve incentive parameters, ensuring any adjustments receive approval from leadership, trade partners, and/or suppliers before entry into the goaling tool
Enter incentive goaling criteria into a goaling tool to set incentive goals
Track ongoing incentive attainment by developing reporting (e.g., Exec Summary, Leaderboard, etc.)
Track ongoing incentive attainment and monitor progress
Create final recap of incentive results, including payout calculations
Provide final incentive recap to leadership/supplier for payout approval
Consolidate and send all approved incentive recaps to payroll
Review all approved incentive recaps and chargeback supplier (if supplier funded)
Send approved payout to payrollServe as point of contact for questions regarding sales performance models
Plan monthly SPP objectives by determining objectives for each RTM Goal Segment in line with Goal Strategy and ensure supplier contractual quotas are met
Enter SPP objective criteria into goaling tool
Track ongoing SPP objective attainment using existing SPP Dashboard and other tools (e.g., SPP Management Report, EOM Recap, etc.) and maintain secondary excel files for leadership
Develop and track sales performance goals and attainment for national programs


Additional Primary Responsibilities

Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in related field; or an equivalent combination of education and experience
1 or more years of relevant analytical, and/or commercial work, consulting, project management experience is required
Work experience in a data intensive position using Microsoft Excel
Communicate clearly and effectively in writing and verbally
Able to obtain and meet industry licensing requirements as needed
Critical nature of this job may require extended hours, overtime, and weekends


Physical Demands


Physical demands with activity or condition for a considerable amount of time may include sitting and typing/keyboarding using a computer (e.g., keyboard, mouse, and monitor) or mobile device
Physical demands with activity or condition may include walking, bending, reaching, standing, squatting, and stooping
May require occasional lifting/lowering, pushing, carrying, or pulling up to 20lbs


EEO Statement

Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits, an Affirmative Action/EEO employer, prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type and provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training. SGWS complies with all federal, state and local laws concerning consideration of a qualified applicant's arrest and/or criminal conviction records. Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits provides competitive compensation based on estimated performance level consistent with the past relevant experience, knowledge, skills, abilities and education of employees. Unless otherwise expressly stated, any pay ranges posted here are estimates from outside of Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits and do not reflect Southern Glazer's pay bands or ranges.

If you have any questions or concerns about whether this posting complies/adheres with local pay transparency requirements, please contact the SGWS talent acquisition team at NationalTA@sgws.com

Title: Associate
Description:
Riff City Strategies is a mission-driven communications firm working at the intersection of climate, emerging technology, and public policy. We build movements and tell stories that help our clients, from VC-backed startups and foundations to social justice campaigns and government leaders, create change that matters.




We tackle the most challenging issues facing our society and planet – our firm believes in the power of advocacy and communication as tools for change. Our work has helped shift hundreds of millions of dollars to BIPOC-led groups fighting for climate justice, brought landmark military justice reforms into law with Protect Our Defenders, and supported community-based public safety efforts with Urban Alchemy.




We’ve led launches for the Mercedes-Benz High-Power Charging Network, Sam Altman’s World Network, and some of the most pivotal democracy, justice, and AI governance initiatives of our time — helping government leaders, VC-backed startups, and major foundations build trust and momentum in moments that matter.




Our culture is rooted in integrity, creativity, and results. That commitment has earned us international recognition:

PRWeek Purpose Awards -Small Organization of the Year 
Fast Company - Most Innovative Companies in the World 
Bulldog PR - Best Boutique Agency




The Opportunity

We are looking for a sharp and self-starting Associate with a passion for media relations, storytelling, and public affairs. The ideal candidate is a creative, detail-oriented story-teller, driven to make an impact, and excited to work on campaigns that shape policy, launch technologies, and shift culture.




You’re a confident writer and communicator who can deliver top-tier work across formats, from press releases to long-form thought leadership. You know how to manage projects, and you’re fearless when it comes to pitching and delivering results.




Working with us means you’ll be surrounded by high-quality teammates and learn something new every day. Riff has a positive, team-oriented culture where you’ll be challenged, continue to grow, and laugh a lot. We take our work seriously but not ourselves — you’ll get to do meaningful work with a team that values creativity, purpose, and collaboration.




Riff is a remote-first organization with the team working PT hours and primarily based in the San Francisco Bay Area and Los Angeles. For this role, we’re prioritizing California-based applicants. This position reports to a senior member of the Riff team.




Responsibilities:

Get to know our clients inside and out — their mission, products, challenges, and the people and trends shaping their industries
Pitch media and conduct community outreach on behalf of clients
Build and maintain media lists for multiple clients 
Draft a wide variety of communications materials (press releases, pitches, statements, blog posts, newsletters, thought leadership pieces, etc.)
Support management of client teams through drafting agendas, recaps, and coverage reports
Monitoring news trends 
Conduct miscellaneous research for clients as needed
Provide general business support, which could include updating the Riff City Strategies' website, drafting newsletters, posting to company’s social channels or contributing to new business efforts




Required Qualifications:

1-3 years of experience in public relations, public affairs, or corporate communications, with a proven track record of success in media relations and storytelling
Exceptional media relations – comfortable and confident pitching compelling stories directly to top-tier reporters
Passionate storyteller dedicated to amplifying the voices of organizations advancing climate action, social justice, and mission-driven technology
Highly organized with excellent prioritization and time management abilities to efficiently handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment
Self-motivated and adaptable with strong initiative to take ownership of tasks while being receptive to feedback and guidance
Top-notch research, proofreading, writing, and editing skills that ensure accuracy and clarity in all communications
Outgoing, personable, and generally nice! We like to have fun and get into good trouble!
Proficiency with digital tools, including G Suite, Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, with an openness to learning new platforms as needed
Note: While remote, we are specifically looking for CA-based applicants

Preferred Qualifications:

Existing media contacts/relationships
2+ years of PR/agency experience




The Details

Riff City is best-in-class, and this extends to our employee-centric company culture and benefits. Our perks are unrivaled, like our team and clients! We provide big company benefits (competitive salary, healthcare, a personal development stipend for business, internet and phone reimbursement, 401(k) with matching, quarterly profit sharing, half-day Fridays every other week during the summer + more) while maintaining the size and energy of a startup.




Riff is a remote-first organization with the team primarily based in the San Francisco Bay Area. The annual salary range for this position is $65,000-$68,500 per year. We believe in action from the ground up — whether that’s helping accelerate clean energy or supporting changemakers in the field. If you thrive on purpose, collaboration, and creativity, you’ll fit right in.




Riff City Strategies, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics.

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About The Company

Focus Group Panel is a leading market research firm dedicated to providing insightful and actionable data to clients across various industries. Our company specializes in designing and conducting qualitative and quantitative research studies to help organizations understand consumer behavior, market trends, and competitive landscapes. With a commitment to excellence and innovation, Focus Group Panel leverages advanced methodologies and cutting-edge technology to deliver accurate and timely insights. Our team is composed of experienced professionals who are passionate about helping clients make informed decisions that drive growth and success. We pride ourselves on maintaining high standards of integrity, confidentiality, and customer satisfaction in every project we undertake.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Market Research Analyst to join our dynamic team at FocusGroupPanel. In this role, you will be responsible for designing research projects, analyzing data, and delivering comprehensive reports to clients. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical skills, excellent communication abilities, and a keen understanding of market research techniques. You will collaborate closely with clients to understand their needs, develop tailored research strategies, and provide actionable insights that support their business objectives. This position offers an excellent opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment, contribute to impactful projects, and grow your career within the market research industry.

Qualifications

The ideal candidate should have a bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Statistics, or a related field. A minimum of 2-3 years of experience in market research or data analysis is preferred. Strong proficiency in data analysis tools such as SPSS, SAS, or R, along with advanced skills in Excel, is essential. Excellent written and verbal communication skills are required to effectively present findings and insights. The candidate must demonstrate strong problem-solving abilities, attention to detail, and the capacity to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Familiarity with survey design, focus group moderation, and qualitative research methodologies will be considered an advantage.

Responsibilities

As a Market Research Analyst at FocusGroupPanel, your key responsibilities will include:


Designing and implementing research methodologies tailored to client needs, including surveys, focus groups, and interviews.
Collecting, analyzing, and interpreting quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends and patterns.
Developing comprehensive reports and presentations that clearly communicate research findings and strategic recommendations.
Collaborating with clients to understand their objectives and providing expert guidance throughout the research process.
Managing multiple research projects simultaneously while adhering to deadlines and quality standards.
Staying updated with industry trends, new research techniques, and emerging tools to continuously improve research quality and efficiency.
Ensuring data accuracy, confidentiality, and compliance with ethical standards.


Benefits

FocusGroupPanel offers a competitive benefits package designed to support our employees' well-being and professional growth. Employees enjoy comprehensive health insurance coverage, including medical, dental, and vision plans. We provide generous paid time off, including vacation, personal days, and holidays, to promote work-life balance. Our company invests in ongoing training and development programs to enhance skills and career advancement. Additionally, employees have access to a collaborative and inclusive work environment that encourages innovation and teamwork. We also offer performance-based incentives and opportunities for advancement within the organization, ensuring our team members are recognized and rewarded for their contributions.

Equal Opportunity

FocusGroupPanel is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We believe that a diverse team enhances creativity, innovation, and overall company success. All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply, and we are dedicated to providing a fair and equitable hiring process for all applicants.

Title: Business Intelligence & Data Specialist
Description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This Position reports to:

Business Intelligence Manager




As a Business Intelligence & Data Specialist, you will prioritize data quality and management as the foundation of all business intelligence operations. You'll ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across all systems while addressing technical issues and supporting emergency resolutions. Your expertise will enable you to create and maintain ad-hoc and automated reports that deliver actionable insights. You'll also maintain comprehensive documentation and standardization to ensure consistency and usability, while managing access controls and security to meet compliance requirements. This role is essential for enabling stakeholders to make data-driven decisions.

This role is contributing to the Electrification, Installation Products Division in Canada and is located in Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu.

The work model is hybrid, with 2 days per week at the office.


Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets; query databases using SQL to extract, validate, and transform data while ensuring accuracy and consistency across all BI systems
Identify and implement data quality standards; implement anomaly detection and data governance principles
Serve as the front-line point of contact for BI bugs and technical issues; perform investigations and diagnose application problems
Support IT teams and vendors in resolving emergencies and provide business/process knowledge to expedite resolution
Create and maintain reports tailored to business needs; design interactive dashboards that enable data-driven decision-making
Optimize report performance and support ad-hoc reporting requests for stakeholders
Maintain comprehensive BI documentation including processes, procedures, and system configurations; standardize BI interfaces across systems
Document best practices and create training materials for end users; maintain security group reviews and access controls
Support compliance and governance requirements related to data access; identify improvements to enhance access management efficiency and security



Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, or related field
3+ years of hands-on experience with multiple BI platforms and tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Bilingual French-English. English is necessary to communicate with clients outside Quebec.
Advanced proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation; strong experience with data analysis, data quality management, and data quality tools
Strong knowledge of database management systems, data governance frameworks, and compliance principles
Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in data environments with strong problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and documentation skills with strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Customer service orientation with proven ability to support end users and cross-functional stakeholders
Strong ability to manage multiple priorities, respond to urgent issues, and collaborate effectively with IT teams.





Employment Equity & Inclusion Statement (Canada)

ABB values the dedication, commitment, and expertise of all our employees. As an Employment Equity Employer, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We actively support the principles of the Employment Equity Act and strive to build a workforce that reflects Canada’s diversity, including:


Women
Indigenous Peoples
Members of visible minorities
Persons with disabilities


ABB is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know.

Title: Data Analytics Training and Internship Program
Description:
Company Description

Ededge is a ground-breaking organization dedicated to nurturing the potential of our youth and empowering them to explore the vast world of technology and other high-growth fields. Our upcoming initiatives will provide invaluable learning experiences by allowing audiences to gain insights from accomplished leaders, who will share not only their successes but also their journeys through failure. Despite our accomplishments, we remain dedicated to continuous growth, as our ultimate goal is to advance collectively as an organization and as a community. We elevate the industry of Ed-Tech by bringing in the training in the specific fields along with the industry experience.




Visit Our Website to know about us : www.ededgelearning.com




Role Description

This is a remote Data Analytics Training and Internship program role designed for individuals seeking practical experience while developing core analytical skills. Day-to-day activities include working with datasets to clean, organize, and validate data, as well as supporting data modeling and basic statistical analysis tasks. The role involves assisting with the creation of reports, dashboards, and visualizations that help communicate insights to internal stakeholders and mentors. Participants will follow a structured learning path, attend virtual training sessions, receive feedback on assigned projects, and collaborate with peers on team-based analytical exercises. The program is ideal for individuals looking to transition into data analytics or strengthen their capabilities while balancing other academic or professional commitments.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field
0–1 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or a related role
Basic proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets
Introductory knowledge of SQL or databases
Strong attention to detail and analytical thinking
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, essential for effective collaboration and articulation of complex ideas.
Demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a cohesive team.




Perks & Growth Opportunities

Hands-on experience working on high-impact analytics projects.
Access to expert mentorship and professional development resources.
Potential for performance-based incentives during the internship.
Opportunities for full-time roles may be offered to exceptional interns.
A valuable addition to your portfolio and resume with real-world experience.




Location: Remote




Note : This is an unpaid internship. Upon application, our team will contact you to proceed with the application details and joining process.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Key Responsibilities:

Data Infrastructure development and maintenance:

Maintain scalable data pipelines and architectures using Databricks and other cloud-based platforms
Maintain and implement robust ETL processes to ensure data quality and integrity across various research data sources

Collaboration and Support:

Work closely with Portfolio Managers, analysts and the research team to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that support user objectives
Collaborate with IT and other engineering teams to integrate data solutions into the broader technology ecosystem

Vendor Management:

Manage relationships with research vendors, ensuring data acquisition aligns with business needs and compliance standards
Evaluate and onboard new data vendors, negotiating contracts and ensuring service level agreements are met

Technical Expertise:

Utilize Python and other programming languages to develop data models, scripts, and tools that enhance data processing capabilities
Apply knowledge of AI and machine learning to support advanced analytics and predictive modeling efforts utilizing Cinctive’s AI framework




Qualifications:

4+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on hedge funds or financial research environments
Strong technical skillset including expertise using PySpark on Databricks, Python, Kusto/KQL optimization
Familiarity with Tableau, PowerBI, and data warehousing/data lake design
Alt Data experience with a background working with different alt-data source is a plus
Familiarity with AI and machine learning frameworks is a plus
Strong understanding of financial markets, hedge fund operations, and research methodologies
Experience in managing and integrating data from various research vendors, as well as vendor management
Ability to perform high-level work both independently and as part of a team
Excellent organizational and time management skills
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment




Education:

Required: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field




Compensation:

The salary range for this position is $175,000 to $200,000 per year. The actual base pay is dependent upon several factors, including, but not limited to, relevant experience, business needs and market demands. This role may also be eligible for bonus compensation and employee benefits.

Title: Business Systems Analyst, Data Governance
Description:
ORION GROUPS is a professional services firm based in McKinney, Texas that provides experienced consultants to key organizations across the United States. We focus our talent expertise in both the financial and healthcare industries. Our capabilities in technology include application management, enterprise software systems, data strategies, integration, and IT infrastructure. We work diligently to listen and provide value to your unique needs. www.OrionGRPS.com




OVERVIEW

At Orion, we are seeking a Business Systems Analyst to assist the Business Architects on key initiatives, mapping the current and future state of business processes, people and technology, and hitting project milestones through multiple phases. This opportunity would drive the day-to-day deliverables for defensible deletion, partner projects with Data Security, Metric builds, and process documentation.




OPPORTUNITY

6-month contract; Fully Remote (U.S. – Central Time Zone)




SALARY

$115,000 – $130,000 W2 plus benefits




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Build data visualizations to support projects and track ILM Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).




Gathers information from business units and translates them to current work processes within data security and governance tools (key foundational artifacts for build or modification of Data Policy, standards, procedures, ontology maps, and data models).




Reconstructing project provenance following a system upgrade and maturing our tracking to ensure every piece of data shared with external partners (HIEs, Research, etc.) is fully governed, authorized, and documented.




Create a "traceability matrix" that links a signed contract ID to a ServiceNow Project ID and a specific technical interface endpoint.




Maps and enhances existing business processes/functions and recommends changes required to fit a tightly integrated system




Designs and implements projects requiring systems integration, small teams and multiple technical platforms




Assist in maturing governance process around data sharing platform




Collaborates on driving improvement activities to achieve department and organizational goals




Coordinates with functional team to assure consistency and optimization of overall work product




KEY EXPERIENCE

Experience with visualization software such as Looker, Tableau, etc.




Experience with Logic & Querying (SQL, BigQuery) to pull reports from BigQuery to find the “orphaned” data from ServiceNow upgrade




Interoperability standards: basic understanding of healthcare data exchange (HL7, FHIR, or EDI) to accurately map "Data Elements" mentioned in a contract to actual fields in an API or interface.




Data Governance Frameworks: familiarity with the concept of Data Provenance, knowing not just where data is, but its origin and the authority by which it is being shared.




Experience working in a healthcare environment preferred




Experience working in Data Governance preferred




General knowledge of hospital operations, cloud technologies (GCP), contracting, data sharing (HIEs, registries, etc.) the interrelationship of systems, and how technology enables business processes




Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Mathematics or other related major and 2 years of applicable cumulative job specific experience




INTERVIEW PROCESS

Quick turnaround: preliminary interview followed by team interview.




Typically, one round with the hiring manager and team, possibly two rounds if needed.







Orion Groups is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other characteristic protected by law. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact Orion@OrionGRPS.com

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We’re building a world of health around every individual — shaping a more connected, convenient and compassionate health experience. At CVS Health®, you’ll be surrounded by passionate colleagues who care deeply, innovate with purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize safety and quality in everything we do. Join us and be part of something bigger – helping to simplify health care one person, one family and one community at a time.

Position Summary: Caremark LLC, a CVS Health company, is hiring for the following role in Irving, TX: Data Engineer to analyze data engineering problems and develop, build and manage large-scale data structures, pipelines and efficient Extract/Load/Transform (ETL) workflows to address complex problems and support business applications. Duties include: develop large scale data structures and pipelines to organize, collect and standardize data to generate insights and addresses reporting needs; write ETL (Extract/Transform/Load) processes, design database systems, and develop tools for real-time and offline analytic processing that improve existing systems and expand capabilities; collaborate with Data Science team to transform data and integrate algorithms and models into automated processes; test and maintain systems and troubleshoot malfunctions; leverage knowledge of Hadoop architecture, HDFS commands, and designing and optimizing queries to build data pipelines; utilize programming skills in Python, Java, or similar languages to build robust data pipelines and dynamic systems; build data marts and data models to support Data Science and other internal customers; integrate data from a variety of sources and ensure adherence to data quality and accessibility standards; analyze current information technology environments to identify and assess critical capabilities and recommend solutions to complex business problems; and experiment with available tools and advise on new tools to provide optimal solutions that meet the requirements dictated by the model/use case. Telecommuting available. Multiple positions.

Requirements: Master’s degree (or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Business Analytics, Computer Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field and one (1) year of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Also requires one (1) year of experience with each of the following:

XML, JSON, HTML, CSS, or JavaScript;

Agile methodologies or SAFe Software Development Principles;

React, jQuery, Ajax, or Bootstrap;

JIRA, Rally, or Confluence;

Creation of SQL queries and procedures to extract data based on business requirements;

Writing application code and deploying to production;

Developing backend services, performing code reviews, and collaborating with peers on software development solutions; and

Software development best practices.

Pay Range: $114379.00/year to $144200.00/year.

This pay range represents the base hourly rate or base annual full-time salary for all positions in the job grade within which this position falls. The actual base salary offer will depend on a variety of factors including experience, education, geography and other relevant factors. This position is eligible for a CVS Health bonus, commission or short-term incentive program in addition to the base pay range listed above. This position also includes an award target in the company’s equity award program.

In addition to your compensation, enjoy the rewards of an organization that puts our heart into caring for our colleagues and our communities. The Company offers a full range of medical, dental, and vision benefits. Eligible employees may enroll in the Company’s 401(k) retirement savings plan, and an Employee Stock Purchase Plan is also available for eligible employees. The Company provides a fully-paid term life insurance plan to eligible employees, and short-term and long term disability benefits. CVS Health also offers numerous well-being programs, education assistance, free development courses, a CVS store discount, and discount programs with participating partners. As for time off, Company employees enjoy Paid Time Off (“PTO”) or vacation pay, as well as paid holidays throughout the calendar year. Number of paid holidays, sick time and other time off are provided consistent with relevant state law and Company policies.

Great Benefits For Great People

We take pride in offering a comprehensive and competitive mix of pay and benefits that reflects our commitment to our colleagues and their families.

This full‑time position is eligible for a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the physical, emotional, and financial well‑being of colleagues and their families. The benefits for this position include medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, retirement savings options, wellness programs, and other resources, based on eligibility.

Additional details about available benefits are provided during the application process and on Benefits Moments.

This job does not have an application deadline, as CVS Health accepts applications on an ongoing basis.

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state and local laws.

Title: Tableau Consultant
Description:
Tableau Consultant

 

We are currently seeking an experienced BI professional with 3+ years of Tableau and data visualization experience. This candidate must possess technical, business analysis, and communication skills. This position offers an opportunity to work directly with business teams to design strategic solutions that visualize complex data in a focused manner. This individual will work closely with customers to gather requirements, prototype designs, and develop the final deliverable. Familiarity with the Tableau architecture and best practices is a must.

 

Position Requirements:

 

* 3+ years experience with implementing Tableau solutions

* Experience with the Tableau Desktop & Tableau Prep

* Expertise in dashboard and data visualization design

* Advanced skills in data analysis and data discovery

* Experience with Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud

* Tableau Administration experience with security & permissions

* Experience with Salesforce preferred

* Strong communication skills: ability to lead IT architecture design sessions and/or business dashboard working sessions

* Strong SQL, relational database, & data warehousing skills

* Experience with REST APIs is a plus

* Industry experience in Retail, Finance, Supply Chain, Pharmaceuticals, and/or Manufacturing

* Agile development experience is a plus

* Experience with Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks, Azure SQL Database is a plus

* Experience with Matillion, Fivetran, Azure Data Factory, AWS Glue is a plus

 

XeoMatrix is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation or any other classification protected by Federal, State, or local law. Employment decisions are based solely on qualifications, merit, and business need.

Title: Delivery Support Analyst
Description:
Level Access is a leader in digital accessibility, dedicated to creating inclusive digital experiences for everyone. 

As an Associate Delivery Support Analyst, you are responsible for triaging, routing, or resolving delivery-related support tickets to ensure efficient and timely service. This position involves working closely with internal teams and stakeholders to address issues and maintain operational excellence. You will follow established processes and guidelines to ensure smooth ticket management and delivery. Your main priority is to expertly understand the content of the ticket and route or respond to it quickly and efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

Ticket Management and Triage: 

Review incoming support tickets related to delivery operations, categorize and prioritize them based on predefined criteria, and escalate issues that require specialized handling or fall outside standard guidelines
Assign tickets to appropriate teams or individuals based on issue complexity and urgency, ensuring compliance with SLAs
Monitor ticket queues regularly to ensure timely responses and resolutions; alerting team leads to any delays that may affect service timelines
Respond to ticket handling questions from internal teams and external partners using established workflows, escalating non-standard issues as needed 

Customer Communication

Serve as the first point of contact for internal teams and clients when issues are raised, maintaining clear and consistent communication throughout the ticket resolution process
Provide stakeholders with regular updates on the progress of tickets, ensuring transparency and helping manage expectations
Recognize unresolved or critical issues that require escalation to senior analysts or management and raise these concerns promptly

Quality Control

Perform quality checks on completed tickets or tasks, ensuring they meet established guidelines before finalizing or moving forward in the workflow
Ensure tickets handled by internal teams or external partners adhere to defined workflow standards and routing expectations
Ensure work is rerouted appropriately when it fails to meet quality standards, escalating to senior analysts as necessary
Regularly verify that processes and workflows are being followed as designed, flagging any inconsistencies or inefficiencies to leadership for review

 

Key Skills
Detail-Oriented: Strong attention to detail in ticket management and ensuring accuracy in task categorization and routing
Collaboration and Communication: Comfortable communicating effectively with team members and stakeholders across departments, both verbally and in writing
Technical Aptitude: A willingness to learn and work within workflow automation, ticketing, and project management systems
Problem-Solving: Identifies issues in workflows or processes and exercises sound judgement to recognize when support is needed and flagging to leadership
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in business administration, information systems, supply chain, or related field (or equivalent experience) 
Strong organizational and multitasking skills
Proficiency with ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow) or a willingness to learn
Basic proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook)
Exposure to tools such as Salesforce, Zendesk, or workflow automation platforms is an asset
Application Process

If you thrive in a fast‑paced environment and you are eager to make a significant impact in the field of accessibility, we would love to hear from you!

This is a full‑time, salaried position offering a competitive benefits package, including bonus opportunities, generous paid time off, paid holidays, and a range of programs designed to support employee well‑being and work‑life balance.Interested in immediate consideration? Please submit your resume to be considered for this role.

Level Access is committed to workforce diversity and is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations throughout the hiring process.

Level Access uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support and enhance our recruitment and hiring processes. These tools may assist with activities such as résumé screening, skills assessments, interview scheduling, and communication These tools are designed to assist our recruiters and do not make final hiring decisions. All AI-assisted processes are subject to human review and candidate information is handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy and applicable regulations.To learn more about how we collect and use personal data, please review our Privacy Statement (https://www.levelaccess.com/privacy-statement) and our Trust Center (https://trust.levelaccess.com).

Title: Chief Data Officer
Description:
Location: Remote (U.S.), with a handful of trips each year to Washington, D.C. and/or Minneapolis, MN.




About the Role

Inquoro is seeking a Chief Data Officer to own the data strategy at the heart of our business. This is a senior leadership role - reporting to the CEO and serving on the executive team - for someone who deeply understands modern progressive data, knows the research the progressive community relies on, and can translate that understanding into technology, products, and a high-performing team.




We’re a small, technical team building something with real momentum, and we’re looking for a partner to help us grow it. You’ll be the connective tissue between our data, the people who do the work, and the clients and partners who depend on it. You’ll own entire product lines end to end, set the direction for how we turn our data assets into new products, and build the foundation for where Inquoro goes next.




Why Inquoro

Inquoro is a technology-driven research infrastructure company with a simple promise: public opinion, precisely known.




We build and field our own directly-recruited, identity-validated respondent panel, matched against best-in-class person-level data, so every response can be trusted. That panel becomes larger, more efficient, and higher-quality with every campaign we run - a genuine compounding advantage.




We’re early, the team is small, and the person in this role will help decide what we build and how far it goes. For someone who knows progressive data and research from the inside, this is a rare chance to take that expertise and turn it into products, infrastructure, and a business - with real ownership and real upside.




In This Role, You Will:

Own our data strategy - the architecture, quality, and analytic value of our panel and our person-level data ecosystem, and how that data powers everything we ship
Own entire product lines end to end, including our omnibus reporting and the data products we build from the rich data our panel generates, with full responsibility for quality, roadmap, and growth
Build out a new generation of digital measurement products that help media buyers and digital advertisers evaluate and understand the impact of their efforts - and the return on their investments
Lead and grow the team - managing analysts and researchers with strong personnel management, mentorship, clear performance expectations, and professional development
Run the work - bring rigorous project management to scoping, resourcing, sequencing, and on-time delivery across the full portfolio
Scope new offerings that extend our methods and products beyond the political and advocacy space, navigating the data-rights, partnership, and product considerations that come with entering new markets
Serve as a trusted partner to clients and partners on data, research design, and what the results actually mean for their decisions
Drive innovation - bring AI, automation, and emerging data sources into our workflows from design through delivery




Qualifications

Required:

Deep, hands-on understanding of modern voter-file and person-level data, and the analytic infrastructure that powers progressive campaigns, organizations, and research
Demonstrated experience leading and managing people - building, mentoring, and growing a team of practitioners and researchers
Strong project management skills: scoping, resourcing, and delivering complex work on time across multiple priorities
A track record of owning a product, data set, or research line end to end - not just contributing to it
Fluency translating between research questions and technical execution - you can sit with researchers, understand what they need, and direct the data and engineering work to deliver it
Strong working knowledge of SQL and large-scale data environments; comfort with Python or R
Familiarity with the research the progressive community runs and how data and panels are used to support it
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex data and findings to both technical and non-technical audiences




Nice to Have:

Experience building or scoping commercial/data products, including in markets beyond politics
Background in survey methodology - sampling, weighting, questionnaire design, or experimental methods
Experience with measurement and attribution for media or digital advertising
Familiarity with omnibus survey products or panel-based research
Experience applying AI and automation across the research lifecycle
Advanced degree in a quantitative, data, or social-science field




Work Environment

This is a fully remote position. You’ll work closely with the executive team, clients, and partners over video and asynchronous communication, with a few trips each year to Washington, D.C. and/or Minneapolis for in-person collaboration and company gatherings.




Compensation and Benefits

The salary range for this position is $180,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience and qualifications.




This is a partner-level opportunity, and the package reflects that. In addition to salary, Inquoro offers:

A meaningful equity stake - we’re looking for someone to help build and grow Inquoro
A health insurance stipend
A matching 401(k) plan
Generous paid leave
A company laptop and a remote-first culture built around outcomes, not hours




Application Materials

Interested candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter to: jobs@inquoro.io




About Inquoro

Inquoro is a technology-driven survey research firm that builds and fields its own directly-recruited respondent panel. We combine advanced digital recruitment with person-level data authentication to deliver accurate, representative public opinion research - free from the fraud and sampling problems that undermine traditional panels.




Inquoro is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), military service, or any basis prohibited under federal, state, or local law.

Title: IT Support Technician
Description:
🗂 We’re Hiring: IT Support Technician

🕒 Employment Type: Full-Time

💼 Level: Entry-Level to Mid-Level

We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Support Technician to provide technical support, troubleshoot IT issues, and maintain the smooth operation of hardware, software, networks, and business systems. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, helping users, and working with technology in a fast-paced environment.

🎯 Key Responsibilities

• Provide first-level and second-level technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues.

• Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve technical problems reported by users through helpdesk, phone, email, or in-person support.

• Install, configure, maintain, and upgrade desktops, laptops, printers, mobile devices, and peripheral equipment.

• Support Microsoft Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email systems, and business applications.

• Manage user accounts, access permissions, password resets, and onboarding/offboarding activities.

• Monitor and maintain IT assets, software licenses, and hardware inventory records.

• Assist with network troubleshooting, connectivity issues, VPN access, and wireless network support.

• Document support requests, resolutions, and technical procedures within ticketing systems and knowledge bases.

• Escalate complex technical issues to senior IT personnel or external vendors when required.

• Support software deployments, system upgrades, backups, and IT infrastructure projects.

• Ensure compliance with IT security policies, cybersecurity best practices, and data protection standards.

• Deliver professional and responsive customer service while maintaining high user satisfaction levels.

✅ Requirements

• Experience in IT support, technical support, helpdesk, desktop support, or related fields is preferred.

• Strong knowledge of Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email platforms, and common business applications.

• Basic understanding of networking concepts including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPNs, Wi-Fi, and network troubleshooting.

• Familiarity with hardware diagnostics, software installation, and endpoint support.

• Experience using ticketing systems and remote support tools is advantageous.

• Understanding of cybersecurity principles, endpoint protection, and IT security best practices is a plus.

• Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

• Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service abilities.

• Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple support requests effectively.

• Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field is preferred. Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft, Google IT Support, or ITIL are advantageous.

🌟 What We Offer

• Opportunity to work with modern technologies and enterprise IT environments.

• Exposure to infrastructure, cloud services, cybersecurity, networking, and systems administration.

• Career progression into Senior IT Support Technician, Systems Administrator, Network Administrator, IT Analyst, Infrastructure Engineer, Cybersecurity Analyst, or IT Manager roles.

• A collaborative, technology-driven, and supportive work environment.

• Training, mentorship, certification support, and professional development opportunities.

• Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.

🗂 We’re Hiring: IT Support Technician

🕒 Employment Type: Full-Time

💼 Level: Entry-Level to Mid-Level

We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Support Technician to provide technical support, troubleshoot IT issues, and maintain the smooth operation of hardware, software, networks, and business systems. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, helping users, and working with technology in a fast-paced environment.

🎯 Key Responsibilities

• Provide first-level and second-level technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues.

• Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve technical problems reported by users through helpdesk, phone, email, or in-person support.

• Install, configure, maintain, and upgrade desktops, laptops, printers, mobile devices, and peripheral equipment.

• Support Microsoft Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email systems, and business applications.

• Manage user accounts, access permissions, password resets, and onboarding/offboarding activities.

• Monitor and maintain IT assets, software licenses, and hardware inventory records.

• Assist with network troubleshooting, connectivity issues, VPN access, and wireless network support.

• Document support requests, resolutions, and technical procedures within ticketing systems and knowledge bases.

• Escalate complex technical issues to senior IT personnel or external vendors when required.

• Support software deployments, system upgrades, backups, and IT infrastructure projects.

• Ensure compliance with IT security policies, cybersecurity best practices, and data protection standards.

• Deliver professional and responsive customer service while maintaining high user satisfaction levels.

✅ Requirements

• Experience in IT support, technical support, helpdesk, desktop support, or related fields is preferred.

• Strong knowledge of Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email platforms, and common business applications.

• Basic understanding of networking concepts including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPNs, Wi-Fi, and network troubleshooting.

• Familiarity with hardware diagnostics, software installation, and endpoint support.

• Experience using ticketing systems and remote support tools is advantageous.

• Understanding of cybersecurity principles, endpoint protection, and IT security best practices is a plus.

• Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

• Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service abilities.

• Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple support requests effectively.

• Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field is preferred. Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft, Google IT Support, or ITIL are advantageous.

🌟 What We Offer

• Opportunity to work with modern technologies and enterprise IT environments.

• Exposure to infrastructure, cloud services, cybersecurity, networking, and systems administration.

• Career progression into Senior IT Support Technician, Systems Administrator, Network Administrator, IT Analyst, Infrastructure Engineer, Cybersecurity Analyst, or IT Manager roles.

• A collaborative, technology-driven, and supportive work environment.

• Training, mentorship, certification support, and professional development opportunities.

  • • Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Role: Data Engineer

Type: 12 month contract

Location: Hybrid 2x/week in Toronto, Mississauga, Burlington, or Guelph, ON

Rate: $55-$60/hr




JOB DESCRIPTION




Insight Global is seeking two qualified Data Engineers for one of Canada's leading insurance companies. The successful candidate will be joining a senior team in the Enterprise Data Office to support with data transformation for an ongoing modernization project. The team is migrating from SAS to Databricks, and these individuals will be working alongside business users to create containers for the users, so they can do the work in Databricks. Day-to-day will include working through the modernization, helping users get offloaded from SAS to Databricks, teaching the users how to use the AI portion of the work, and some business analysis.




REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE




5+ years of experience as a data engineer
Strong knowledge of Databricks
Proficiency with AI-augmented delivery (coding)
Experience coding SAS (Statistical Analysis System)
Proficiency in Python and PySpark
Excellent communication skills




NICE TO HAVE SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE




Insurance experience
