Title: Associate
Description:
Riff City Strategies is a mission-driven communications firm working at the intersection of climate, emerging technology, and public policy. We build movements and tell stories that help our clients, from VC-backed startups and foundations to social justice campaigns and government leaders, create change that matters.




We tackle the most challenging issues facing our society and planet – our firm believes in the power of advocacy and communication as tools for change. Our work has helped shift hundreds of millions of dollars to BIPOC-led groups fighting for climate justice, brought landmark military justice reforms into law with Protect Our Defenders, and supported community-based public safety efforts with Urban Alchemy.




We’ve led launches for the Mercedes-Benz High-Power Charging Network, Sam Altman’s World Network, and some of the most pivotal democracy, justice, and AI governance initiatives of our time — helping government leaders, VC-backed startups, and major foundations build trust and momentum in moments that matter.




Our culture is rooted in integrity, creativity, and results. That commitment has earned us international recognition:

PRWeek Purpose Awards -Small Organization of the Year 
Fast Company - Most Innovative Companies in the World 
Bulldog PR - Best Boutique Agency




The Opportunity

We are looking for a sharp and self-starting Associate with a passion for media relations, storytelling, and public affairs. The ideal candidate is a creative, detail-oriented story-teller, driven to make an impact, and excited to work on campaigns that shape policy, launch technologies, and shift culture.




You’re a confident writer and communicator who can deliver top-tier work across formats, from press releases to long-form thought leadership. You know how to manage projects, and you’re fearless when it comes to pitching and delivering results.




Working with us means you’ll be surrounded by high-quality teammates and learn something new every day. Riff has a positive, team-oriented culture where you’ll be challenged, continue to grow, and laugh a lot. We take our work seriously but not ourselves — you’ll get to do meaningful work with a team that values creativity, purpose, and collaboration.




Riff is a remote-first organization with the team working PT hours and primarily based in the San Francisco Bay Area and Los Angeles. For this role, we’re prioritizing California-based applicants. This position reports to a senior member of the Riff team.




Responsibilities:

Get to know our clients inside and out — their mission, products, challenges, and the people and trends shaping their industries
Pitch media and conduct community outreach on behalf of clients
Build and maintain media lists for multiple clients 
Draft a wide variety of communications materials (press releases, pitches, statements, blog posts, newsletters, thought leadership pieces, etc.)
Support management of client teams through drafting agendas, recaps, and coverage reports
Monitoring news trends 
Conduct miscellaneous research for clients as needed
Provide general business support, which could include updating the Riff City Strategies' website, drafting newsletters, posting to company’s social channels or contributing to new business efforts




Required Qualifications:

1-3 years of experience in public relations, public affairs, or corporate communications, with a proven track record of success in media relations and storytelling
Exceptional media relations – comfortable and confident pitching compelling stories directly to top-tier reporters
Passionate storyteller dedicated to amplifying the voices of organizations advancing climate action, social justice, and mission-driven technology
Highly organized with excellent prioritization and time management abilities to efficiently handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment
Self-motivated and adaptable with strong initiative to take ownership of tasks while being receptive to feedback and guidance
Top-notch research, proofreading, writing, and editing skills that ensure accuracy and clarity in all communications
Outgoing, personable, and generally nice! We like to have fun and get into good trouble!
Proficiency with digital tools, including G Suite, Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, with an openness to learning new platforms as needed
Note: While remote, we are specifically looking for CA-based applicants

Preferred Qualifications:

Existing media contacts/relationships
2+ years of PR/agency experience




The Details

Riff City is best-in-class, and this extends to our employee-centric company culture and benefits. Our perks are unrivaled, like our team and clients! We provide big company benefits (competitive salary, healthcare, a personal development stipend for business, internet and phone reimbursement, 401(k) with matching, quarterly profit sharing, half-day Fridays every other week during the summer + more) while maintaining the size and energy of a startup.




Riff is a remote-first organization with the team primarily based in the San Francisco Bay Area. The annual salary range for this position is $65,000-$68,500 per year. We believe in action from the ground up — whether that’s helping accelerate clean energy or supporting changemakers in the field. If you thrive on purpose, collaboration, and creativity, you’ll fit right in.




Riff City Strategies, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics.

Title: Business Intelligence & Data Specialist
Description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This Position reports to:

Business Intelligence Manager




As a Business Intelligence & Data Specialist, you will prioritize data quality and management as the foundation of all business intelligence operations. You'll ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across all systems while addressing technical issues and supporting emergency resolutions. Your expertise will enable you to create and maintain ad-hoc and automated reports that deliver actionable insights. You'll also maintain comprehensive documentation and standardization to ensure consistency and usability, while managing access controls and security to meet compliance requirements. This role is essential for enabling stakeholders to make data-driven decisions.

This role is contributing to the Electrification, Installation Products Division in Canada and is located in Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu.

The work model is hybrid, with 2 days per week at the office.


Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets; query databases using SQL to extract, validate, and transform data while ensuring accuracy and consistency across all BI systems
Identify and implement data quality standards; implement anomaly detection and data governance principles
Serve as the front-line point of contact for BI bugs and technical issues; perform investigations and diagnose application problems
Support IT teams and vendors in resolving emergencies and provide business/process knowledge to expedite resolution
Create and maintain reports tailored to business needs; design interactive dashboards that enable data-driven decision-making
Optimize report performance and support ad-hoc reporting requests for stakeholders
Maintain comprehensive BI documentation including processes, procedures, and system configurations; standardize BI interfaces across systems
Document best practices and create training materials for end users; maintain security group reviews and access controls
Support compliance and governance requirements related to data access; identify improvements to enhance access management efficiency and security



Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, or related field
3+ years of hands-on experience with multiple BI platforms and tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Bilingual French-English. English is necessary to communicate with clients outside Quebec.
Advanced proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation; strong experience with data analysis, data quality management, and data quality tools
Strong knowledge of database management systems, data governance frameworks, and compliance principles
Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in data environments with strong problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and documentation skills with strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Customer service orientation with proven ability to support end users and cross-functional stakeholders
Strong ability to manage multiple priorities, respond to urgent issues, and collaborate effectively with IT teams.





Employment Equity & Inclusion Statement (Canada)

ABB values the dedication, commitment, and expertise of all our employees. As an Employment Equity Employer, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We actively support the principles of the Employment Equity Act and strive to build a workforce that reflects Canada’s diversity, including:


Women
Indigenous Peoples
Members of visible minorities
Persons with disabilities


ABB is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know.

Title: Executive Assistant
Description:
Why join Giga


The Pace: We're building at a speed most companies talk about but never actually operate at. If you've been bored somewhere else, you won't be here.
The Impact: Every person at Giga touches the work that matters. No layers, no waiting for approval chains. You'll see your fingerprints on what we ship.
The Team: A team of operators defining the future of AI infrastructure. We recruit A-players from the most innovative companies in the world, and they choose Giga because the work here means more.
The Moment: AI doesn't run without power, and that's us. Giga is the picks and shovels behind the AI revolution. This industry is about to explode, and you'll be in the room where it's already happening.


What You’ll Do

As Executive Assistant to one of Giga Energy's Founders, you'll be the operational backbone that keeps everything running at full speed. This role is for someone who wants to be in the room where decisions happen, not watching from the sidelines. You'll manage the details so your executive can stay focused on the big picture, acting as a trusted partner who anticipates needs, solves problems independently, and never lets anything fall through the cracks. At the core of this role is mastering three things: owning the executive's calendar so every minute is accounted for and protected, managing a high-volume inbox by drafting and responding to emails with the executive's voice and judgment, and handling all bookings — from complex travel itineraries to key meetings and reservations — so nothing is ever left to chance. If you're energized by fast-moving environments, thrive under pressure, and take pride in being the most reliable person in the room, this is the role for you.

Where you’ll work

This role will be based in our Houston office. You must be willing to work in the office on a hybrid schedule, this is dependent on which days your executive is in office.

Responsibilities


Own and manage the executive's calendar end-to-end, proactively resolving conflicts and prioritizing commitments
Draft, manage, and respond to emails on behalf of the executive with accuracy and professionalism
Handle NDAs, confidential documents, and time-sensitive paperwork with full discretion
Coordinate domestic and international travel logistics, including itineraries, accommodations, and ground transportation (~30% travel expected)
Maintain and update CRM records in HubSpot and manage day-to-day workflows across Google Suite
Make sound, independent decisions in the executive's absence — serving as a trusted proxy when needed
Identify and take ownership of time-consuming tasks before they become bottlenecks
Remain available and responsive during extended business hours (7 AM – 11 PM) as operational needs require


Requirements


3+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a comparable high-responsibility administrative role
Proficiency in Google Suite — able to hit the ground running without training
Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment
Proven track record of exercising independent judgment and making decisions on behalf of a senior leader
Strong written communication skills, including experience managing executive-level correspondence
Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with the highest level of discretion
Willingness to work extended hours and remain responsive outside of standard business hours


Bonus points


Experience working in a tech company or high-growth startup environment
Familiarity with Microsoft Excel and other business productivity tools
Background or degree in Business, Engineering, or a related field
Experience coordinating complex, multi-leg travel itineraries
Experience with Hubspot


Who We Hire

We hire people who thrive in chaos and get sharper under pressure. You don't wait to be told what to do, you spot the gap and fill it. When there's work to be done, you do it. You make decisions with the information you have, move fast, and adjust along the way. We're not looking for perfect; we're looking for people who get things done.

Equal Opportunity Employer Statement

Giga Energy is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. Giga Energy complies with all applicable labor laws, including the California Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) and other relevant state and federal regulations. We provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and encourage applicants who require accommodations during the hiring process to contact us.

Requirements

  • 3+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a comparable high-responsibility administrative role
  • Proficiency in Google Suite — able to hit the ground running without training
  • Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment
  • Proven track record of exercising independent judgment and making decisions on behalf of a senior leader
  • Strong written communication skills, including experience managing executive-level correspondence
  • Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with the highest level of discretion
  • Willingness to work extended hours and remain responsive outside of standard business hours

Bonus points

  • Experience working in a tech company or high-growth startup environment
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Excel and other business productivity tools
  • Background or degree in Business, Engineering, or a related field
  • Experience coordinating complex, multi-leg travel itineraries
  • Experience with Hubspot

Who We Hire

We hire people who thrive in chaos and get sharper under pressure. You don't wait to be told what to do, you spot the gap and fill it. When there's work to be done, you do it. You make decisions with the information you have, move fast, and adjust along the way. We're not looking for perfect; we're looking for people who get things done.

Title: Data Analytics Training and Internship Program
Description:
Company Description

Ededge is a ground-breaking organization dedicated to nurturing the potential of our youth and empowering them to explore the vast world of technology and other high-growth fields. Our upcoming initiatives will provide invaluable learning experiences by allowing audiences to gain insights from accomplished leaders, who will share not only their successes but also their journeys through failure. Despite our accomplishments, we remain dedicated to continuous growth, as our ultimate goal is to advance collectively as an organization and as a community. We elevate the industry of Ed-Tech by bringing in the training in the specific fields along with the industry experience.




Visit Our Website to know about us : www.ededgelearning.com




Role Description

This is a remote Data Analytics Training and Internship program role designed for individuals seeking practical experience while developing core analytical skills. Day-to-day activities include working with datasets to clean, organize, and validate data, as well as supporting data modeling and basic statistical analysis tasks. The role involves assisting with the creation of reports, dashboards, and visualizations that help communicate insights to internal stakeholders and mentors. Participants will follow a structured learning path, attend virtual training sessions, receive feedback on assigned projects, and collaborate with peers on team-based analytical exercises. The program is ideal for individuals looking to transition into data analytics or strengthen their capabilities while balancing other academic or professional commitments.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field
0–1 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or a related role
Basic proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets
Introductory knowledge of SQL or databases
Strong attention to detail and analytical thinking
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, essential for effective collaboration and articulation of complex ideas.
Demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a cohesive team.




Perks & Growth Opportunities

Hands-on experience working on high-impact analytics projects.
Access to expert mentorship and professional development resources.
Potential for performance-based incentives during the internship.
Opportunities for full-time roles may be offered to exceptional interns.
A valuable addition to your portfolio and resume with real-world experience.




Location: Remote




Note : This is an unpaid internship. Upon application, our team will contact you to proceed with the application details and joining process.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Key Responsibilities:

Data Infrastructure development and maintenance:

Maintain scalable data pipelines and architectures using Databricks and other cloud-based platforms
Maintain and implement robust ETL processes to ensure data quality and integrity across various research data sources

Collaboration and Support:

Work closely with Portfolio Managers, analysts and the research team to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that support user objectives
Collaborate with IT and other engineering teams to integrate data solutions into the broader technology ecosystem

Vendor Management:

Manage relationships with research vendors, ensuring data acquisition aligns with business needs and compliance standards
Evaluate and onboard new data vendors, negotiating contracts and ensuring service level agreements are met

Technical Expertise:

Utilize Python and other programming languages to develop data models, scripts, and tools that enhance data processing capabilities
Apply knowledge of AI and machine learning to support advanced analytics and predictive modeling efforts utilizing Cinctive’s AI framework




Qualifications:

4+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on hedge funds or financial research environments
Strong technical skillset including expertise using PySpark on Databricks, Python, Kusto/KQL optimization
Familiarity with Tableau, PowerBI, and data warehousing/data lake design
Alt Data experience with a background working with different alt-data source is a plus
Familiarity with AI and machine learning frameworks is a plus
Strong understanding of financial markets, hedge fund operations, and research methodologies
Experience in managing and integrating data from various research vendors, as well as vendor management
Ability to perform high-level work both independently and as part of a team
Excellent organizational and time management skills
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment




Education:

Required: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field




Compensation:

The salary range for this position is $175,000 to $200,000 per year. The actual base pay is dependent upon several factors, including, but not limited to, relevant experience, business needs and market demands. This role may also be eligible for bonus compensation and employee benefits.

Title: Power BI Admin
Description:
Role: Power BI Admin(Tableau Admin)

Location: Canada (Remote)




Responsibilities:

Administer and maintain Tableau Server environments, ensuring high availability, performance, and scalability.
Manage user access, security groups, roles, permissions, and licensing across BI platforms.
Monitor system health, platform usage, and performance metrics; proactively identify and resolve issues.
Configure and maintain Tableau sites, projects, data sources, extracts, refresh schedules, and subscriptions.
Implement and enforce data governance, security policies, and compliance controls.
Collaborate with BI Developers, Data Engineers, and Business Users to support reporting and analytics initiatives.
Troubleshoot and resolve platform, connectivity, and data refresh issues.
Support deployment, migration, upgrades, and maintenance activities for BI environments.
Develop and maintain operational documentation, standards, and best practices.
Provide end-user support, training, and guidance on Tableau platform usage.




Required Skills:

10+ years of experience in Tableau Administration and BI platform support.
Strong hands-on experience with Tableau Server Administration.
Experience managing users, permissions, authentication methods, and security configurations.
Strong understanding of Tableau architecture, performance tuning, and troubleshooting.
Experience with data source management, extract refreshes, and scheduling.
Knowledge of data governance, compliance, and security best practices.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Experience with Power BI Administration and/or SSRS.
Knowledge of SQL and relational databases.
Experience with Active Directory, SSO, SAML, or LDAP integrations.
Exposure to cloud-based BI environments and enterprise reporting solutions.







Regards,

Praveen Kumar R (Praveen)

Title: Administrative Support
Description:
Job Title: Administrative Support

Location: Tampa, Florida 

Job Type: Full-Time 

Salary: $40,000-$50,000/ Annual




About HemWell MD

HemWell MD is a medical technology company that transforms hemorrhoid care through a proprietary, non-invasive treatment that is fast, effective, and painless. We partner with physicians across the country to deliver consistent results, comprehensive training, and outstanding patient outcomes. As we grow our national footprint, we need sharp, reliable people behind the scenes keeping our operations running smoothly.




About the Role

We are looking for a motivated Administrative Support with an interest in medical device or medical operations to help anchor our internal operations. This is not a generic admin role — you will be a key contributor who keeps our day-to-day organized, supports physician partners when issues arise, and grows into a vital part of our team. If you are organized, eager to learn, comfortable in a fast-moving environment, and excited to be trained and grow with the team, we want to hear from you.




Responsibilities 

Manage day-to-day internal operations including scheduling, communications, and document management
Document common issues and resolutions to build out internal support resources and a shared knowledge base
Serve as a primary point of contact for physician offices and provider partners — guiding them through troubleshooting steps, answering procedural questions, and resolving issues with professionalism and patience
Maintain accurate financial records, process invoices, and support billing workflows 
Comfortable working in a CRM system (entering data, managing contacts, running reports)
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite: Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint
Coordinate with vendors, physician partners, and internal team members with professionalism and speed
Prepare reports, memos, and correspondence; maintain organized digital and physical filing systems
Handle confidential patient and business information with discretion and in compliance with HIPAA standards
Highly organized with strong follow-through; you don’t let things fall through the cracks
Self-directed and dependable — you manage your workload without constant oversight
Manage office supply inventory and place orders as needed
Support leadership with administrative tasks and step in wherever the team needs you

Qualifications 

2+ years of administrative or operations experience in a healthcare, medical, or clinical setting — this is strongly preferred, not optional
Hands-on experience with QuickBooks for invoicing, expense tracking, or financial recordkeeping
Comfortable working in a CRM system (entering data, managing contacts, running reports)
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite: Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint
Exceptional written and verbal communication — you represent us professionally in every interaction
Highly organized with strong follow-through; you don't let things fall through the cracks
Self-directed and dependable — you manage your workload without constant oversight
High school diploma required; Associate or bachelor's degree preferred 




Preferred but not required

Experience supporting a small or growing healthcare / Medical Device Manufacturing company
Experience in a helpdesk, patient services, or provider support role
Experience with QuickBooks and CRM systems
Familiarity with EHR/EMR systems or medical billing workflows




How to Apply  

Please send your resume and a brief cover letter to spaolitto@medfiniti.com. In your cover letter, please highlight your healthcare admin experience, any hands-on work with QuickBooks or CRM tools, and examples of support or troubleshooting you’ve handled. We look forward to hearing from you.

Join us and be part of something bigger - helping to simplify health care one person, one family and one community at a time.

Position Summary: Caremark, LLC, a CVS Health company, is hiring for the following role in Downers Grove, IL: Data Engineer to analyze data engineering problems and develop, build and manage large-scale data structures, pipelines and efficient Extract/Load/Transform (ETL) workflows to address complex problems and support business applications. Duties include: develop large scale data structures and pipelines to organize, collect and standardize data to generate insights and addresses reporting needs; write ETL (Extract/Transform/Load) processes, design database systems, and develop tools for real-time and offline analytic processing that improve existing systems and expand capabilities; collaborate with Data Science team to transform data and integrate algorithms and models into automated processes; test and maintain systems and troubleshoot malfunctions; leverage knowledge of Hadoop architecture, HDFS commands, and designing and optimizing queries to build data pipelines; utilize programming skills in Python, Java, or similar languages to build robust data pipelines and dynamic systems; build data marts and data models to support Data Science and other internal customers; integrate data from a variety of sources and ensure adherence to data quality and accessibility standards; analyze current information technology environments to identify and assess critical capabilities and recommend solutions to complex business problems; and experiment with available tools and advise on new tools to provide optimal solutions that meet the requirements dictated by the model/use case. Telecommuting available. Multiple positions. Requirements: Master's degree (or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Business Analytics, Computer Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field and completion of a university-level course, research project, internship, or thesis in each of the following:Java, Python, or Node.js;Programming in R;Data Visualization tools, including PowerBI or Tableau;Machine learning, statistical analysis, and predictive modeling;Decisions Analytics;Data modeling and processing;Database management systems; andData Mining and analytics. Pay Range: $113235.00/year to $158620.00/year.

This pay range represents the base hourly rate or base annual full-time salary for all positions in the job grade within which this position falls. The actual base salary offer will depend on a variety of factors including experience, education, geography and other relevant factors. This position also includes an award target in the company's equity award program.

In addition to your compensation, enjoy the rewards of an organization that puts our heart into caring for our colleagues and our communities. Eligible employees may enroll in the Company's 401(k) retirement savings plan, and an Employee Stock Purchase Plan is also available for eligible employees. The Company provides a fully-paid term life insurance plan to eligible employees, and short-term and long term disability benefits. CVS Health also offers numerous well-being programs, education assistance, free development courses, a CVS store discount, and discount programs with participating partners. As for time off, Company employees enjoy Paid Time Off ("PTO") or vacation pay, as well as paid holidays throughout the calendar year. Number of paid holidays, sick time and other time off are provided consistent with relevant state law and Company policies.

Title: Chief Data Officer
Description:
Location: Remote (U.S.), with a handful of trips each year to Washington, D.C. and/or Minneapolis, MN.




About the Role

Inquoro is seeking a Chief Data Officer to own the data strategy at the heart of our business. This is a senior leadership role - reporting to the CEO and serving on the executive team - for someone who deeply understands modern progressive data, knows the research the progressive community relies on, and can translate that understanding into technology, products, and a high-performing team.




We’re a small, technical team building something with real momentum, and we’re looking for a partner to help us grow it. You’ll be the connective tissue between our data, the people who do the work, and the clients and partners who depend on it. You’ll own entire product lines end to end, set the direction for how we turn our data assets into new products, and build the foundation for where Inquoro goes next.




Why Inquoro

Inquoro is a technology-driven research infrastructure company with a simple promise: public opinion, precisely known.




We build and field our own directly-recruited, identity-validated respondent panel, matched against best-in-class person-level data, so every response can be trusted. That panel becomes larger, more efficient, and higher-quality with every campaign we run - a genuine compounding advantage.




We’re early, the team is small, and the person in this role will help decide what we build and how far it goes. For someone who knows progressive data and research from the inside, this is a rare chance to take that expertise and turn it into products, infrastructure, and a business - with real ownership and real upside.




In This Role, You Will:

Own our data strategy - the architecture, quality, and analytic value of our panel and our person-level data ecosystem, and how that data powers everything we ship
Own entire product lines end to end, including our omnibus reporting and the data products we build from the rich data our panel generates, with full responsibility for quality, roadmap, and growth
Build out a new generation of digital measurement products that help media buyers and digital advertisers evaluate and understand the impact of their efforts - and the return on their investments
Lead and grow the team - managing analysts and researchers with strong personnel management, mentorship, clear performance expectations, and professional development
Run the work - bring rigorous project management to scoping, resourcing, sequencing, and on-time delivery across the full portfolio
Scope new offerings that extend our methods and products beyond the political and advocacy space, navigating the data-rights, partnership, and product considerations that come with entering new markets
Serve as a trusted partner to clients and partners on data, research design, and what the results actually mean for their decisions
Drive innovation - bring AI, automation, and emerging data sources into our workflows from design through delivery




Qualifications

Required:

Deep, hands-on understanding of modern voter-file and person-level data, and the analytic infrastructure that powers progressive campaigns, organizations, and research
Demonstrated experience leading and managing people - building, mentoring, and growing a team of practitioners and researchers
Strong project management skills: scoping, resourcing, and delivering complex work on time across multiple priorities
A track record of owning a product, data set, or research line end to end - not just contributing to it
Fluency translating between research questions and technical execution - you can sit with researchers, understand what they need, and direct the data and engineering work to deliver it
Strong working knowledge of SQL and large-scale data environments; comfort with Python or R
Familiarity with the research the progressive community runs and how data and panels are used to support it
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex data and findings to both technical and non-technical audiences




Nice to Have:

Experience building or scoping commercial/data products, including in markets beyond politics
Background in survey methodology - sampling, weighting, questionnaire design, or experimental methods
Experience with measurement and attribution for media or digital advertising
Familiarity with omnibus survey products or panel-based research
Experience applying AI and automation across the research lifecycle
Advanced degree in a quantitative, data, or social-science field




Work Environment

This is a fully remote position. You’ll work closely with the executive team, clients, and partners over video and asynchronous communication, with a few trips each year to Washington, D.C. and/or Minneapolis for in-person collaboration and company gatherings.




Compensation and Benefits

The salary range for this position is $180,000–$200,000, commensurate with experience and qualifications.




This is a partner-level opportunity, and the package reflects that. In addition to salary, Inquoro offers:

A meaningful equity stake - we’re looking for someone to help build and grow Inquoro
A health insurance stipend
A matching 401(k) plan
Generous paid leave
A company laptop and a remote-first culture built around outcomes, not hours




Application Materials

Interested candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter to: jobs@inquoro.io

Inquoro is a technology-driven survey research firm that builds and fields its own directly-recruited respondent panel. We’re a small, technical team building something with real momentum, and we’re looking for a partner to help us grow it. Title: Chief Executive Officer
Description:
Entrepreneur in Residence (EIR) → Fractional Founder, CEO → Founder/CEO




Build a company. Lead growth. Raise capital. Earn meaningful ownership.




Altura Ventures is seeking an Entrepreneur in Residence (EIR) to work directly with founder Lee Lorenzen to help fund and scale one of the ideas the the EIR already has or one of the ones that Altura Ventures has been incubating.




This role is designed for entrepreneurial operators who want more than a traditional executive position. You will begin as a fractional Founder/CEO, helping shape product, growth, fundraising, and execution—with the opportunity based on the quality of the concept and the initial fundraising success (which Altura Ventures will manage) to transition into a full-time Founder/CEO role.




Through Altura Ventures’ GEM System, you will receive hands-on support for fundraising, go-to-market strategy, pricing, and growth execution.




WHAT YOU’LL DO




• Optimize your existing startup's positioning and/or brainstorm a new idea with Lee Lorenzen (see www.leelorenzen.com)

• Select the opportunity that best fits your experience and interests

• Work alongside Altura Ventures using the GEM System (www.altura.net/gem) to raise $250K in 3 months

• Operate as a fractional Founder / CEO during the initial fundraising and growth phase

• Transition to a full-time Founder / CEO role as milestones are achieved




The Big Question




Do you have what it takes to be the Founder, CEO of one of these two startups?




If you are confident you can take this next step then please read on...




WHO YOU ARE

• Founder mentality with high ownership and accountability

• Comfortable operating in ambiguity and making fast decisions

• Strong growth, product, GTM, or operational experience

• Excited by startups and motivated by building long-term value

• Demonstrated GRIT, resilience, and execution under uncertainty




COMPENSATION

• Fundraising success bonuses of up to $25,000 as the first $250,000 is raised with Altura's help

• Initial fractional Founder/CEO equity grant of 100% -- it is your company

• Monthly salary when you transition to a full-time Founder/CEO -- expected to be in 12 to 18 months




If you have ever wanted to be a Founder, CEO and help build a company—not just manage one—this role was designed for you.




Please apply now because we'd like to learn more about you.

Title: IT Support Technician
Description:
🗂 We’re Hiring: IT Support Technician

🕒 Employment Type: Full-Time

💼 Level: Entry-Level to Mid-Level

We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Support Technician to provide technical support, troubleshoot IT issues, and maintain the smooth operation of hardware, software, networks, and business systems. This role is ideal for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, helping users, and working with technology in a fast-paced environment.

🎯 Key Responsibilities

• Provide first-level and second-level technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues.

• Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve technical problems reported by users through helpdesk, phone, email, or in-person support.

• Install, configure, maintain, and upgrade desktops, laptops, printers, mobile devices, and peripheral equipment.

• Support Microsoft Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email systems, and business applications.

• Manage user accounts, access permissions, password resets, and onboarding/offboarding activities.

• Monitor and maintain IT assets, software licenses, and hardware inventory records.

• Assist with network troubleshooting, connectivity issues, VPN access, and wireless network support.

• Document support requests, resolutions, and technical procedures within ticketing systems and knowledge bases.

• Escalate complex technical issues to senior IT personnel or external vendors when required.

• Support software deployments, system upgrades, backups, and IT infrastructure projects.

• Ensure compliance with IT security policies, cybersecurity best practices, and data protection standards.

• Deliver professional and responsive customer service while maintaining high user satisfaction levels.

✅ Requirements

• Experience in IT support, technical support, helpdesk, desktop support, or related fields is preferred.

• Strong knowledge of Windows, macOS, Microsoft 365, email platforms, and common business applications.

• Basic understanding of networking concepts including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPNs, Wi-Fi, and network troubleshooting.

• Familiarity with hardware diagnostics, software installation, and endpoint support.

• Experience using ticketing systems and remote support tools is advantageous.

• Understanding of cybersecurity principles, endpoint protection, and IT security best practices is a plus.

• Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

• Excellent communication, interpersonal, and customer service abilities.

• Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple support requests effectively.

• Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field is preferred. Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft, Google IT Support, or ITIL are advantageous.

🌟 What We Offer

• Opportunity to work with modern technologies and enterprise IT environments.

• Exposure to infrastructure, cloud services, cybersecurity, networking, and systems administration.

• Career progression into Senior IT Support Technician, Systems Administrator, Network Administrator, IT Analyst, Infrastructure Engineer, Cybersecurity Analyst, or IT Manager roles.

• A collaborative, technology-driven, and supportive work environment.

• Training, mentorship, certification support, and professional development opportunities.

• Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.

Title: Assistant, Client Success
Description:
We are seeking a highly organized and analytical Client Success Assistant to join our SoldThrough team. We are a fast-moving startup with a passion for bringing unique, relevant, and elevated brands from around the world to the U.S. consumer. We are transforming how brands execute in the digital marketplace and elevating the direct-to-consumer experience.




Position Summary

The Client Success Assistant plays a key role in ensuring the smooth execution of product onboarding, maintenance processes and retail sales analysis for a portfolio of brands across multiple Marketplace platforms to drive sales. This role is responsible for managing all data and image uploads accurately and efficiently, ensuring that products go live on time and remain active on the platform. In addition, this role provides daily operational support to Client Success Managers (CSMs), assisting with the ongoing management of client accounts and ensuring that client needs are met promptly and effectively. This role requires strong attention to detail, excellent communication, and a proactive approach to maintaining product and client success.




Key Responsibilities

Primary Job Duties and Responsibilities

Manage current live product assortment listings daily along with product data templates for all new uploads.   
Proactively monitor and improve product listing content to drive conversion and sales for assigned clients within portfolio of brands.
Manage and maintain tracker of all retailer disputes for Marketplace and Dropship programs, proactively investigating discrepancies, driving timely resolution, and escalating recurring or significant issues to the appropriate stakeholders
Own weekly reporting, providing insights and recommendations to brands in partnership with CSMs related to operational responsibilities that impact overall performance and processes.
Manage and update pricing and inventory management based on brand and retailer requirements.
Work closely with clients to ensure timely and efficient updates around PO deliveries including PO creation within systems along with updating all warehouse communications and trackers.
Utilize Excel to organize and analyze product data to generate reports and insights for broader Operations team and Leadership teams.
Assist with special projects and cross-functional initiatives as needed, including retailer audits, performance analysis, competitive research, process improvement opportunities, and other activities that support client and company growth.
Participate in monthly business reviews with dedicated brands and retailer partners alongside CSM.
Conduct regular site audits daily regarding promotional activity and new product drops.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree, fashion, business, marketing or e-commerce degree preferred.
3+ years of retail, digital and operational experience.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets to manage data and create and maintain templates.
Working knowledge of retail operations, inventory management, and basic retail math concepts
Outstanding communication skills to collaborate effectively with internal teams and third-party partners and retailers.
Detail-oriented with a keen eye for accuracy and data integrity.
Problem-solving skills and ability to think critically to translate analysis into actionable business solutions and opportunities.
Ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and work effectively in a fast-paced environment with shifting business needs.
General understanding of retail channels and systems to support business initiatives and growth (NetSuite, Marketplace, Shopify, Dropship, CRM tools, etc.)




Why Join SoldThrough

This is an opportunity to play an important role in shaping the future of a fast-growing company. You will work across departments, have room for significant professional growth, and help build scalable processes that directly impact company growth and the client success team.

Title: Analyst/Senior Analyst, Commercial Real Estate Investments
Description:
Role Overview:

The Analyst, Investments will provide analytical and qualitative support to senior investment team members in the pursuit of retail and office acquisitions. This role will support the evaluation of markets, contribute to the development of market-specific investment theses, create comprehensive deal materials, and assist in underwriting and executing investment opportunities across office and retail assets.




The ideal candidate will have strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills, with the ability to navigate complex problems and apply critical thinking as they grow within the real estate investment platform.




Job Functions:

The Analyst, Investments is responsible for, including but not limited to, the following:

Support new business generation by assisting with the underwriting and execution of acquisition opportunities in collaboration with the Perform Investments team, regional market leads, and Blackstone investment professionals
Develop and support investment materials, including financial models, presentations, and investment memoranda that clearly articulate investment opportunities and associated risks
Analyze market and submarket dynamics by working with brokers and leasing teams to research trends, fundamentals, and comparable transactions
Assist in preparing acquisition capital budgets in coordination with asset management, development, operations, and regional teams
Contribute to market-level deep dives that support investment theses for both new and existing markets
Coordinate and support due diligence efforts through transaction closings




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Real Estate, Economics, or related field
1-3 years of investment experience, preferably in investment banking, real estate, private equity, brokerage, or management consulting
Ability to work independently while managing multiple concurrent projects under tight deadlines
Strong attention to detail and organization, particularly with respect to deal materials and data management
Strategic thinker with a proactive approach to supporting investment decision-making
Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills; working knowledge of IRR, NPV, and core real estate metrics; ARGUS experience required
Strong written communication skills with the ability to distill complex analyses into clear, concise investment memos and correspondence
Demonstrated professionalism, transparency, and a collaborative mindset
Self-motivated, team-oriented individual who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
Ability to work effectively with geographically dispersed teams across markets, including but not limited to Los Angeles, San Francisco, Seattle, and Atlanta
Ability to travel on a quarterly basis

  • Self-motivated, team-oriented individual who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Ability to work effectively with geographically dispersed teams across markets, including but not limited to Los Angeles, San Francisco, Seattle, and Atlanta
  • Ability to travel on a quarterly basis

Title: Data Annotator for AI Models (English)
Description:
Location: Remote working in the following US States: CA, CO, DE, FL, GA, IL, IN, KS, MA, MN, NC, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NY, OK, OR, PA, TN, TX, WA, WI

Work Schedule: Full-time, Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Pacific Standard Time)

Experience: Previous experience in data annotation or content review is a plus

Education: Bachelor’s degree required

Contract duration: End of September, 2026

Compensation: 40 USD/hour




Does this sound like you?

Are you looking for remote, work-from-home jobs? Are you interested in helping to improve the reliability of today’s AI models? If yes, then this opportunity is for you!




What we are looking for!

RWS Group is looking for USA-based Data Annotators to annotate, evaluate and curate text, video and geographic data. This information will be used to train and improve generative AI and machine learning models.

This role requires close collaboration with RWS Project Managers and client stakeholders through regular video meetings to ensure alignment, quality, and continuous improvement.




Responsibilities:

- Annotate data accurately and consistently according to predefined guidelines in the required language.

- Perform basic research as needed to ensure accurate annotation.

- Provide feedback regarding observed patterns in the annotated data and contribute to continuous improvement of the data annotation process.

- Maintain high attention to detail and quality throughout the annotation process.

- Continuous learning and adaptability; willingness to provide feedback and ask questions.

- Training will be provided post-hire.

- Collaborate closely with RWS Project Managers and client stakeholders through regular video calls to discuss guidelines, quality expectations, and project updates.




Work benefits:

- Work from home, full time.

- Work-life balance — maintain your lifestyle while you work.

- Timely payments made directly to your bank account.

- Laptop will be provided.




Job Requirements

- Native-level proficiency in English. Additional language knowledge is a plus.

- Ability to work full time, from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Pacific Standard Time).

- Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience required.

- Familiarity with Excel or Google Sheets, including basic formulas.

- Good general communication skills.

- Detail-oriented with the ability to understand and follow instructions.

- Ability to meet deadlines.

- Responsible, reliable, and communicative.

- Must successfully pass a client exam and interview with the hiring team as part of the selection process.

- A personal laptop/desktop to access RWS systems.




Preferred Qualifications:

- Familiarity with the Appen Annotation Platform (ADAP) and machine learning principles.

- A broad range of interests with specific areas of expertise is a plus.

- Experience in a professional work environment.

- Previous experience with data annotation tasks.

Description:

Location: Position is remote; but travel to Columbus for meeting Only local resources required.

Develop and execute test cases, scripts, plans, and procedures

Excellent understanding of the software development life cycle

Must have excellent written and oral communication skills

Ability to understand and document technical/functional IT requirements

Ability to understand and test technical/functional IT requirements

Experience with integration of applications to outside internal or external applications.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Title: Senior PySpark Data Engineer

Location: Irving, TX (onsite) Local only from Texas

Duration: Fulltime

Interview Process: Must be ready to attend in-person Client interview at Dallas office if they get shortlisted in the 1st round

Experience: 8+ years










Job Description:

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Data Engineer to play a pivotal role in designing, building, and optimizing our next-generation scalable data pipelines. This position requires expertise in processing massive datasets using cutting-edge technologies like Apache Spark, PySpark, and Hive within a dynamic cloud environment. Your primary objective will be to ensure the utmost data reliability, speed, and efficiency, providing a robust foundation for downstream business intelligence and advanced analytics initiatives.







Qualifications:

Big Data Frameworks Expertise: Demonstrated high proficiency in Apache Spark architecture, including a deep understanding of drivers, executors, and Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs).
Advanced Programming: Exceptional coding skills in Python and extensive experience with the PySpark API for developing intricate data transformations and processing logic.
Querying & Schema Management: Strong command of HiveQL and ANSI SQL, coupled with expertise in data partitioning techniques and effective schema definition.
Optimized Storage Formats: In-depth understanding and practical experience with optimized big data storage file formats such as Parquet, ORC, and Avro.
Cloud Ecosystem Development: Hands-on development experience utilizing cloud-native big data utilities (e.g., AWS EMR, Azure Databricks) with in major cloud platforms.
Data Warehousing Fundamentals: Solid foundation in Dimensional Data Modelling, including Star and Snowflake schemas, and practical experience with Data Lakes concepts and implementation.
Preferred Qualifications
CI/CD & DevOps Automation: Experience with Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) practices and automation tools like Git, Jenkins, or Ansible.
NoSQL Database Integration: Exposure to and experience with NoSQL databases such as HBase, Cassandra, or MongoDB.
Professional Cloud Certifications: Relevant professional cloud certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Data Engineer, Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate) are highly valued







Roles & Responsibilities:

Data Pipeline Development & Maintenance: Design, build, and maintain highly scalable and efficient ETL/ELT data pipelines utilizing PySpark and Spark SQL for complex data transformations.
Cloud Data Infrastructure Management: Deploy, manage, and scale critical data infrastructure components on leading cloud platforms such as Amazon Web Services (AWS) (e.g., EMR, Glue), Microsoft Azure (e.g., Databricks, Synapse), or Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Data Warehousing & Storage Optimization: Strategically manage data layout, partitioning, and indexing within Apache Hive and various cloud data lake solutions to optimize performance and accessibility.
Performance Tuning & Optimization: Proactively identify and resolve performance bottlenecks in Spark jobs, leveraging Spark UI for in-depth analysis, effectively managing data skewness, and optimizing memory utilization.
Diverse Data Integration: Develop robust solutions for ingesting high-volume and diverse datasets from both structured relational databases and unstructured flat files into our data ecosystem.
Automated Workflow Orchestration: Implement and manage automated data workflows using industry-standard scheduling tools like Apache Airflow or platform-native schedulers, ensuring timely and reliable data delivery.
Strategic Collaboration: Partner closely with data scientists, business analysts, and cross-functional enterprise teams to translate complex business requirements into technically sound and efficient data solutions.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
An exciting career awaits you

At MPC, we’re committed to being a great place to work – one that welcomes new ideas, encourages diverse perspectives, develops our people, and fosters a collaborative team environment.

Position Summary

The Central Commercial Management Data & Insights team’s mission is to provide exceptional, cutting-edge analytics and meaningful insights to empower our Commercial organization to thrive and achieve unparalleled success. We do that by enhancing the trust and confidence in the data and analytics within Commercial, reducing barriers to timely, actionable insights.

This position belongs to a family of jobs with increasing responsibility, competency, and skill level. Actual position title and pay grade will be based on the selected candidate’s experience and qualifications.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:


Acquires, cleans and preprocesses raw data from various sources to ensure accuracy and reliability; maintains knowledge of key systems and queries those systems for data.
Conducts in-depth analysis of large datasets to extract meaningful insights and trends
Develops and implements analytical models to forecast future trends and outcomes
Develops and performs statistical analysis to identify patterns and correlations in the data
Creates visually compelling and easy-to-understand dashboards and reports
Communicates findings to stakeholders through charts, graphs, and presentations
Ensures integrity and quality of data through regular audits and validation processes
Works closely with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and deliver insights
Drive continuous improvement and defect elimination in organization using data insights
Establishes and supports data governance to ensure data is fit for optimum use by the organization
Partners and leads initiatives to build sustainable data pipelines, automations, and advanced analytics solutions


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:


Bachelor's Degree
2 years experience

Title: Data Analyst - Client Data Analytics-1
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

Provides guidance and support to advisors and marketing on capturing, reporting, analyzing and utilizing customer information. Applies complete knowledge, skills, and best practices to perform assignments.

What will you do?

Responsibilities:


Provide oversight and data quality control for Client Regulatory and KYC Operations
Monitor Regulatory and KYC data reconciliation and exception reports
Act as a member of the business-led configuration team for the Capital Markets Client Lifecycle Management application
Provide inputs into KYC & Regulatory data dictionary and assist in regular maintenance
Evaluate data quality results for identification of issues, patterns, trends and opportunities for enhancement of KYC and regulatory onboarding & refresh programmes and processes
Document data related platform processes and controls, procedures and training materials
Publish monthly/quarterly KRI metrics through a Data Quality Scorecard as well as detailed data issue memos
Understand firm procedures and industry best practices
Maintain proficiency on use of the firm’s automated software systems
Assists Policy & Quality Control in identifying any concerns or issues that may arise regarding AML or regulatory issues
Assist in preparing related documentation for upcoming examinations and audits, as requested
Ensure that management controls, designed to minimize the operational errors that occur, are in place and working effectivly
Develop strategies to alleviate operational issues, promote client satisfaction and/or regulatory issues
Track and Report status updates to key stakeholders including Client Lifecycle Management Group, Compliance and Front Office
Act as a subject matter expert on regulatory and enterprise policies, procedures related to client and data management
Participate in regulatory projects for Capital Markets which come through the Client Lifecycle Management Group


Key Relationships:


Regional MLROs and delegates
Advisory/Regulatory Compliance
Client Lifecycle Management Group
Develop and maintain relationships with front and back-office staff


What you need to succeed?

Required


Ability to break down problems into basic programming logic questions
Previous experience working with data
Knowledge of scripting languages like Python and query languages like SQL
Sound written and spoken communication skills
Good problem solving and analytical skills


Nice to Have


Formal qualification at Undergraduate or Graduate level, preferably in Computer Science, Mathematics, or other quantitative field.
Knowledge of products and operations of Capital Markets Investment Banking
Knowledge of regional laws and regulations related to Anti Money Laundering//Terrorist Financing/Economic Sanctions and Customer Categorisation
Previous experience of working in KYC/AML Operations
Knowledge of AML, TF and ES regulatory obligations and operational implications
Ability to influence and direct internal and external colleagues
Good presentation skills


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Opportunities to do challenging work
Flexible work/life balance options


Job Skills

Anti-Money Laundering (AML) Policy, Business Analytics, Capital Markets, Critical Thinking, Data Analytics, Detail-Oriented, Know Your Customer (KYC), Professional Presentation, Python (Programming Language), Regulatory Compliance, Structured Query Language (SQL)

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
What You’re Applying For

We're looking for a Data Analyst to help turn Finance, Compliance, Insurance, and Actuarial questions into trusted answers and shipped analytical capabilities. You'll sit inside the FCIA team as the embedded data expert, working alongside Finance, Actuarial, Insurance Product, and Compliance leaders on the numbers that actually drive decisions — the metrics that shape how we price risk, manage reserves, close the books, and report to regulators, owned and governed by you, not passed off through a ticket queue.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.

In this role you'll


Own metric definitions for FCIA domains in the semantic layer — loss ratio, premium earning, reserves, unit economics by product and state — so every dashboard, query, and AI agent pulls from the same source of truth
Diagnose what's driving movement across loss ratios, reserve adequacy, premium pacing, and expense trends, and bring those findings into the Finance close cadence and monthly reviews where decisions actually get made
Build AI-assisted analytical tools — simulators, scenario models, agentic workflows — that let Finance and Actuarial partners explore "what if" questions without booking analyst time
Productionize recurring analyses so ad-hoc requests get replaced by governed, reusable capabilities that free the team up for higher-leverage work
Validate AI-generated outputs — SQL, summaries, recommendations — with the same rigor you'd apply to a junior analyst's work, because speed without judgment isn't useful
Partner across Engineering, Biz Ops, Insurance Product, Actuarial, and Finance on data projects that span the full policy lifecycle and financial close


What You'll Need


3+ years in a data analytics role, ideally at a fast-growing consumer, fintech, or insurtech company
Advanced SQL and working proficiency in Python or R for analysis, scripting, and lightweight tooling-a MUST to qualify for this role
Hands-on experience with the modern analytics stack — Snowflake or equivalent, dbt, a BI tool (Looker preferred), and Git-based workflows
Fluency with AI-assisted development tools like Cursor, Claude, or Copilot — and the judgment to know when the output needs to be challenged
Strong communication skills: you can defend a metric definition under scrutiny and explain a complex finding to a non-technical Finance leader without losing either audience
A bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience
Ready to work in an office environment at least 3 days a week from our New York location


*Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas.

Unfortunately, we cannot consider applicants from these states: Alaska, California, Colorado, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico and Puerto Rico.

Lemonade's US base salary range for this full-time position is $95,000 - $117,500 plus equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Speak to your recruiter to hear more about the specific salary range for your preferred location.

Things to know…

Lemonade is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusivity. We never discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status. If you require reasonable accommodations due to religious beliefs, pregnancy, or disabilities, let us know at any time.

Title: Data Analyst – Operations: Manhattan, NY
Description:
At CAVA we make it deliciously simple to eat well and feel good every day. We are guided by a Mediterranean heritage that’s been perfecting how to eat and live for four thousand years. We prioritize authenticity, curiosity, and the pursuit of excellence in everything we do. We are working towards something big together.

We foster a culture built on five core values:

Generosity First, Always: We lead with kindness. Our best work happens when we act in service of others.
Constant Curiosity: We are eager to learn, grow, and explore beyond the obvious.
Act with Agility: We welcome change; it’s the only constant. We embrace, adjust, adapt.
Passion for Positivity: We greet each day with warmth and possibility.
Collective Ambition: We have high aspirations that are achieved when we work together with a shared purpose.

Data Analyst – Operations (Restaurant & Catering)

The Data Analyst at CAVA provides analytical support by preparing data, delivering insights, and creating dashboards that inform strategic reporting requirements while supporting short-term ad-hoc needs. This role partners closely with Data Product Managers to ensure data accuracy, usability, and alignment with enterprise standards.




Data Analysts play a critical role in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling business teams to make informed decisions and optimize performance.




*This role is part of the Data Product Team supporting our Restaurant & Catering Operation*




Success at CAVA: At CAVA, Individual Contributors focus on delivering high-quality work, owning tasks, and contributing to team success. We look for team members who take initiative, collaborate across teams, and stay curious about how their work supports broader goals.




What You’ll Do:

Analytics & Reporting

Query, clean, and transform datasets to support reporting and analysis.
Validate data accuracy for new metrics, dashboards, and analytical assets.
Support ad-hoc analytics requests and long-term strategic reporting needs.

Visualization & UX

Create interactive dashboards and reports aligned with business needs and UX best practices.
Ensure consistency in branding using enterprise visualization templates.
Optimize dashboards for scalability, usability, and performance.

Collaboration & Governance

Work closely with Data Product Managers as domain SMEs to understand requirements.
Partner with Data Engineers to align on logic and data source integration.
Define and implement data quality checks to ensure compliance with governance standards.
Collaborate with product owners to iterate designs and improve user experience.

The Qualifications:

Required

2–5 years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence.
Proficiency in SQL (or python) and experience with data querying and transformation.
Strong skills in data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
Ability to work with structured and semi-structured data.
Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Preferred

Experience with Python or R for data manipulation and analysis.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (Snowflake, Databricks).
Understanding of data governance and quality assurance practices.

Physical Requirements:

Ability to maintain stationary position to be able to operate a computer and other office equipment
Must be able to identify, analyze and assess details
For certain positions, must be able to occasionally move or transport items up to 50 pounds
Ability to communicate with others and exchange information accurately and effectively
Constantly positions self and move about to support ordinary restaurant or food production support or office operations, as applicable
Ability to work in a constant state of alertness and in a safe manner

What we offer:

Competitive salary, plus bonus and long-term incentives*
Early Wage Access!
Unlimited PTO, paid parental leave, plus paid opportunities to give back to the community
Health, Dental, Vision, Telemedicine, Pet Insurance plus more!
401k enrollment with CAVA contribution
Company-paid STD, LTD, Life and AD&D coverage for salaried positions*
Free CAVA food
Casual work environment
The opportunity to be on the ground floor of a rapidly growing brand
All exempt and non exempt employees are eligible for benefits. Benefits are effective the 1st of the month following 30 days of service and you have until the day before the effective date to enroll. A new hire can enroll in our benefit program by selecting a link that is emailed directly to the new hire at their personal email address once hired.
Please note that visa sponsorship is not available.
The posting range is an all cash range.







*Indicates qualifying eligible positions only




CAVA – Joining “A culture, not a concept”




As an equal opportunity employer, CAVA considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, age, height, weight, marital status, sexual orientation, familial status, genetic information or any other characteristic or protected classes as defined by federal, state, or local law.

California applicants: Please visit our Notice at Collection and Privacy Policy to learn about our information practices in the job application and employment context.

Title: Data Analyst, Operations Planning
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Data and analytics are at the heart of Lyft's products and decision-making. As a member of the Lyft Urban Solutions team, you will play a key role in shaping the future of bike & scooter share by leveraging data to improve the performance of our bikeshare and scooter markets across America. A successful candidate thrives in a dynamic and collaborative environment, has a natural curiosity, and isn’t afraid to dive deep.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with Operations, Hardware, Software, Product and Finance teams to identify opportunities and gaps in performance. You will work in a fast-paced environment where your analytical insights will directly impact strategic decisions around hardware product development, staffing, and product changes, ultimately driving better performance and long-term investments in micromobility infrastructure.

We’re looking for a passionate and driven Data Analyst to tackle some of the most complex and impactful challenges in micromobility. If you’re excited about shaping the future of urban mobility through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Responsibilities:


Partner with Product, Engineering, Data Science & Analytics, Operations, Finance and other cross-functional stakeholders on initiatives to improve operational performance
Develop frameworks and scalable processes to streamline reporting, drive decision-making and prioritization
Forecast operational requirements needed to maintain high service levels and meet contractual and financial targets
Work with our bike & scooter share markets to deliver ongoing support and deep dive analyses on performance; monitor and diagnose performance and present findings to key stakeholders
Collaborate with cross-functional teammates to tackle complex problems including: asset maintenance, system health and labor forecasting


Experience:


3+ years experience in data analytics in a high-growth environment, preferably a consulting, operations or transportation / logistics space
Bachelor's Degree or equivalent relevant professional experience
Highly skilled in SQL and quantitative analysis. You can deep dive into large amounts of data, draw meaningful insights, dissect business issues and draw actionable conclusions
Ability to develop scalable approaches and produce data visualizations to drive business insights and provide tangible solutions; experience building dashboards for performance analysis is a plus
Extreme comfort working with ambiguity. Ability to translate unclear issues or unstructured problems into clearly defined requirements with minimal oversight
Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships, trust and influence with cross-functional partners
Great communication (listening, written, and oral) skills with the ability to present findings & recommendations targeted to the audience in question
Strong attention to detail, structured thinking and experiences developing processes to reduce human error
Adept at contextualizing real world operations into analytical problem solving
Passionate about sustainable mobility and active transportation
A strong sense of product ownership - you’re constantly looking for ways to improve the customer’s experience and aren’t afraid to get your hands dirty to do so
Bonus: Proficiency in Python and associated data science libraries


Benefits:


Great medical, dental, and vision insurance options with additional programs available when enrolled
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
401(k) plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to 12 observed holidays, salaried team members have discretionary paid time off, hourly team members have 15 days paid time off
18 weeks of paid parental leave. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible
Subsidized commuter benefits
Monthly Lyft credits and complimentary Lyft Pink membership


Lyft is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive workplace that fosters belonging. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regards to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, age, genetic information, or any other basis prohibited by law. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the New York City area is $82,800 - $103,500, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.
