Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst / Power BI Developer

Location: Richmond, VA (Hybrid)

Duration: 12-Month Contract (Potential Extension)

Rate: $40–45/hr




About the Role:




Brooksource is seeking a Data Analyst / Power BI Developer to serve as a key contributor within a growing analytics function. This individual will help transform raw data into actionable insights, supporting stakeholders across multiple program areas.




A large public-sector organization is continuing its expansion of enterprise data and analytics capabilities, leveraging Power BI and modern data platforms to support program performance and operational decision-making. This environment sits at the intersection of policy, data, and technology, supporting critical human services programs across a statewide network.




This is an excellent opportunity for a mid-level data professional to expand their experience working with enterprise-scale data systems, gain exposure to government and human services data, and further develop advanced Power BI and SQL skills in a highly impactful environment.




Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports to support operational and program-level decision-making
Write and optimize SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple relational data sources
Build and maintain data models using DAX and Power Query (M language) to support scalable reporting solutions
Translate business requirements from non-technical stakeholders into clear, intuitive visualizations and reporting outputs
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including IT, program staff, and leadership







Qualifications

2–4 years of experience in a Data Analyst or BI Developer role
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI, including dashboards, DAX, Power Query, and data modeling
Proficiency in SQL and experience querying relational databases (SQL Server preferred)
Ability to translate business needs into technical reporting solutions
Strong attention to detail with a focus on data accuracy and integrity
Experience working independently and managing deliverables in a structured environment
Excellent communication skills — ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders




EEO Statement:

Brooksource is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate on the basis of actual or perceived race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, lactation and related medical conditions), gender identity or gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, physical or mental disability, protected medical condition as defined by applicable state or local law, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws and ordinances.




Benefits & Perks

Benefits & Perks: Brooksource offers competitive medical, dental, vision, Health Savings Account, Dependent Care FSA, and supplemental coverage with plans that can fit each employee’s needs. We offer a 401k plan that includes a company match and is fully vested after you become eligible, paid time off, sick time, and paid company holidays. We also offer an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) that provides services like virtual counseling, financial services, legal services, life coaching, etc.




Pay Disclaimer:

The pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

At ITA International, we ’ re a tech-enabled professional services company. Headquartered in Newport News, Virginia, we leverage subject matter expertise, data analytics and technology to challenge boundaries and transform possibilities.

With a global presence and a passionate team of over 200 ITAers, we ’ re driven by mission success for our customers, “ In The Arena. ” Our expertise spans Operations, Training, Engineering, Nanotechnology, Statistics, Machine Learning and Software Engineering – enabling data and tech-enabled solutions that deliver real value.

Join our impactful journey at ITA International. As Theodore Roosevelt said, “ The credit belongs to the man who is actually in the arena. ” We ’ re here, standing beside our customers , ready to serve and succeed.

ITA is seeking a Junior Data Analyst to join our team.

Responsibilities

We are looking for a motivated Junior Data Analyst to join our analytics team. This entry-level position provides an exciting opportunity for individuals seeking to launch their careers in data analysis. The successful candidate will assist in collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to support decision-making processes and enhance operational effectiveness.

Responsibilities:


Assist in gathering and preparing data from various sources for analysis.
Conduct preliminary data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies.
Create visualizations and reports to communicate findings to team members and stakeholders.
Support the team in standardizing data sets and ensuring data quality.
Collaborate with senior analysts to understand business needs and provide insights.
Document analytical processes and maintain accurate records of data sources.
Participate in team meetings to discuss ongoing projects and findings.


Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Economics.
0-3 years of experience in data analysis, statistics, or a related field.
Basic knowledge of data analysis tools and techniques (e.g., Excel, SQL).
Familiarity with programming languages such as Python or R.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent attention to detail and the ability to work with large datasets.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
A willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment.


Desired Qualifications:


Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) is a plus.
Familiarity with statistical analysis methods and software.
Knowledge of data management best practices including SQL.
Prior internship or coursework in data analysis or a related area is beneficial.
Active Secret Clearance or the ability to obtain a Secret Clearance


Benefit And Compensation Transparency

ITA International proudly complies with all federal and state benefit and pay transparency laws. Employees of ITA can expect a robust benefit package, including:


Medical, dental and vision plans
Life Insurance
Short Term Disability insurance (where applicable)
Voluntary ancillary benefit options
401k retirement benefits with employer matching contributions


Pay range for this position is $107,000 - $115,000 annually.

Title: Data Analyst 2
Company: New York State Higher Education Services Corporation
Description:
Description

Duties Description

Working under the supervision of the Data Analyst 3, the Data Analyst 2 will work closely with the staff within HESC and the Division of Budget, to conduct the conversion of financial education related data that will help inform business/policy decisions and be incorporated into various internal and external reports and publications.

Duties Include But Are Not Limited To


Identify, analyze, mine, parse and cleanse data related to NYS or national education and/or higher education spending, trends, and outcomes.
Convene with individuals representing college campuses, academic partners and state agencies for data collection and validation; interpret data and communicate results.
Compare quantitative and statistical analyses on large, complicative sets of education data.
Review data for quality assurance.
Utilize and maintain an internal master reporting repository that includes data sources, and metadata.
Devise methods for identifying data patterns and trends in data sources; assess data structures and how they can be used to produce desired reports.
Identify trends in data and identify risks in programs based on these trends.
Create and present data visualizations using dashboards, graphs, charts and other visual representation of data for use in print and online. Contribute to the development of reports and publications.
Translate technical data into lay terms.
Create and deliver reports and analyses as requested.
Compile other ad-hoc data analysis based on current needs and executive management priorities.
Perform other duties as assigned.


Minimum Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in data or computer science, informatics, economics, statistics, mathematics, or related field; and three years of experience in data visualization, and statistical models and data mining tools.

A master’s degree in the above fields may substitute for one year of experience. A Ph.D. may substitute for two years of experience.

Preferred Qualifications

Strong quantitative skills, and experience utilizing analytic methods and tools such as Microsoft Excel, SAS and SQL (or R, Python)


Experience working with large datasets, confidential data, and relational databases.
Experience creating financial reports.
Knowledge of financial concepts, including budget forecasting and costing
Must be able to work well under pressure and adjust to changing deadlines/priorities and handle multiple tasks assigned.
Strong communication skills and ability to simplify difficult technical concepts.


Additional Comments

NYS is an equal opportunity employer.

Benefits of Working for NYS

Generous benefits package, worth 65% of salary, including:

Holiday & Paid Time Off


Thirteen (13) paid holidays annually, plus two (2) floating holidays
Up to Thirteen (13) days of paid vacation leave annually
Up to Five (5) days of paid personal leave annually
Up to Thirteen (13) days of paid sick leave annually for CSEA or PEF; up to eight (8) days of paid sick leave annually for M/C
Up to three (3) days of professional leave annually to participate in professional development
Initially positions will work full-time in the office and a hybrid telecommuting schedule of up to 50% remote work can be discussed with the hiring manager during the interview .


Health Care Benefits


Eligible employees and dependents can pick from a variety of affordable health insurance programs
Family dental and vision benefits at no additional cost


Additional Benefits


New York State Employees’ Retirement System (ERS) Membership
NYS Deferred Compensation
Access to NY 529 and NY ABLE College Savings Programs, as well as U.S. Savings Bonds
Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF)
And many more.

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
About Lantern

Lantern is the specialty care platform connecting people with the best care when they need it most. By curating a Network of Excellence comprised of the nation's top specialists for surgery, cancer care, infusions and more, Lantern delivers excellent care with significant cost savings to employers and their workforces. Lantern also pairs members with a dedicated care team, including Care Advocates and nurses, for the entirety of their care journey, helping them get back to good health, back to their families and back to work. With convenient access to specialists nationwide, Lantern means quality care is within driving distance for most. Lantern is trusted by the nation's largest employers to deliver care to more than 6 million members across the country. Learn more about us at lanterncare.com.

About You:


You use LOGIC in your decision making and understand that progress is critical to making change. You focus on the execution of your content while balancing a fast-paced environment and you take the time to celebrate both the small & big wins.
INCLUSION is a core tenant of your personal beliefs. A diverse and inclusive environment is incredibly important to you. You understand and desire to be a part of a diverse team with different experiences and perspectives & you cherish the differences in each individual that you interact with.
You have the GRIT, drive and ambition to tackle big problems. Big problems require big ideas and a team that supports new ideas.
You care deeply for your customers are driven to keep HUMANITY in all decisions. Your customers aren’t just the individuals using your product. They are the driving factor in your motivation to make a change.
Integrity guides you in life. Focusing on the TRUTH vs. giving people the answers they want to hear.
You thrive in a Team Environment. Collaboration is key in innovation and creating change.


These pillars of LIGHT are a reminder to our team that we are making a difference by providing guidance and support in navigating the often complex and confusing landscape of healthcare. We hope that through this LIGHT, individuals can find their way to the best care, resources, and support they need to get back to life.

If this sounds like you, we would love to connect to speak further about career opportunities at Lantern.

Please apply to our role & someone from our Talent Acquisition Team will reach out to help you navigate our interview process.

Operations Data Analyst

As the Operations Data Analyst for the Outbound Program, you will support the day-to-day data and reporting needs of the outbound Care Advocate team. You will compile, organize, and analyze operational data to help leadership and the team understand performance, identify trends, and make informed decisions. Working closely with the leadership team, you will support the reporting infrastructure and provide ongoing data support as the program grows. This role is a great fit for someone who is detail-oriented, eager to learn, and excited to contribute to a fast paced, growing operation.

Location: Remote | USA | Full-time | Preference for Hybrid in Dallas, TX, New York, NY and Washington, DC

Responsibilities:

Data Collection & Reporting


Pull, compile, and organize operational data from CRM systems, dialer platforms, and other contact center tools on a regular cadence
Build and maintain dashboards and reports that track key outbound program metrics including contact rates, conversion, call volume, and advocate productivity
Deliver daily, weekly, and monthly performance reports to the leadership team
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across all reports and tracking tools


Performance Analysis & Insights


Analyze team and individual advocate performance data to identify trends, patterns, and areas of opportunity
Support the leadership team with data requests, ad hoc analyses, and performance deep dives
Help translate data findings into clear, easy-to-understand summaries for leadership and non-technical stakeholders
Flag data anomalies or performance concerns to leadership in a timely manner


Reporting Infrastructure & Process Support


Assist in building and maintaining the reporting infrastructure and data tracking processes for the outbound program
Develop and maintain tracking tools, spreadsheets, and reporting templates that support program operations
Identify opportunities to streamline data collection and reporting processes and recommend improvements
Document data definitions, reporting processes, and data sources to ensure consistency and continuity


Collaboration & Program Support


Work closely with the leadership team to align reporting with program goals and performance standards
Respond to data requests from cross-functional partners in a timely and accurate manner
Support special projects and initiatives requiring data analysis or reporting as assigned
Other duties and responsibilities as assigned


Requirements:


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Analytics, Statistics, Healthcare Administration, or a related field preferred; equivalent experience considered
2–3 years of experience in a data, reporting, or operations support role required; contact center or healthcare experience a plus
Exposure to or experience working with AI tools, automation platforms, or emerging analytics technologies a plus
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets, including pivot tables, formulas, and data visualization
Experience with or exposure to CRM systems, reporting dashboards, or BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) preferred
Strong attention to detail with a commitment to data accuracy and consistency
Ability to manage multiple reporting tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
Clear written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present data findings to non-technical audiences
Eagerness to learn, grow, and take on increasing responsibility over time
Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a small, cross functional team
Knowledge of healthcare, insurance, or contact center operations a plus


Strong Candidates will:


Be naturally detail-oriented and take pride in accurate, clean, well-organized data
Be comfortable working with large data sets and eager to develop their analytical skills in a real-world environment
Communicate findings clearly – able to turn numbers into a story that non technical teammates can act on
Be proactive and self-directed, taking ownership of recurring reports and flagging issues without being asked
Be excited to help establish and continuously improve reporting processes
Bring curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset to every dataset they touch


Benefits


Medical Insurance
Dental Insurance
Vision Insurance
Short & Long Term Disability
Life Insurance
401k with company match
Paid Time Off
Paid Parental Leave




Lantern does not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, religion, age, national origin, marital status, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other reason prohibited by law in provision of employment opportunities and benefits.

Title: Data Analyst (Central Analytics)
Description:
Hadrian - Manufacturing the Future

Hadrian is building autonomous factories that help aerospace and defense companies manufacture rockets, satellites, jets, and ships up to 10x faster and up to 2x cheaper. By combining advanced software, robotics, and full-stack manufacturing, we are reinventing how America produces its most critical parts.

We’re accelerating our mission with the launch of Factory 3 in Mesa, Arizona, a 290,000-square-foot facility creating 350 new jobs. We are expanding rapidly to support thousands of future hires, launching Hadrian Maritime to expand into naval production, and introducing a Factory-as-a-Service model that delivers complete systems instead of individual parts.

Hadrian is backed by leading investors including T. Rowe Price, Lux Capital, Founders Fund, and Andreessen Horowitz, our fast-growing team is united around reindustrializing American manufacturing for the 21st century and beyond.

The Role

As a Data Analyst, you will own the analytical foundation that the entire company runs on. Reporting to the Sr. Director of Data & IT, you will define what "certified data" means at Hadrian, build the forecasting models that drive planning decisions, and set the standards that let analytics scale across 20+ autonomous factories. This role sits at the intersection of every business function—Finance, Manufacturing Ops, Production Planning, Quality Engineering, and Sales—connecting financial models to factory floor reality.

What You'll Do


Architect and maintain the certified dataset layer in dbt—models, tests, documentation, and SLAs that the entire company trusts
Build forecasting models for demand planning, capacity utilization, delivery risk, and financial projections
Define metric standards: canonical definitions, calculation logic, ownership, and refresh cadence
Perform cross-functional deep dives connecting financial and operational data (e.g., margin erosion vs. throughput changes)
Build scalable reporting frameworks that work at 1 factory and 20
Develop anomaly detection for manufacturing KPIs—surface problems before ops does
Present findings to executives, ops leaders, and finance with appropriate precision
Mentor BizOps analysts and junior team members on SQL, statistics, and communication
Partner with Data Engineering on pipeline architecture, data contracts, and quality SLAs


What We're Looking For


Expert SQL including complex window functions, recursive CTEs, query plan analysis, and cost-based optimization
Deep dbt knowledge including advanced macros, custom tests, package development, and CI/CD integration
Experience with Snowflake and/or ClickHouse at scale
Python proficiency (pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels)
Statistical rigor: hypothesis testing, regression, time series, and experimental design
Ability to build, validate, and communicate forecast models with proper uncertainty quantification
Strong cross-functional communication—can translate between data and business with any audience
Bachelor's or Master's in Statistics, Mathematics, Operations Research, Economics, or equivalent
P3: 2–4 years | P4: 4–7 years | P5: 7+ years in analytics, data science, or quantitative roles


What Will Set You Apart


Experience building metric layers (Transform, MetricFlow, or custom)
ClickHouse optimization (materialized views, projections, TTL)
Bayesian methods or causal inference experience
Manufacturing-specific knowledge: SPC, control charts, process capability
Orchestration experience (Dagster, Airflow)
Background in OR, industrial engineering, logistics, supply chain, or quantitative finance


Compensation

For this role, the target salary range is $140,000-$175,000 (actual range may vary based on experience). This is the lowest to highest salary we reasonably and in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee's pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, performance, and business or organizational needs.

Benefits For Full-time Employees


Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance plans for employees
401k
Relocation support may be provided for certain situations, based on business need.
Flexible vacation policy


ITAR Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) you must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident of the U.S., protected individual as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here.

Hadrian Is An Equal Opportunity Employer

It is the Company’s policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. The Company does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race inclusive of traits historically associated with race (including, but not limited to, hair texture and protective hairstyles, such as braids, locks and twists), color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, national origin (including, in California, possession of a drivers license), ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, height or weight, medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, exercise of reproductive rights, any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws, or any combination of the above characteristics. When necessary, the Company also makes reasonable accommodations for disabled candidates and employees, including for candidates or employees who are disabled by pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.

We are expanding rapidly to support thousands of future hires, launching Hadrian Maritime to expand into naval production, and introducing a Factory-as-a-Service model that delivers complete systems instead of individual parts.

Hadrian is backed by leading investors including T. Rowe Price, Lux Capital, Founders Fund, and Andreessen Horowitz, our fast-growing team is united around reindustrializing American manufacturing for the 21st century and beyond.

The Role

As a Data Analyst, you will own the analytical foundation that the entire company runs on. Reporting to the Sr. Director of Data & IT, you will define what "certified data" means at Hadrian, build the forecasting models that drive planning decisions, and set the standards that let analytics scale across 20+ autonomous factories. This role sits at the intersection of every business function—Finance, Manufacturing Ops, Production Planning, Quality Engineering, and Sales—connecting financial models to factory floor reality.

What You'll Do

  • Architect and maintain the certified dataset layer in dbt—models, tests, documentation, and SLAs that the entire company trusts
  • Build forecasting models for demand planning, capacity utilization, delivery risk, and financial projections
  • Define metric standards: canonical definitions, calculation logic, ownership, and refresh cadence
  • Perform cross-functional deep dives connecting financial and operational data (e.g., margin erosion vs. throughput changes)
  • Build scalable reporting frameworks that work at 1 factory and 20
  • Develop anomaly detection for manufacturing KPIs—surface problems before ops does
  • Present findings to executives, ops leaders, and finance with appropriate precision
  • Mentor BizOps analysts and junior team members on SQL, statistics, and communication
  • Partner with Data Engineering on pipeline architecture, data contracts, and quality SLAs

What We're Looking For

  • Expert SQL including complex window functions, recursive CTEs, query plan analysis, and cost-based optimization
  • Deep dbt knowledge including advanced macros, custom tests, package development, and CI/CD integration
  • Experience with Snowflake and/or ClickHouse at scale
  • Python proficiency (pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels)
  • Statistical rigor: hypothesis testing, regression, time series, and experimental design
  • Ability to build, validate, and communicate forecast models with proper uncertainty quantification
  • Strong cross-functional communication—can translate between data and business with any audience
  • Bachelor's or Master's in Statistics, Mathematics, Operations Research, Economics, or equivalent
  • P3: 2–4 years | P4: 4–7 years | P5: 7+ years in analytics, data science, or quantitative roles

What Will Set You Apart

  • Experience building metric layers (Transform, MetricFlow, or custom)
  • ClickHouse optimization (materialized views, projections, TTL)
  • Bayesian methods or causal inference experience
  • Manufacturing-specific knowledge: SPC, control charts, process capability
  • Orchestration experience (Dagster, Airflow)
  • Background in OR, industrial engineering, logistics, supply chain, or quantitative finance

Compensation

For this role, the target salary range is $140,000-$175,000 (actual range may vary based on experience). This is the lowest to highest salary we reasonably and in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee's pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, performance, and business or organizational needs.

Benefits For Full-time Employees

  • Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance plans for employees
  • 401k
  • Relocation support may be provided for certain situations, based on business need.
  • Flexible vacation policy

ITAR Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) you must be a U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident of the U.S., protected individual as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here.

Hadrian Is An Equal Opportunity Employer

It is the Company’s policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. The Company does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race inclusive of traits historically associated with race (including, but not limited to, hair texture and protective hairstyles, such as braids, locks and twists), color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, national origin (including, in California, possession of a drivers license), ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, height or weight, medical condition, family care status, military or veteran status, marital status, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, genetic information, exercise of reproductive rights, any other basis protected by local, state, or federal laws, or any combination of the above characteristics. When necessary, the Company also makes reasonable accommodations for disabled candidates and employees, including for candidates or employees who are disabled by pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.

Title: Business Intelligence Jr.
Description:
La atracción del mejor talento es un factor estratégico para Walmart. ¿te imaginas trabajar en una compañía que contrata al año más de 150,000 nuevos asociados?

Nuestro equipo está formado por profesionales de reclutamiento que trabajamos todos los días por innovar y hacer de la experiencia de nuestros candidatos nuestro principal objetivo. ¡Ven y únete a nosotros!




¿Qué harás?

Asegurar el monitoreo, análisis y disponibilidad de información clave del negocio, mediante el seguimiento de dashboards, detección de incidencias en datos y coordinación con equipos técnicos, para garantizar la continuidad operativa y habilitar la toma de decisiones basada en datos en un entorno global.




Principales Responsabilidades:

Monitorear dashboards y sistemas de información para identificar desviaciones o fallas en datos.
Identificar y dar seguimiento a incidencias en procesos de data, asegurando su resolución oportuna.
Analizar información para detectar tendencias, errores o áreas de oportunidad en la operación.
Generar y actualizar reportes operativos y estratégicos para diferentes áreas del negocio.
Apoyar en la construcción y mejora de dashboards y herramientas de Business Intelligence.
Colaborar con equipos de producto e ingeniería en la mejora de soluciones de datos.
Traducir necesidades de negocio en requerimientos técnicos básicos.
Comunicar hallazgos, incidencias y estatus a stakeholders locales y globales (en inglés y español).
Asegurar la calidad y consistencia de la información utilizada en la operación.
Actuar con sentido de urgencia ante incidentes que impacten la operación del negocio.







¿A quién buscamos?

Licenciatura en Actuaría, Ingeniería, Matemáticas, Economía, Ciencia de Datos o afín
1 a 2 años de experiencia en análisis de datos, BI, reporting o soporte a sistemas
Manejo de Excel avanzado
Conocimientos de SQL (básico a intermedio)
Experiencia con herramientas de visualización (Power BI, Tableau, Looker o similares)
Inglés avanzado (conversacional y técnico indispensable)
Capacidad analítica y atención al detalle







“En Walmart de México y Centroamérica estamos comprometidos con una cultura de pertenencia, por lo que buscamos incorporar el mejor talento basándonos en competencias, habilidades y potencial; no discriminamos por ningún motivo, incluyendo género, edad, origen étnico, apariencia física, estado civil, condiciones de salud por VIH o cualquier enfermedad, situación socioeconómica, discapacidad, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género

por ello durante el proceso de atracción y selección no solicitaremos información personal, fotografías o análisis clínicos de ningún tipo.

Los datos personales del candidato o candidata no son relevantes durante el proceso de entrevista ni para la selección final. En Walmart de México y Centroamérica nos comprometemos con nuestros/as asociados/as para que encuentren día a día en el trabajo un ambiente de respeto, libre de discriminación y acoso sexual

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

MRM is looking for a Data Analyst. The ideal candidate for this role has a solid fundamental background in statistical analysis and data science, with an openness to learn and expand their skills. As this may eventually become a client-facing role, strong communication skills are a valuable asset.

The Responsibilities Include


Handle routine reporting (primarily bi-weekly and eventually, quarterly reports)
Manage ad-hoc analytics questions & requests in a timely fashion
Assist in website analytics initiatives, including website tag implementation in Google Tag Manager and running custom analytics reports using Google Analytics
Create custom analytics dashboards in Tableau, PowerBI or similar tools
Assist in report automation
Assist in campaign planning and performance analysis


Required Skills

Required skills and attributes (You don’t have to be an expert in any of these, but we are looking for someone with a basic understanding and a willingness to learn / grow these skills):


Bachelor’s degree preferably in Statistics, Data Science, Analytics, Mathematics or similar with 0-3 years’ professional experience
Basic knowledge of Python & Pandas, with a keen interest in developing their programming skills.
Basic SQL capabilities: joining tables, using ‘group by’ clauses, case statements
Basic understanding of statistical concepts such as hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, statistical tests (chi-squared, etc) and how to use them
Familiar with the basic fundamentals of data visualization, and at least one data visualization tool or package (Tableau, PowerBI, Plot.ly, Seaborn, ggplot etc)
Basic to intermediate skills in Excel: Pivot tables, etc.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
Comfortable working independently (once properly on-boarded)
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment


Preferred, But Not Required


Interest or background in machine learning / predictive modelling
Knowledge or experience working within Google Tag Manager and / or Google Analytics
Knowledge or experience working with Firebase
Experience working with scientific polling data—bonus if applicant has experience with large .sav files and either SPSS / pyreadstat
Experience with automated data collection (web scraping, APIs)
Experience writing VBA macros a plus
Experienced and comfortable collaborating remotely


About MRM

MRM is a modern relationship marketing agency dedicated to building enduring brand relationships. MRM's specialized capabilities drive relationship lifetime value and span CRM, Customer Experience, and Commerce with data and martech at the core. MRM operates in a borderless, integrated way to enable greater collaboration, creativity, and true speed to market. For more information, please visit www.mrm.com.

At MRM, we value a culture in which all people are treated with dignity, fairness and respect. We’re committed to fostering a positive environment free from harassment and discrimination. We empower individuals to take action and be creative when solving problems. And we expect self-awareness and accountability from team members at all levels of the organization. This is much more than a statement – it’s in the act of living our daily lives.

MRM is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, gender, religion, age, sexual orientation, national or ethnic origin, disability, marital status, veteran status, or any other occupationally irrelevant criteria.

The salary range for this position is $45,000 to $160,000. Where an employee or prospective employee is paid within this range will depend on, among other factors, actual ranges for current/former employees in the subject position; the associated discipline; market considerations; budgetary considerations; tenure and standing with the company (applicable to current employees); as well as the employee’s/applicant’s background, pertinent experience, and qualifications.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our growing Data & Analytics team at Funko. In this role, you will leverage data to deliver actionable insights that support business growth, operational efficiency, and smarter decision-making. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build scalable reporting, analyze trends, and help drive a more data-informed culture.This is a hands-on individual contributor role ideal for someone who enjoys translating data into meaningful business impact.What You’ll Do
Partner with Operations, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and other teams to support data-driven decisions
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPI tracking in BI tools (Power BI, Tableau Looker, etc.)
Develop AI-enabled and automated reporting solutions to enhance insight generation and reduce manual work
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and business recommendations
Write and optimize SQL queries to work with large, complex datasets
Identify predictive modeling opportunities and build models
Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data models, data quality, and reporting scalability
Support reporting and analytics needs tied to ERP and operational systems
What You’ll Bring
Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related field (or equivalent experience)
3–5+ years of hands-on experience in data analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Proficiency in BI and data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker, etc.)
Data science skill set: knowledgeable in statistical modeling (e.g. linear and logistical regression, classification, clustering) and tooling (python, R, etc.) with broad domain applicability
Experience with AI-driven analytics, automated reporting, or leveraging machine learning outputs in reporting workflows
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.)
Experience working with ERP systems (Microsoft D365, Oracle, or similar) strongly preferred
Solid understanding of data warehousing and data modeling concepts
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and ability to manage multiple priorities
Clear communication skills with the ability to present insights to non-technical stakeholders 
Salary InformationThe base salary range for this position in the selected city is $90,000 - $110,000 annually.​Compensation may vary outside of this range depending on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience, and location. Base pay is one part of the Total Package that is provided to compensate and recognize employees for their work, and this role may be eligible for additional discretionary bonuses/incentives, and restricted stock units.

What Funko Offers
Funko offers a competitive compensation package with full benefits and a 401(K) plan with matching contributions from the company. Most importantly, we offer a creative work environment with people who love pop culture just as much as you do. Can’t wait to gush about your latest binge? Neither can we! Looking for a place where your favorite pop culture t-shirt will receive the compliments it deserves? We know how you feel!


This role will be based in our Everett, WA HQ office. We will prioritize local candidates first and candidates located near a local office. This is NOT a remote opportunity at this moment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
General Information

Requisition # 702

Locations USA-VA-Arlington

Posting Date 03/04/2026

Security Clearance Required - IRS - MBI

Remote Type Hybrid

Time Type Full time

Description & Requirements

Elder Research Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of MANTECH international Corporation seeks a motivated, career and customer-oriented Data Engineer to join our team in Arlington, VA. This is a hybrid position Preferably located in the Washington DC area.

As a Data Analyst of the Elder Research team, you will join a functional team of accomplished Data Scientists, Software Engineers and Data Engineers that deliver custom analytic solutions to our federal government clients. Some of your responsibilities will include: consulting with technical and non-technical stakeholders, exploring and analyzing large structured, semi-structured and unstructured datasets using SQL and/or Python, developing and deploying data visualization dashboards, and communicating data analysis results, dashboards and reports to end-users

Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To


Experience with cleaning, management and processing large volumes of data.
Experience building dashboards and reports using data visualization tools including Power BI, and Tableau.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify and address complex data issues.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to gather requirements, understand business needs and to build towards technical solution or capability.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively with stakeholders at all levels.


Minimum Qualifications


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify and address complex data issues.
2+ years of Data Analysis or Data Management experience.
Bachelor of Science degree in a relevant field such as statistics, computer science, economics, mathematics, analytics, data science, data engineering, business, or social sciences
Clearance Required
Proficient in SQL and Python for data exploration and data analysis to identify issues, trends, and patterns with the data.
Understanding of databases and familiarity with database design.


Preferred Qualifications


Advanced degree (MS) in analytics, computer science, data science, mathematics, statistics, engineering, management information systems, decision science, or related fields
Ideally located in the DC Metro area to attend regularly scheduled meetings
Statistical/Predictive Analysis
Domain Expertise (fraud) or management


Clearance Requirements


Must currently possess an IRS Public Trust clearance with Full Background Investigation


Physical Requirements


Must be able to remain in a stationary position 50%
Needs to occasionally move about inside the office to access file cabinets, office machinery, etc.
Frequently communicates with co-workers, management, and customers, which may involve delivering presentations. Must be able to exchange accurate information in these situations


About Elder Research, Inc - People Centered. Data Driven

Elder Research considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to disability or veteran status or any other status protected under any federal, state, or local law or regulation.

If you need a reasonable accommodation to apply for a position with Elder Research, please email us at careers@elderresearch.com and provide your name and contact information.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About MCU:

At Municipal Credit Union, we believe that an incredible culture helps create a happy and motivated team that works hard to achieve the best results for themselves and their members. For more than 100 years, MCU has provided affordable financial products and convenient services to a membership base that is now comprised of over 600,000 individuals. With each new generation we have the pleasure of interacting with, we maintain the credit union promise of people helping people.

Our Mission:

To help hard-working New Yorkers build better tomorrows

Our Vision:

Be New York's most loved financial institution by helping our members build their best financial futures

Overview: The Data Analyst plays a critical role in transforming data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making. Partner with business stakeholders and other analysts to understand business data needs, capture data requirements, and perform data transformation, integration, and storage. Provide inputs for complex data architecture and perform ELT on large data sets to support departmental and organizational objectives. Advanced skill set and proficiency with data transformation practices, query languages, script languages, ELT/ETL pipelines and analysis techniques.

Responsibilities :

Specific duties include, but are not limited to, the following:


Develop and maintain data pipelines, workflows, and APIs.
Document data assets and map data flows across systems to support transparency, traceability, and compliance.
Define, describe, organize, and categorize data sets to support efficient access and understanding across the organization.
Analyze data to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable insights that inform operational improvements and strategic initiatives.
Design, build, and maintain dashboards, scorecards, and ad hoc reports using tools like Power BI and Tableau.
Translate business needs into data requirements and reporting specifications.
Partner with business units to identify KPIs and develop visualizations that support operational and strategic goals.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reporting platforms.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver insights that inform product, member, and market strategies.
Maintain documentation and metadata for reports and dashboards.
Maintain and regularly update the enterprise data dictionary and a centralized index of reports and dashboards to ensure transparency and accessibility.
Contribute to data literacy efforts by supporting training and enablement programs.
Develop and deliver training sessions, workshops, and documentation to help business users effectively use BI tools.
Provide ongoing support and guidance to users on interpreting dashboards and reports.
Promote self-service analytics and foster a data-driven culture across departments.
Identify and compile data sets using a variety of transformation tools to help predict, improve, and measure the success of key business-to-business outcomes
Analyze and resolve complex data quality issues, utilizing advanced data profiling, cleansing, and monitoring techniques to maintain data integrity and reliability
Clean and organize raw data and generate data tables in support of business intelligence and data science projects
Build working relationships with team members and subject matter experts



Requirements:

Title: Sr. Data & Reporting Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Sr. Data & Reporting Analyst

Location: Santa Ana, CA, USA

Top Skills: SQL, Power BI, SAP

(Korean Bilingual not required but is a plus)




Top 4 things to focus on:

1. Experience in logistics, distribution center, warehouse, and/or supply chain environments

2. High proficiency in MS Excel, SQL, Access, PowerPoint, and BI Tools

3. Proficiency in SAP and WMS (Warehouse Management Systems)

4. Report automation & design




Job Description:




The Sr. Data & Reporting Analyst is responsible for all points of data mining, extraction, manipulation, analysis, and reporting. Accountable for developing and reporting daily, weekly and monthly analysis of key performance indicators related to warehouse operational metrics. This position requires an extremely detailed, timely, consistent and accurate approach to reporting. Having strong written and verbal communication skills are critical to succeed in this position as senior management will be the targeted audiences for the reports and analysis performed by this position. Reporting can also be used to set the priorities throughout the operations, ensuring that key issues are addressed. Daily/weekly briefings and communications are needed to ensure consistent information is shared throughout the operations. This position is a task-oriented role, requiring very good organizational skills and follow through. The position requires interaction with a broad number of employees to validate data and collaborate on discrepancies found. This position is a key role in the critical analysis of costs associated with the logistics warehouse and transportation operations & cost. This position is also responsible for supporting improvement opportunities throughout the organization, for monitoring discrepancies and escalating to appropriate parties.

In order to meet daily responsibilities, the candidate must be proficient with SAP, MS Excel, SQL, PowerPoint; and, must have experience developing dashboards using BI Tools.




Required Knowledge, Education and Experience:

• Minimum 3 years of experience in reports and data analysis within logistics, distribution center, warehouse, and/or supply chain environments

• Experience in cost analysis, and reporting of key performance indicators

• High proficiency in MS Excel (40%), SQL (35%), Access, BI Tools, and PowerPoint (25%) is required

• Experience in a logistics, warehouse, and/or supply chain (operations) environment is required

• Experience with SAP is required

• Bachelor’s Degree in Business Analytics, Engineering, Mathematics, Accounting, or related field

• Results driven and ability to handle multiple projects




Necessary Skills / Attributes:




• Excellent analytical, decision-making, and problem solving skills

• Strong data mining skills and experience extracting & manipulating data from multiple sources

• Critical thinking skills - analysis, interpretation, inference, explanation, self-regulation, open-mindedness, and problem-solving

• Strong math, communication, and organizational skills

• Excellent attention to detail

• Must be comfortable working in a multi-cultural business environment

• Strong verbal and written communications skills

• Ability to employ effective time management skills

• Independently follow and execute work assignments

• Must be able to work without supervision; self-starter

• Ability to interact with all levels of management including executives

• Performs work under time schedules and stresses which are normally periodic or cyclical, including time sensitive job stress, intellectual challenge, constant technical data feedback, and language barriers.




Duties & responsibilities will include but are not limited to:

• Data & business analysis

• Data mining & extraction from multiple sources to build reports

• Daily, weekly, monthly report generation

• Report automation & design

• Maintaining existing dashboards; weekly & monthly analysis and reporting

• Help drive critical business decisions by in depth analysis; provide recommendations based on findings

• Develop models that help with decision-making

• Identify opportunities for performance improvement

• Publish standard and complex ad-hoc reports with varying detail for different audiences

Title: Business Intelligence & Data Specialist
Description:
At ABB, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world.

This Position reports to:

Business Intelligence Manager




As a Business Intelligence & Data Specialist, you will prioritize data quality and management as the foundation of all business intelligence operations. You'll ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across all systems while addressing technical issues and supporting emergency resolutions. Your expertise will enable you to create and maintain ad-hoc and automated reports that deliver actionable insights. You'll also maintain comprehensive documentation and standardization to ensure consistency and usability, while managing access controls and security to meet compliance requirements. This role is essential for enabling stakeholders to make data-driven decisions.

This role is contributing to the Electrification, Installation Products Division in Canada and is located in Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu.

The work model is hybrid, with 2 days per week at the office.


Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets; query databases using SQL to extract, validate, and transform data while ensuring accuracy and consistency across all BI systems
Identify and implement data quality standards; implement anomaly detection and data governance principles
Serve as the front-line point of contact for BI bugs and technical issues; perform investigations and diagnose application problems
Support IT teams and vendors in resolving emergencies and provide business/process knowledge to expedite resolution
Create and maintain reports tailored to business needs; design interactive dashboards that enable data-driven decision-making
Optimize report performance and support ad-hoc reporting requests for stakeholders
Maintain comprehensive BI documentation including processes, procedures, and system configurations; standardize BI interfaces across systems
Document best practices and create training materials for end users; maintain security group reviews and access controls
Support compliance and governance requirements related to data access; identify improvements to enhance access management efficiency and security



Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, or related field
3+ years of hands-on experience with multiple BI platforms and tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Bilingual French-English. English is necessary to communicate with clients outside Quebec.
Advanced proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation; strong experience with data analysis, data quality management, and data quality tools
Strong knowledge of database management systems, data governance frameworks, and compliance principles
Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in data environments with strong problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and documentation skills with strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Customer service orientation with proven ability to support end users and cross-functional stakeholders
Strong ability to manage multiple priorities, respond to urgent issues, and collaborate effectively with IT teams.





Employment Equity & Inclusion Statement (Canada)

ABB values the dedication, commitment, and expertise of all our employees. As an Employment Equity Employer, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We actively support the principles of the Employment Equity Act and strive to build a workforce that reflects Canada’s diversity, including:


Women
Indigenous Peoples
Members of visible minorities
Persons with disabilities


ABB is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know.

Title: Executive Assistant
Description:
Why join Giga


The Pace: We're building at a speed most companies talk about but never actually operate at. If you've been bored somewhere else, you won't be here.
The Impact: Every person at Giga touches the work that matters. No layers, no waiting for approval chains. You'll see your fingerprints on what we ship.
The Team: A team of operators defining the future of AI infrastructure. We recruit A-players from the most innovative companies in the world, and they choose Giga because the work here means more.
The Moment: AI doesn't run without power, and that's us. Giga is the picks and shovels behind the AI revolution. This industry is about to explode, and you'll be in the room where it's already happening.


What You’ll Do

As Executive Assistant to one of Giga Energy's Founders, you'll be the operational backbone that keeps everything running at full speed. This role is for someone who wants to be in the room where decisions happen, not watching from the sidelines. You'll manage the details so your executive can stay focused on the big picture, acting as a trusted partner who anticipates needs, solves problems independently, and never lets anything fall through the cracks. At the core of this role is mastering three things: owning the executive's calendar so every minute is accounted for and protected, managing a high-volume inbox by drafting and responding to emails with the executive's voice and judgment, and handling all bookings — from complex travel itineraries to key meetings and reservations — so nothing is ever left to chance. If you're energized by fast-moving environments, thrive under pressure, and take pride in being the most reliable person in the room, this is the role for you.

Where you’ll work

This role will be based in our Houston office. You must be willing to work in the office on a hybrid schedule, this is dependent on which days your executive is in office.

Responsibilities


Own and manage the executive's calendar end-to-end, proactively resolving conflicts and prioritizing commitments
Draft, manage, and respond to emails on behalf of the executive with accuracy and professionalism
Handle NDAs, confidential documents, and time-sensitive paperwork with full discretion
Coordinate domestic and international travel logistics, including itineraries, accommodations, and ground transportation (~30% travel expected)
Maintain and update CRM records in HubSpot and manage day-to-day workflows across Google Suite
Make sound, independent decisions in the executive's absence — serving as a trusted proxy when needed
Identify and take ownership of time-consuming tasks before they become bottlenecks
Remain available and responsive during extended business hours (7 AM – 11 PM) as operational needs require


Requirements


3+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a comparable high-responsibility administrative role
Proficiency in Google Suite — able to hit the ground running without training
Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment
Proven track record of exercising independent judgment and making decisions on behalf of a senior leader
Strong written communication skills, including experience managing executive-level correspondence
Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with the highest level of discretion
Willingness to work extended hours and remain responsive outside of standard business hours


Bonus points


Experience working in a tech company or high-growth startup environment
Familiarity with Microsoft Excel and other business productivity tools
Background or degree in Business, Engineering, or a related field
Experience coordinating complex, multi-leg travel itineraries
Experience with Hubspot


Who We Hire

We hire people who thrive in chaos and get sharper under pressure. You don't wait to be told what to do, you spot the gap and fill it. When there's work to be done, you do it. You make decisions with the information you have, move fast, and adjust along the way. We're not looking for perfect; we're looking for people who get things done.

Equal Opportunity Employer Statement

Giga Energy is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by federal, state, or local laws. Giga Energy complies with all applicable labor laws, including the California Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) and other relevant state and federal regulations. We provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and encourage applicants who require accommodations during the hiring process to contact us.

Title: Data Analytics Training and Internship Program
Description:
Company Description

Ededge is a ground-breaking organization dedicated to nurturing the potential of our youth and empowering them to explore the vast world of technology and other high-growth fields. Our upcoming initiatives will provide invaluable learning experiences by allowing audiences to gain insights from accomplished leaders, who will share not only their successes but also their journeys through failure. Despite our accomplishments, we remain dedicated to continuous growth, as our ultimate goal is to advance collectively as an organization and as a community. We elevate the industry of Ed-Tech by bringing in the training in the specific fields along with the industry experience.




Visit Our Website to know about us : www.ededgelearning.com




Role Description

This is a remote Data Analytics Training and Internship program role designed for individuals seeking practical experience while developing core analytical skills. Day-to-day activities include working with datasets to clean, organize, and validate data, as well as supporting data modeling and basic statistical analysis tasks. The role involves assisting with the creation of reports, dashboards, and visualizations that help communicate insights to internal stakeholders and mentors. Participants will follow a structured learning path, attend virtual training sessions, receive feedback on assigned projects, and collaborate with peers on team-based analytical exercises. The program is ideal for individuals looking to transition into data analytics or strengthen their capabilities while balancing other academic or professional commitments.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field
0–1 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or a related role
Basic proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets
Introductory knowledge of SQL or databases
Strong attention to detail and analytical thinking
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, essential for effective collaboration and articulation of complex ideas.
Demonstrated ability to work independently and as part of a cohesive team.




Perks & Growth Opportunities

Hands-on experience working on high-impact analytics projects.
Access to expert mentorship and professional development resources.
Potential for performance-based incentives during the internship.
Opportunities for full-time roles may be offered to exceptional interns.
A valuable addition to your portfolio and resume with real-world experience.




Location: Remote




Note : This is an unpaid internship. Upon application, our team will contact you to proceed with the application details and joining process.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Key Responsibilities:

Data Infrastructure development and maintenance:

Maintain scalable data pipelines and architectures using Databricks and other cloud-based platforms
Maintain and implement robust ETL processes to ensure data quality and integrity across various research data sources

Collaboration and Support:

Work closely with Portfolio Managers, analysts and the research team to understand data requirements and deliver solutions that support user objectives
Collaborate with IT and other engineering teams to integrate data solutions into the broader technology ecosystem

Vendor Management:

Manage relationships with research vendors, ensuring data acquisition aligns with business needs and compliance standards
Evaluate and onboard new data vendors, negotiating contracts and ensuring service level agreements are met

Technical Expertise:

Utilize Python and other programming languages to develop data models, scripts, and tools that enhance data processing capabilities
Apply knowledge of AI and machine learning to support advanced analytics and predictive modeling efforts utilizing Cinctive’s AI framework




Qualifications:

4+ years of experience in data engineering, with a focus on hedge funds or financial research environments
Strong technical skillset including expertise using PySpark on Databricks, Python, Kusto/KQL optimization
Familiarity with Tableau, PowerBI, and data warehousing/data lake design
Alt Data experience with a background working with different alt-data source is a plus
Familiarity with AI and machine learning frameworks is a plus
Strong understanding of financial markets, hedge fund operations, and research methodologies
Experience in managing and integrating data from various research vendors, as well as vendor management
Ability to perform high-level work both independently and as part of a team
Excellent organizational and time management skills
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment




Education:

Required: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related field




Compensation:

The salary range for this position is $175,000 to $200,000 per year. The actual base pay is dependent upon several factors, including, but not limited to, relevant experience, business needs and market demands. This role may also be eligible for bonus compensation and employee benefits.

Title: Power BI Admin
Description:
Role: Power BI Admin(Tableau Admin)

Location: Canada (Remote)




Responsibilities:

Administer and maintain Tableau Server environments, ensuring high availability, performance, and scalability.
Manage user access, security groups, roles, permissions, and licensing across BI platforms.
Monitor system health, platform usage, and performance metrics; proactively identify and resolve issues.
Configure and maintain Tableau sites, projects, data sources, extracts, refresh schedules, and subscriptions.
Implement and enforce data governance, security policies, and compliance controls.
Collaborate with BI Developers, Data Engineers, and Business Users to support reporting and analytics initiatives.
Troubleshoot and resolve platform, connectivity, and data refresh issues.
Support deployment, migration, upgrades, and maintenance activities for BI environments.
Develop and maintain operational documentation, standards, and best practices.
Provide end-user support, training, and guidance on Tableau platform usage.




Required Skills:

10+ years of experience in Tableau Administration and BI platform support.
Strong hands-on experience with Tableau Server Administration.
Experience managing users, permissions, authentication methods, and security configurations.
Strong understanding of Tableau architecture, performance tuning, and troubleshooting.
Experience with data source management, extract refreshes, and scheduling.
Knowledge of data governance, compliance, and security best practices.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Experience with Power BI Administration and/or SSRS.
Knowledge of SQL and relational databases.
Experience with Active Directory, SSO, SAML, or LDAP integrations.
Exposure to cloud-based BI environments and enterprise reporting solutions.







Regards,

Praveen Kumar R (Praveen)

Title: Business Systems Analyst, Data Governance
Description:
ORION GROUPS is a professional services firm based in McKinney, Texas that provides experienced consultants to key organizations across the United States. We focus our talent expertise in both the financial and healthcare industries. Our capabilities in technology include application management, enterprise software systems, data strategies, integration, and IT infrastructure. We work diligently to listen and provide value to your unique needs. www.OrionGRPS.com




OVERVIEW

At Orion, we are seeking a Business Systems Analyst to assist the Business Architects on key initiatives, mapping the current and future state of business processes, people and technology, and hitting project milestones through multiple phases. This opportunity would drive the day-to-day deliverables for defensible deletion, partner projects with Data Security, Metric builds, and process documentation.




OPPORTUNITY

6-month contract; Fully Remote (U.S. – Central Time Zone)




SALARY

$115,000 – $130,000 W2 plus benefits




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Build data visualizations to support projects and track ILM Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).




Gathers information from business units and translates them to current work processes within data security and governance tools (key foundational artifacts for build or modification of Data Policy, standards, procedures, ontology maps, and data models).




Reconstructing project provenance following a system upgrade and maturing our tracking to ensure every piece of data shared with external partners (HIEs, Research, etc.) is fully governed, authorized, and documented.




Create a "traceability matrix" that links a signed contract ID to a ServiceNow Project ID and a specific technical interface endpoint.




Maps and enhances existing business processes/functions and recommends changes required to fit a tightly integrated system




Designs and implements projects requiring systems integration, small teams and multiple technical platforms




Assist in maturing governance process around data sharing platform




Collaborates on driving improvement activities to achieve department and organizational goals




Coordinates with functional team to assure consistency and optimization of overall work product




KEY EXPERIENCE

Experience with visualization software such as Looker, Tableau, etc.




Experience with Logic & Querying (SQL, BigQuery) to pull reports from BigQuery to find the “orphaned” data from ServiceNow upgrade




Interoperability standards: basic understanding of healthcare data exchange (HL7, FHIR, or EDI) to accurately map "Data Elements" mentioned in a contract to actual fields in an API or interface.




Data Governance Frameworks: familiarity with the concept of Data Provenance, knowing not just where data is, but its origin and the authority by which it is being shared.




Experience working in a healthcare environment preferred




Experience working in Data Governance preferred




General knowledge of hospital operations, cloud technologies (GCP), contracting, data sharing (HIEs, registries, etc.) the interrelationship of systems, and how technology enables business processes




Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Mathematics or other related major and 2 years of applicable cumulative job specific experience




INTERVIEW PROCESS

Quick turnaround: preliminary interview followed by team interview.




Typically, one round with the hiring manager and team, possibly two rounds if needed.







Orion Groups is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other characteristic protected by law. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact Orion@OrionGRPS.com

Tableau Consultant

Title: Administrative Support
Description:
Job Title: Administrative Support

Location: Tampa, Florida 

Job Type: Full-Time 

Salary: $40,000-$50,000/ Annual




About HemWell MD

HemWell MD is a medical technology company that transforms hemorrhoid care through a proprietary, non-invasive treatment that is fast, effective, and painless. We partner with physicians across the country to deliver consistent results, comprehensive training, and outstanding patient outcomes. As we grow our national footprint, we need sharp, reliable people behind the scenes keeping our operations running smoothly.




About the Role

We are looking for a motivated Administrative Support with an interest in medical device or medical operations to help anchor our internal operations. This is not a generic admin role — you will be a key contributor who keeps our day-to-day organized, supports physician partners when issues arise, and grows into a vital part of our team. If you are organized, eager to learn, comfortable in a fast-moving environment, and excited to be trained and grow with the team, we want to hear from you.




Responsibilities 

Manage day-to-day internal operations including scheduling, communications, and document management
Document common issues and resolutions to build out internal support resources and a shared knowledge base
Serve as a primary point of contact for physician offices and provider partners — guiding them through troubleshooting steps, answering procedural questions, and resolving issues with professionalism and patience
Maintain accurate financial records, process invoices, and support billing workflows 
Comfortable working in a CRM system (entering data, managing contacts, running reports)
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite: Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint
Coordinate with vendors, physician partners, and internal team members with professionalism and speed
Prepare reports, memos, and correspondence; maintain organized digital and physical filing systems
Handle confidential patient and business information with discretion and in compliance with HIPAA standards
Highly organized with strong follow-through; you don’t let things fall through the cracks
Self-directed and dependable — you manage your workload without constant oversight
Manage office supply inventory and place orders as needed
Support leadership with administrative tasks and step in wherever the team needs you

Qualifications 

2+ years of administrative or operations experience in a healthcare, medical, or clinical setting — this is strongly preferred, not optional
Hands-on experience with QuickBooks for invoicing, expense tracking, or financial recordkeeping
Comfortable working in a CRM system (entering data, managing contacts, running reports)
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite: Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint
Exceptional written and verbal communication — you represent us professionally in every interaction
Highly organized with strong follow-through; you don't let things fall through the cracks
Self-directed and dependable — you manage your workload without constant oversight
High school diploma required; Associate or bachelor's degree preferred 




Preferred but not required

Experience supporting a small or growing healthcare / Medical Device Manufacturing company
Experience in a helpdesk, patient services, or provider support role
Experience with QuickBooks and CRM systems
Familiarity with EHR/EMR systems or medical billing workflows




How to Apply  

Please send your resume and a brief cover letter to spaolitto@medfiniti.com. In your cover letter, please highlight your healthcare admin experience, any hands-on work with QuickBooks or CRM tools, and examples of support or troubleshooting you’ve handled. We look forward to hearing from you.

Title: Delivery Support Analyst
Description:
Level Access is a leader in digital accessibility, dedicated to creating inclusive digital experiences for everyone. 

As an Associate Delivery Support Analyst, you are responsible for triaging, routing, or resolving delivery-related support tickets to ensure efficient and timely service. This position involves working closely with internal teams and stakeholders to address issues and maintain operational excellence. You will follow established processes and guidelines to ensure smooth ticket management and delivery. Your main priority is to expertly understand the content of the ticket and route or respond to it quickly and efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

Ticket Management and Triage: 

Review incoming support tickets related to delivery operations, categorize and prioritize them based on predefined criteria, and escalate issues that require specialized handling or fall outside standard guidelines
Assign tickets to appropriate teams or individuals based on issue complexity and urgency, ensuring compliance with SLAs
Monitor ticket queues regularly to ensure timely responses and resolutions; alerting team leads to any delays that may affect service timelines
Respond to ticket handling questions from internal teams and external partners using established workflows, escalating non-standard issues as needed 

Customer Communication

Serve as the first point of contact for internal teams and clients when issues are raised, maintaining clear and consistent communication throughout the ticket resolution process
Provide stakeholders with regular updates on the progress of tickets, ensuring transparency and helping manage expectations
Recognize unresolved or critical issues that require escalation to senior analysts or management and raise these concerns promptly

Quality Control

Perform quality checks on completed tickets or tasks, ensuring they meet established guidelines before finalizing or moving forward in the workflow
Ensure tickets handled by internal teams or external partners adhere to defined workflow standards and routing expectations
Ensure work is rerouted appropriately when it fails to meet quality standards, escalating to senior analysts as necessary
Regularly verify that processes and workflows are being followed as designed, flagging any inconsistencies or inefficiencies to leadership for review

 

Key Skills
Detail-Oriented: Strong attention to detail in ticket management and ensuring accuracy in task categorization and routing
Collaboration and Communication: Comfortable communicating effectively with team members and stakeholders across departments, both verbally and in writing
