Title: Transformation Data & AI
HTML:
A Robert Walters está à procura de um perfil para integrar um cliente de referência no setor financeiro, no departamento de Transformation Data & AI.


Esta é a tua oportunidade de integrares uma equipa focada em iniciativas de transformação organizacional e digital, com forte componente de gestão de dados, governação da informação e ligação entre áreas de negócio e IT.


Principais Responsabilidades:

  • Apoiar o desenvolvimento e implementação de iniciativas de transformação organizacional e digital;
  • Atuar como interface entre áreas de negócio e IT, garantindo alinhamento de requisitos e soluções;
  • Contribuir para práticas de gestão de dados (qualidade, controlo, governação e reporting);
  • Participar em projetos de transformação de sistemas de informação (análise, desenho e implementação);
  • Apoiar a definição e monitorização de requisitos funcionais e de dados;
  • Identificar oportunidades de melhoria de processos e eficiência operacional;
  • Elaborar análises, relatórios e apresentações de suporte à decisão;
  • Acompanhar e reportar iniciativas (estado, riscos e dependências);
  • Colaborar com stakeholders, promovendo uma cultura de dados e transformação.


Requisitos:

  • Mestrado em Matemática, Data Science, Engenharia, Economia, Gestão ou áreas similares;
  • Conhecimentos em gestão de projetos e gestão de dados;
  • Familiaridade com enquadramentos regulatórios como BCBS 239 (valorizado);
  • Conhecimentos de analytics e inteligência artificial;
  • Capacidade de comunicação e tradução entre linguagem técnica e de negócio;
  • Pensamento crítico, adaptação e orientação para a resolução de problemas;
  • Boa capacidade de relacionamento interpessoal e trabalho com stakeholders.


Estás pronto para dar o próximo passo na tua carreira? 🚀


Se sim, candidata-te!

Title: Analytics Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Wir sind nicht der lokale Stromanbieter um die Ecke. Wir sind das globale EnerTech-Unternehmen, das den Energiemarkt revolutionieren wird. Wir haben kein geringeres Ziel, als die global benötigte Energie 100% nachhaltig zu gestalten. Global, agil und zukunftsorientiert verändern wir den Energiemarkt mit neuer Technologie zum Besseren.




Dabei sind unsere Ökostromtarife nur die Basis. Bereits seit 2022 installieren wir Wärmepumpen-Komplettlösungen mit eigenen Fachkräften. Außerdem arbeiten wir aktiv an der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern, Elektromobilitätslösungen und der eigenen Stromerzeugung aus Wind- und Sonnenenergie.




Das schaffen wir allerdings nicht alleine, sondern dazu brauchen wir dich! Werde Teil unseres Teams und fordere den Status Quo heraus. Mit Energie, die gut für den Planeten ist und gut für dein Karma. 💚  Finde jetzt deinen Traumjob und werde Teil der Energierevolution von Octopus Energy Germany.




Für unser Analytics-Team suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt engagierte Analytics Engineers (m/w/d) für zwei spannende Schwerpunkte: Gross Margin & Cost Modelling. 




Beide Rollen leisten einen zentralen Beitrag zur datengetriebenen Steuerung unseres Energiegeschäfts und verbinden moderne Datenarchitekturen mit geschäftskritischen Entscheidungen. Je nach Schwerpunkt arbeitest du entweder an der Transparenz und Optimierung unserer Bruttomarge oder an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Kosten- und Pricing-Logik. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenprodukte, modellierst komplexe Datenlandschaften und schaffst die Grundlage für fundierte Entscheidungen in einem dynamischen Marktumfeld.


Schwerpunkt: Gross Margin


In diesem Bereich bist du das technische Rückgrat unserer Margensteuerung. Du baust und verantwortest zentrale Datenpipelines, die Verbrauchs-, Kosten-, Umsatz- und Rechnungsdaten zusammenführen, um ein vollständiges Bild unserer Bruttomarge zu schaffen. Du liebst es, große und komplexe Datenmengen performant zu modellieren und darauf aufbauend skalierbare Data Products zu entwickeln, die unser Business messbar voranbringen.


Schwerpunkt: Cost Modelling


In diesem Bereich hebst du unsere Datenarchitektur rund um direkte Kosten wie Netzentgelte, Umlagen und Steuern auf das nächste Level. Du fungierst als Bindeglied zwischen komplexen regulatorischen Anforderungen, energiewirtschaftlichen Forecasts und unserer Pricing-Strategie. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenpipelines, behältst auch bei sich wandelnden regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen den Überblick und gestaltest durch moderne Architekturansätze die Zukunftsfähigkeit unserer Systeme aktiv mit.




 




Deine Aufgaben:Datenarchitektur & Pipeline Engineering



Du konzipierst, entwickelst und betreibst skalierbare Datenpipelines und Datenmodelle, die geschäftskritische Prozesse im Energievertrieb unterstützen


Du integrierst und verknüpfst komplexe Datenquellen aus internen Systemen, Marktdaten sowie regulatorischen Quellen und stellst deren konsistente Verarbeitung sicher


Du verantwortest die Transformation, Modellierung und Aufbereitung großer Datenmengen, um fundierte Analysen und datengetriebene Entscheidungen zu ermöglichen




Data Engineering & Plattform-Exzellenz



Du optimierst Datenmodelle und Pipelines hinsichtlich Performance, Skalierbarkeit und Wartbarkeit


Mit modernen Tools wie dbt und Airflow stellst du eine hohe Datenqualität, Stabilität und Transparenz unserer Datenlandschaft sicher


Du treibst die Weiterentwicklung unserer Datenarchitektur voran und etablierst Best Practices im Bereich Analytics Engineering




Business-nahe Datenprodukte & Modellierung



Du entwickelst skalierbare Data Products und analytische Modelle, die zentrale Geschäftsentscheidungen in den Bereichen Pricing, Kostensteuerung und Profitabilität unterstützen


Du übersetzt komplexe fachliche Anforderungen - von energiewirtschaftlichen Forecasts bis hin zu regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen - in robuste und performante Datenlösungen


Du stellst sicher, dass fachliche und regulatorische Änderungen zeitnah in unseren Datenmodellen und Prozessen berücksichtigt werden




Zusammenarbeit & Impact



Du arbeitest eng mit Stakeholdern aus Pricing, Controlling, Energy Markets und weiteren Fachbereichen zusammen, um datengetriebene Lösungen für komplexe Business-Fragestellungen zu entwickeln


Du fungierst als Sparringspartner für analytische und technische Fragestellungen und sorgst dafür, dass unsere Datenprodukte einen direkten Mehrwert für das Business schaffen


Du unterstützt die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung unseres Analytics-Teams durch Wissensaustausch, technische Standards und moderne Engineering-Praktiken







Dein Background:


Du hast ein Studium in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Mathematik, Statistik, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem ähnlichen quantitativen Fach erfolgreich abgeschlossen


Du bringst fundierte Berufserfahrung (Mid-Level bis Senior) als Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer oder in einer vergleichbaren datengetriebenen Rolle mit


Du bist ein Profi im Umgang mit SQL und Python. Idealerweise hast du bereits Erfahrung mit dbt und Apache Airflow in produktiven Umgebungen gesammelt


Du hast ein ausgeprägtes Verständnis für Datenarchitekturen und die Fähigkeit, dynamische fachliche/regulatorische Anforderungen in sauberen, wartbaren Code zu übersetzen


Du arbeitest analytisch, konzeptionell und findest pragmatische Lösungen für technische Herausforderungen


Du kommunizierst klar - auf Deutsch und Englisch - und kannst fachliche Komplexität für Stakeholder verständlich aufbereiten


Du hast eine ausgeprägte Ownership-Mentalität: Du baust Systeme nicht nur, du verantwortest ihren Betrieb und ihre kontinuierliche Anpassung an den Markt










Deine Benefits bei uns:




✅ Vergünstigungen & Vorsorge:

- Aktienoptionen der Octopus Energy Group Ltd.

- Vergünstigte Konditionen für unsere Strom- und Gastarife

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zum Nahverkehrsticket

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zum Mittagessen

- Vergünstigtes Fahrradleasing

- Betriebliche Altersvorsorge




✅ Familie

- Beratungsangebote und Unterstützung bei Kinderbetreuung über HeyNanny

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zur Kinderbetreuung

- Elterngeld-Bonus: Wir stocken dein Elterngeld für 3 Monate auf, damit du deine Elternzeit entspannter genießen kannst 




✅ Sport & Gesundheit

- Zuzahlungen zu Fitness- Angeboten über Wellpass

- Förderung deines mentalen Wohlbefindens mit unserem Partner Open Up




✅ Lernen & Weiterbildung

- Attraktive Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und Aufstiegsmöglichkeiten

- Kostenlose Sprachkurse über Busuu




✅ Extra Goodies

- Gutschein zu deinem Geburtstag 

_________________










Bereit, den Energiemarkt mit uns zu revolutionieren? Erzähle uns, warum genau du uns dabei unterstützen solltest. Schicke uns deinen CV und alles, was wir wissen sollten, über unser Online-Formular. Wir freuen uns auf dich!




Du findest die Stelle super spannend, bist dir aber unsicher, ob du alle geforderten Skills zu 100% erfüllst? Bitte zögere nicht, dich trotzdem zu bewerben!




P.S.: Octopusse gibt es in allen Farben, Formen und Größen. Wir freuen uns auf Mitarbeiter*innen jeden Geschlechts, Alters, jeder sexuellen Identität, Herkunft, Religion, Weltanschauung, mit und ohne Behinderungen. Für uns zählt nur eins: du passt zu uns und brennst darauf, etwas zu verändern.







Where necessary, we sometimes use AI tools to help with parts of our hiring process, like reviewing applications or assessing responses.

We’re lucky to receive a huge amount of interest, so these tools help our recruiters manage that volume, not replace them. Ultimately, every hiring decision (for example, whether to progress your application or not) is always made by a real person in our Talent team.

If you’d like to know more about how we use your data, you can reach out to us via email at talent@octoenergy.com. We’re happy to help!

Title: Personal administrativo
Description:
Descripción

En Global Omnium, necesitamos incorporar personal comprometido y proactivo para unirse a nuestro equipo de Servicios, en el área de Medio Ambiente y Laboratorio .

Tus Responsabilidades


Atender y asesorar a los clientes de manera efectiva, manteniendo altos estándares de servicio al cliente.
Gestionar de manera eficiente las certificaciones de pedidos, asegurando la precisión y puntualidad en la entrega.
Encargarse de la facturación de forma precisa y oportuna, siguiendo los procedimientos internos establecidos.
Administrar la deuda de manera responsable y proactiva, manteniendo una comunicación clara y efectiva con los clientes.
Coordinar la recogida de muestras de los clientes de manera eficiente y puntual.
Realizar el registro adecuado de las muestras del laboratorio.


Requisitos


Ciclo formativo Grado medio en gestión administrativa, o similar.
Experiencia previa en posición administrativa, preferiblemente en entornos de laboratorio o industrias relacionadas.
Excelentes habilidades de comunicación verbal y escrita, con capacidad demostrada para interactuar de manera efectiva con clientes.
Fuertes habilidades organizativas y capacidad.
Competencia en el uso de software y herramientas de gestión de documentos y bases de datos.


¿Qué te ofrecemos?


Incorporación a un equipo de trabajo colaborativo.
Empleo estable y de calidad.
Conciliación familiar.
Formación continua.
Programas de voluntariado.
Hábitos de vida saludable.
Beneficios sociales.


Empresa comprometida con la Diversidad “igualdad de oportunidades entre mujeres y hombres en el empleo, así como, con la integración de personas con diversidad funcional”

SPR-2195

Title: *CALABRIA - RICERCA PER INSERIMENTO IMMEDIATO*
Description:
Per Primaria S.p.A. presente da anni su tutto il territorio nazionale, ricerchiamo varie figure da inserire immediatamente.

Figure ricercate: commerciali, procacciatori, networker, consulenti, area manager.

Ricerchiamo anche persone disoccupate o che vogliono cambiare lavoro per iniziare subito una nuova professione e guadagnare dal primo mese di lavoro.

L'azienda è un Gruppo S.p.A. costituito da 20 aziende ed offre un rimborso spese mensile più provvigioni e rendita al raggiungimento di piccoli obiettivi.

Offre un rimborso di € 1800,00 mese + provvigioni + mantenimento al raggiungimento di 10 consulenze dual/mese.

Per le figure del settore e per chi vuole fare carriera è previsto un piano di incentivazione che permette di raggiungere in breve tempo il terzo livello manageriale.

I settori principali del Gruppo sono: energia green, telefonia, domotica, efficientamento, stazioni di ricarica, illuminazione a led, smart-mobility, sistemi di sanificazione.

HTML:
Per Primaria S.p.A. presente da anni su tutto il territorio nazionale, ricerchiamo varie figure da inserire immediatamente.

Figure ricercate: commerciali, procacciatori, networker, consulenti, area manager.

Ricerchiamo anche persone disoccupate o che vogliono cambiare lavoro per iniziare subito una nuova professione e guadagnare dal primo mese di lavoro.

L'azienda è un Gruppo S.p.A. costituito da 20 aziende ed offre un rimborso spese mensile più provvigioni e rendita al raggiungimento di piccoli obiettivi.

Offre un rimborso di € 1800,00 mese + provvigioni + mantenimento al raggiungimento di 10 consulenze dual/mese.

Per le figure del settore e per chi vuole fare carriera è previsto un piano di incentivazione che permette di raggiungere in breve tempo il terzo livello manageriale.

I settori principali del Gruppo sono: energia green, telefonia, domotica, efficientamento, stazioni di ricarica, illuminazione a led, smart-mobility, sistemi di sanificazione.

Title: Impiegato/a Data Entry - Servizi Integrati

Diploma di scuola secondaria di secondo grado

Ottima dimestichezza con l'uso del computer e del pacchetto Office con particolare focus su Excel

Retribuzione 1540-2080 € lordi mensili

Title: Técnico/a de Gestión de Medio Ambiente
Description:
¿Te apasiona la sostenibilidad, la protección de la biodiversidad y la gestión ambiental en entornos industriales? ¿Te motiva impulsar iniciativas que tengan un impacto real en la transición energética? ¿Quieres formar parte de una compañía líder en energías renovables a nivel global?

En Iberdrola buscamos incorporar una persona para reforzar nuestro equipo de Medio Ambiente en el área de Renovables. Serás responsable de garantizar la mejora continua en la gestión ambiental de nuestras instalaciones en operación, asegurando el cumplimiento normativo, la implementación de buenas prácticas y la consecución de objetivos alineados con las directrices del Grupo, los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible (ODS) y la protección de la biodiversidad.

Este rol será clave para coordinar la gestión ambiental a nivel transversal, colaborar con diferentes áreas del negocio y fomentar una cultura de sostenibilidad dentro y fuera de la organización.

¿Qué vas a hacer?


Asegurar la implementación y el mantenimiento de todas aquellas medidas de protección de la biodiversidad (fauna, flora,…) se encuentran implantadas, y de todas aquellas otras que como consecuencia de la mejora continua de las instalaciones se puedan plantear.
Asegurarás la correcta gestión ambiental de las instalaciones en operación, liderando la implantación de buenas prácticas, el cumplimiento normativo y el desarrollo de iniciativas de sostenibilidad y biodiversidad.
Representación de Renovables en los Comités de Medio Ambiente, Sostenibilidad y Biodiversidad del Grupo Iberdrola.
Traslado e implantación de las directrices ambientales corporativas en el negocio.
Coordinación de la gestión ambiental de instalaciones renovables, creando y dinamizando grupos de trabajo con equipos regionales.
Definición e implantación de modelos de buenas prácticas ambientales comunes.
Asegurar la correcta aplicación del Sistema de Gestión Ambiental (ISO 14001).
Identificación, evaluación y seguimiento de aspectos e impactos ambientales, definiendo medidas preventivas y correctoras.
Garantizar el cumplimiento de la legislación ambiental y requisitos administrativos aplicables.
Gestión de la interlocución con Administraciones Públicas en materia ambiental.
Revisión ambiental de nuevas instalaciones antes de su entrada en operación.
Coordinación con equipos de desarrollo y promoción para asegurar el cumplimiento ambiental desde fases tempranas.
Definición de objetivos ambientales de mejora continua alineados con la estrategia del Grupo.
Impulso de iniciativas de biodiversidad y sostenibilidad mediante la colaboración con entidades externas (universidades, administraciones, etc.).
Seguimiento y reporte de indicadores, resultados y objetivos ambientales (SYGRIS).
Comunicación transparente de resultados a stakeholders internos y externos.
Gestión y contratación de servicios ambientales (residuos, seguimiento ambiental, etc.).
Elaboración de requisitos ambientales para licitaciones y proveedores.
Seguimiento del desempeño de contratistas.
Formación en materia ambiental a personal de nueva incorporación.
Participación activa en la mejora continua y desarrollo de la cultura ambiental en la organización.


¿Qué estamos buscando?


Grado en Biología, Ciencias Ambientales o titulación similar.
Experiencia mínima de 5 años en gestión ambiental en empresa (valorable en sector energético o industrial).


Conocimientos Técnicos


Biología: zoología, botánica, ecología, gestión de ecosistemas y biodiversidad.
Legislación ambiental y cumplimiento normativo.
Gestión de residuos, emisiones, vertidos y consumos.
Identificación y evaluación de aspectos ambientales.
Sistemas de gestión ambiental (ISO 14001) y auditorías.


Otros Conocimientos Valorables


Economía circular y eficiencia energética.
Gestión de proyectos.
Análisis y evaluación de riesgos ambientales.
Gestión de compras y proveedores (SAP, e-sourcing).


Herramientas


ArcGIS
Excel y Access (nivel avanzado)
Herramientas corporativas de gestión ambiental (ej. SYGRIS)
Nivel de inglés alto.


Competencias


Perfil ambientalista con fuerte enfoque en biodiversidad (fauna y flora).
Capacidad de coordinación transversal y trabajo en equipo.
Rigor técnico y orientación al cumplimiento.
Habilidades de comunicación e interlocución con múltiples stakeholders.
Proactividad y orientación a mejora continua.


Lo que ofrecemos


Formar parte de una compañía global, diversa e innovadora.
Participar en proyectos estratégicos de alto impacto para el Grupo.
Entorno colaborativo con equipos multidisciplinares en diferentes geografías.
Planes de desarrollo profesional y formación continua.
Oportunidad de trabajar en proyectos técnicamente retadores y con impacto real en la transición energética.


Nuestro proceso de selección

En Iberdrola la diversidad, equidad e inclusión son esenciales en nuestro día a día. Nos enorgullece ser una compañía donde todas las personas son bienvenidas, valoradas y respetadas, independientemente de sexo, discapacidad, raza, religión, orientación sexual, edad, estado civil, nacionalidad o identidad de género. Fomentamos un entorno inclusivo donde cada persona puede aportar su talento y desarrollarse plenamente.

Fecha De Finalización De La Publicación

13-07-2026

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Analytics Engineer / Data Engineer - Malta (Hybrid or Fully Remote) - You need to be based in Malta.




iGaming Startup | Real-Time Data | Market Prediction

Competitive Salary up to €80k base + High Impact Role

Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this position.




We're partnering with an ambitious Malta-based iGaming startup building next-generation platform technology from the ground up. This is a rare opportunity to join early, influence core architecture, and shape how critical business metrics are defined across a real-time, event-driven platform.




You'll sit at the intersection of Data Engineering and Analytics, transforming player activity, transactions, and event streams into trusted, scalable data products that drive business decisions.




What you'll be doing:

Building and owning the dbt layer
Designing scalable data models and metric definitions
Working with real-time event data from Kafka-based systems
Turning ambiguous business requirements into robust data solutions
Driving automation and leveraging AI tools to accelerate development
Collaborating closely with engineering, product, and commercial teams




Tech Stack:

Java
Apache Kafka
SQL
AWS (Athena, S3, Glue)
PostgreSQL
dbt




What we're looking for:

5+ years in Analytics Engineering, Data Engineering, or a hybrid role
Strong SQL and data modelling expertise
Experience with cloud data platforms and modern data tooling
Comfortable operating in a fast-moving startup environment
Automation-first mindset with experience using AI coding tools
iGaming experience (player lifecycles, transactions, GGR) highly desirable




Why join?

Build a modern data platform from scratch
High ownership and technical influence
Work on complex real-time data challenges
Join an ambitious, well-funded startup at a pivotal growth stage
Hybrid or fullty remote working in Malta




Interested? Apply now or message me directly for a confidential discussion.

Description:
Únete a Nuestro Equipo

En Agenor Mantenimientos, fomentamos una cultura centrada en la excelencia, la innovación y el compromiso con la calidad. Creemos en el trabajo en equipo, el aprendizaje continuo y la creatividad para superar desafíos.

Si estás preparado/a para unirte a un equipo apasionado que trabaja en la vanguardia de la tecnología médica y el cuidado de la salud, ¡esperamos recibir tu solicitud!

Podemos construir un futuro donde la excelencia y la innovación definan la atención médica.

UBICACIÓN:

Complejo Hospitalario Universitario de Badajoz

RESPONSABILIDADES/FUNCIONES


Gestión de solicitudes: Recepción, análisis y tramitación de solicitudes de compra y venta, coordinando con solicitantes, proveedores y clientes.
Seguimiento de pedidos: Supervisión del estado de los pedidos para asegurar entregas a tiempo y con la calidad esperada.
Resolución de incidencias: Identificación y gestión de incidencias con proveedores, técnicos o clientes, proponiendo soluciones efectivas.
Registro administrativo: Gestión y actualización de la información en el sistema ERP (presupuestos, pedidos, albaranes, trazabilidad, stock).
Documentación y trazabilidad: Control de documentos, comunicación interna y seguimiento de flujos de aprobación en la aplicación de solicitudes.
Cumplimiento de procedimientos: Aplicación rigurosa de los procesos definidos por el departamento de compras.

Salario competitivo

Formación profesional grado superior en electrónica o electromedicina clínica.

Conocimiento mantenimiento electrómedico

Title: Impiegato/a Amministrativo/a - Grande Distribuzione
Description:

Gestione e registrazione delle fatture attive e passive

Controllo e riconciliazione dei movimenti contabili

Supporto nella predisposizione di report e analisi contabili

Collaborazione con il team amministrativo e con altre funzioni aziendali per garantire efficienza e accuratezza dei dati.

Requisiti:

Diploma di scuola secondaria superiore o laurea in discipline economiche/aziendali

Pregressa esperienza in ruoli amministrativi, preferibilmente nel settore GDO o retail

Ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Office, in particolare Excel

Precisione, affidabilità e capacità di lavorare in autonomia e in team

Buone capacità organizzative e attitudine al problem solving.

Offerta:

Contratto a tempo determinato di 3 mesi rinnovabile

Orario di lavoro full-time, 40 ore settimanali.

Retribuzione 1730-2350 € lordi mensili

Title: Analyst, Data Management
Description:
Description

Join a fast-growing company that's transforming the industry! Financial Recovery Technologies (FRT) is a technology-based services firm that helps institutional investors such as hedge funds, mutual funds, custodians, and the like identify, file claims, and collect funds made available in securities class action settlements globally, and our best-in-class people and technology have made FRT a market leader.

As an Analyst on the Client Data Management Team, you will:


Quickly and accurately extract, load and transform client data
Use ETL tools to load FRT client data
Develop SQL programming/scripting experience, specifically in Oracle SQL
Be a source of new business requirements that can be used to automate data loading processes
Identify and communicate data loading issues
Quickly synthesize issues for colleagues to look at/resolve
Document gaps in existing processes
Verify quality of client data through existing processes
Build strong relationships with peers across corporate functions, including:
Data Integration and Client Services professionals, who you’ll work with to provide feedback on imported client data
Operations professionals, who will rely on your ETL skills to file claims, remediate deficiencies, and remit payments
Product and technology teams, who will count on you to serve as a QA resource as they develop technology to support operational requirements

Our Ideal Candidate Has


2-5 years of professional experience in an ETL role using SQL;
A Bachelor’s degree in a Business or Quantitative field (or 1-2 additional years of experience in a data-driven role);
Knowledge of database and data storage concepts;
A willingness to work with messy data;
Self-direction and detail orientation, with strong analytical, problem solving, and creative thinking skills;
A team-first approach, prioritizing collaboration over credit, and interested in building strong relationships with peers across corporate functions;
The mind of a planner, identifying and analyzing problems, proposing solutions, and anticipating future problem areas before initiating action;
Intellectual curiosity, with a demonstrated commitment to continuous growth; and
Of course, a desire to join a growing company with a vibrant, entrepreneurial culture, dedicated to being the top provider in the class action recovery space.


What Frt Offers


Competitive salary and bonus ($60,000-$80,000 annual base salary)
Health, dental, vision
401k (with company match)
Income protection plans (life, accidental death and dismemberment, short- and long-term disability) and access to a suite of voluntary benefits
Fun, smart, diverse colleagues
We are a hybrid company; in office, we offer:
Close to public transit (walking distance to Wellington T on Orange Line)
Free drinks and snacks
Free parking onsite
Free access to onsite gym

THIS POSITION WILL BE BASED IN OUR MEDFORD, MA HEADQUARTERS. LOCAL CANDIDATES ONLY, PLEASE.

AGENCIES: WE ARE NOT WORKING WITH AGENCIES ON THIS REQUISITION.

FRT provides equal employment opportunities by recruiting, hiring, training and promoting applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, age, ancestry, sexual orientation, genetics, pregnancy, marital or partnership status, gender identity/expression, disability, handicap, status as a victim of domestic violence or other similar offenses, consumer credit history, unemployment, military obligations, veteran status or any other category protected by law.

FRT is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the application or employment process, please let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

Title: Systems and Analytics Specialist
Description:
Summary

Reporting to the Team Lead, Pharmacy Business Operations and Clinical Intelligence is intricately involved in the day to day technology integrated within all areas of pharmacy services across Brown University Health (i.e., clinical, operational, safety, quality, financial, regulatory, accreditation, supply chain, retail, academia). Develops custom reports and data visualizations. Maintains a data warehouse along with associated Extract Transfer and Load (ETL) processes. Monitors and improves existing business practices through use of technology and data.

Brown University Health employees are expected to successfully role model the organization's values of Compassion, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence as these values guide our everyday actions with patients, customers and one another.

In Addition To Our Values, All Employees Are Expected To Demonstrate The Core Success Factors Which Tell Us How We Work Together And How We Get Things Done. The Core Success Factors Include

Instill Trust and Value Differences

Patient and Community Focus and Collaborate

Responsibilities


Utilize technical, analytical, and programming skills to support all areas of pharmacy services (i.e., clinical, operational, safety, quality, financial, regulatory, accreditation, supply chain, retail, academia).
Maintain the pharmacy’s data warehouse.
Design, build and troubleshoot data Extract, Transfer Load (ETL) processes.
Design, build and troubleshoot highly functional data visualizations utilizing Microsoft PowerBI or similar tools.
Collaborate with pharmacy clinical and operational staff to understand health system needs and devise possible solutions.
Keep up to date with the latest technology trends.
Collaborate in the design on business processes to allow for appropriate and efficient data collection.
Work jointly with stakeholders through the healthcare organization.
Take part in and complete technological project work.
Maintain and troubleshoot internal webpages both interactive and static
Maintain and troubleshoot Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Access and Filemaker solutions.
Design and/or implement solutions to security vulnerabilities and concert with Brown University Health information systems security.
Serve as an expert in the functionality and troubleshooting of pharmacy technology systems.
Responsible for weekly rotating on call coverage.


Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Science, or related field.
Minimum 3 years experience with SQL, data analytics and programming.Strong background in writing SQL.Able to understand various data structures and common methods in data transformation.
Proven analytic and process redesign skills.
Highly proficient in Microsoft Office tools such as Work, Excel and Access.


WORK ENVIRONMENT AND PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Work is performed in a typical office environment, requiring extended periods of sitting, standing and walking. Requires the visual and manual dexterity to operate a computer.

INDEPENDENT ACTION: Functions independently within broad scope of department policies and procedures.

Refers specific complex problems to Team Lead and when clarification of departmental policies and procedures may be required.

SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES:None

Pay Range

$78,416.00-$129,355.20

EEO Statement

Brown University Health is committed to providing equal employment opportunities and maintaining a work environment free from all forms of unlawful discrimination and harassment.

Location:

Rhode Island Hospital - 593 Eddy Street Providence, Rhode Island 02903

Work Type

M-F 8:00am-5:00pm

Work Shift

Day

Daily Hours

8 hours

Driving Required

No

Title: Data Analyst II (remote)
Description:
Job Seekers can review the Job Applicant Privacy Policy by clicking here (http://ryder.com/job-applicant-privacy-policy) .

Summary

Job Description :

The Data Analyst II is a strategic contributor on Ryder's Supply Chain Solutions (SCS) Products & Analytics team, supporting the Automotive & Industrial (AAI) vertical. This role partners with operations, customers, and cross-functional teams to turn complex supply chain data into durable, reusable analytics products that drive measurable business outcomes — margin, labor, service, and operational performance.

Working on Ryder's modern data stack (Azure, Snowflake, Power BI, Copilot), the Data Analyst II designs persona-based dashboards and analytics solutions that solve once for many , rather than one-off reports. Over time, the analyst will become a domain expert in automotive and industrial supply chain operations.

AI Is Core To How This Team Works.

The Data Analyst II is expected to leverage AI daily to accelerate discovery, diagnose root causes, synthesize customer signals, and deliver insights faster and at scale. Success means owning outcomes end-to-end, making recommendations (not just executing tasks), and tying every initiative to a real business decision and a measurable result.

Essential Functions


Develop Dashboards by understanding business needs, interpret the integrated data and translate this into usable visualizations to drive business decisions, interpreting results and how to utilize the dashboards
Engage the business, evaluating their needs and ad-hoc requests to prioritize product efforts creating new innovative solutions.
Knowledge of the Ryder Business, understanding the complexity of the supply chain world and the role that data has within this ecosystem.
Enhance data maturity and integration to standardize the core value add functionality enabling a multiplier effect.


Additional Responsibilities


Performs other duties as assigned.


Skills And Abilities


Experience developing BI solutions with tools like:
Power BI
Tableau
Exposure using tools like:
ETL tools (Alteryx or SSIS)
Data manipulation tools (SQL)
Object oriented development stacks (.net) (preferred)
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with an ability to express complex technical concepts in business terms.
Support product innovation, working closely with technology throughout the build, test, and release of the product using agile methodologies.


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree required Supply Chain, Data Analytics or equivalent.
Master's degree preferred Supply Chain, Data Analytics, MBA or equivalent
Two (2) years or more Applied Project(s) Experience: Experience in demonstrating, digital transformation, data management in an educational/project or intern type environment required.
Two (2) years or more 2-4 years' experience in creating dashboards for diagnostic analysis across the business. Experience working in a cloud environment a plus. preferred


DOT Regulated: No

Job Category: Data Science & Analytics

Our Culture & Commitment

At Ryder, you’re trusted to make an impact—while enjoying room to grow and having a voice that’s heard. Our culture is built on respect, collaboration, and shared pride in doing great work rooted in innovation and safety.

Your Voice. Your Success. The Future We Build Together.

The compensation offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors, including the candidate’s relevant experience; education, including relevant degrees or certifications; work location; market data/ranges; internal equity; internal salary ranges; etc. The position may also be eligible to receive an annual bonus, commission, and/or long-term incentive plan based on the level and/or type. Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Purple Carrot

Founded in 2014, Purple Carrot delivers clean, long-lasting energy through the power of plants. As the only company in America devoted entirely to plant-based eating, we have spent over a decade perfecting the balance of protein and fiber that only plants can provide. Offering chef-crafted, dietitian-designed meals delivered right to your door, we make plant-forward eating effortless, flexible, and endlessly exciting. Our menu spans quick weeknight dishes, occasion-worthy dinners, breakfast, lunch, snacks, and ready-to-eat meals.

Purple Carrot prioritizes taste and flexibility, offering no membership fees, the freedom to skip or unskip weekly deliveries whenever you want, and a no-pressure, cancel-anytime policy. We pride ourselves on doing plants better than anyone else - and we have the receipts to prove it.

Description

We are looking for an Analytics Engineer to join our mission-driven team. As an integral part of the Data team, you will join forces with the Senior Analytics Engineer in our continued effort to inspire people to eat more plants.

This position will play an important role in building and maintaining resilient, scalable data pipelines and company-wide data infrastructure that empowers Purple Carrot employees to better understand, manage, curate, and analyze the data most relevant to their work.

As the Analytics Engineer, you will be focused on maintaining and improving our data pipelines, supporting data systems and analytics, and helping develop rigorous data products within our modern data stack. This role will synthesize feedback from teams across the business and help translate data needs into reliable, well-documented solutions that allow us to move faster and gain better insights into how our business performs.

We greatly thank all applicants; however, only those under serious consideration will be contacted.

Location


This hybrid role is based in Dedham, MA
Our hybrid approach includes 3 days in-office each week, giving teams the best of both flexibility and collaboration. Many team members choose to come in more often - it's a great way to stay inspired and engaged with the team



On the Plate


Lead and contribute to the implementation and maintenance of end-to-end data pipelines and infrastructure, from ingestion through transformation and analytics
Develop, test, and optimize data models and data warehouse architecture with a focus on performance, scalability, and reliability
Ensure data quality through validation, monitoring, testing, and strong documentation practices
Implement and uphold data governance standards, including data dictionaries, lineage, access controls, and clear ownership of business logic
Build and maintain BI dashboards and reporting tools that help teams understand performance and make data-informed decisions
Support occasional ad hoc and exploratory data analysis to help answer business questions and identify trends
Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for data models, pipelines, reporting logic, and key data definitions
Collaborate closely with the Senior Analytics Engineer and cross-functional partners to execute on defined workstreams within an established data framework
Assist with ad-hoc projects and tasks as necessary



Main Ingredients for the Role


2-3 years of experience in the data space as a senior data analyst, data analyst, analytics engineer, data engineer, or equivalent role
Bachelor's degree in data analytics, computer science, information systems, business analytics, mathematics, statistics, or a related field, or equivalent experience
Able to commute to the Dedham, MA office 3 days per week
Proficiency in SQL and dbt for data transformation, automation, and modeling
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines using modern data stack technologies such as Git, dbt, Stitch, cloud data warehouses, or similar tools
Strong background in data quality assurance, governance frameworks, and best practices for documentation
Experience with BI and reporting tools such as Looker, Omni, or similar platforms
Ability to work independently on assigned workstreams while collaborating closely with a senior team member
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment



Extra Special Sauce


Experience working in or supporting a B2C subscription business model
Comfortable working within an established data framework while driving execution independently on defined workstreams
Strong curiosity and ability to translate business questions into clear, scalable data solutions
Excitement for working in a dynamic environment where data helps teams make faster, smarter decisions
You enjoy documentation, process improvement, and building systems that make work easier for others



Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this role is $90,000 - $100,00. Compensation is based on experience and qualifications, and the posted range is not a guarantee of any specific offer.

Garnish

We love our staff, and show it with benefits (including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, FSA, and life insurance), matching retirement plan, generous and flexible paid time off, Purple Carrot box delivery credits, professional development, and the opportunity to work in collaboration with teammates who are passionate about food!

Final Touches

We are committed to a safe and inclusive work environment, free of discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetics, national origin, ancestry, age, medical condition, mental or physical disability, handicap, veteran status, or marital status.

As the only company in America devoted entirely to plant-based eating, we have spent over a decade perfecting the balance of protein and fiber that only plants can provide. Offering chef-crafted, dietitian-designed meals delivered right to your door, we make plant-forward eating effortless, flexible, and endlessly exciting. Our menu spans quick weeknight dishes, occasion-worthy dinners, breakfast, lunch, snacks, and ready-to-eat meals.

Purple Carrot prioritizes taste and flexibility, offering no membership fees, the freedom to skip or unskip weekly deliveries whenever you want, and a no-pressure, cancel-anytime policy. We pride ourselves on doing plants better than anyone else - and we have the receipts to prove it.

Description

We are looking for an Analytics Engineer to join our mission-driven team. As an integral part of the Data team, you will join forces with the Senior Analytics Engineer in our continued effort to inspire people to eat more plants.

This position will play an important role in building and maintaining resilient, scalable data pipelines and company-wide data infrastructure that empowers Purple Carrot employees to better understand, manage, curate, and analyze the data most relevant to their work.

As the Analytics Engineer, you will be focused on maintaining and improving our data pipelines, supporting data systems and analytics, and helping develop rigorous data products within our modern data stack. This role will synthesize feedback from teams across the business and help translate data needs into reliable, well-documented solutions that allow us to move faster and gain better insights into how our business performs.

We greatly thank all applicants; however, only those under serious consideration will be contacted.

Location

  • This hybrid role is based in Dedham, MA
  • Our hybrid approach includes 3 days in-office each week, giving teams the best of both flexibility and collaboration. Many team members choose to come in more often - it's a great way to stay inspired and engaged with the team


On the Plate

  • Lead and contribute to the implementation and maintenance of end-to-end data pipelines and infrastructure, from ingestion through transformation and analytics
  • Develop, test, and optimize data models and data warehouse architecture with a focus on performance, scalability, and reliability
  • Ensure data quality through validation, monitoring, testing, and strong documentation practices
  • Implement and uphold data governance standards, including data dictionaries, lineage, access controls, and clear ownership of business logic
  • Build and maintain BI dashboards and reporting tools that help teams understand performance and make data-informed decisions
  • Support occasional ad hoc and exploratory data analysis to help answer business questions and identify trends
  • Create and maintain comprehensive documentation for data models, pipelines, reporting logic, and key data definitions
  • Collaborate closely with the Senior Analytics Engineer and cross-functional partners to execute on defined workstreams within an established data framework
  • Assist with ad-hoc projects and tasks as necessary


Main Ingredients for the Role

  • 2-3 years of experience in the data space as a senior data analyst, data analyst, analytics engineer, data engineer, or equivalent role
  • Bachelor's degree in data analytics, computer science, information systems, business analytics, mathematics, statistics, or a related field, or equivalent experience
  • Able to commute to the Dedham, MA office 3 days per week
  • Proficiency in SQL and dbt for data transformation, automation, and modeling
  • Experience building and maintaining data pipelines using modern data stack technologies such as Git, dbt, Stitch, cloud data warehouses, or similar tools
  • Strong background in data quality assurance, governance frameworks, and best practices for documentation
  • Experience with BI and reporting tools such as Looker, Omni, or similar platforms
  • Ability to work independently on assigned workstreams while collaborating closely with a senior team member
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment


Extra Special Sauce

  • Experience working in or supporting a B2C subscription business model
  • Comfortable working within an established data framework while driving execution independently on defined workstreams
  • Strong curiosity and ability to translate business questions into clear, scalable data solutions
  • Excitement for working in a dynamic environment where data helps teams make faster, smarter decisions
  • You enjoy documentation, process improvement, and building systems that make work easier for others


Compensation

The anticipated base salary range for this role is $90,000 - $100,00. Compensation is based on experience and qualifications, and the posted range is not a guarantee of any specific offer.

Garnish

We love our staff, and show it with benefits (including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, FSA, and life insurance), matching retirement plan, generous and flexible paid time off, Purple Carrot box delivery credits, professional development, and the opportunity to work in collaboration with teammates who are passionate about food!

Final Touches

We are committed to a safe and inclusive work environment, free of discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetics, national origin, ancestry, age, medical condition, mental or physical disability, handicap, veteran status, or marital status.

Title: (USA) Senior, Data Analyst
Description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...Senior Data Analyst Req ID: R-2469362 (USA) Senior, Data Analyst

Location: Raymond, NH Role Summary Join Walmart as a Senior Data Analyst supporting field operations within the ambient supply chain network. This role is focused on improving how frontline managers operate by reducing manual work, automating workflows, and enabling smarter, faster decision-making through data and AI-driven solutions. You will partner directly with operations teams to identify pain points, translate business challenges into scalable technical solutions, and build tools that enhance efficiency across distribution centers. This position blends strong technical expertise with business acumen and stakeholder influence, making it ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world operational problems with data and technology. About the Team You will join a small, high-impact technical team led by Spencer Jordan that supports Walmart’s ambient supply chain, specifically general merchandise distribution centers. The team develops network-wide tools and solutions used across both field operations and the home office (HQ). This team is focused on driving innovation through data, automation, and AI—building scalable solutions that improve operational efficiency and decision-making. With a collaborative and fast-paced environment, the team partners closely with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver measurable business impact. What You’ll Do


Partner with field operations teams to understand workflow challenges and identify opportunities for improvement
Design and build scalable data and automation solutions to reduce manual processes
Leverage AI and intelligent workflows to enhance speed, accuracy, and decision-making
Translate complex business problems into actionable, data-driven solutions
Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations to communicate insights effectively
Identify, evaluate, and manage data sources to ensure accuracy and reliability
Perform data quality assessments and maintain ongoing monitoring processes
Collaborate cross-functionally with operations, product, and analytics teams to implement solutions
Influence stakeholders and drive adoption of new tools and processes
Mentor junior analysts and promote best practices in data analysis and visualization


What You’ll Bring


Strong technical skills in tools and technologies such as Python, SQL, JavaScript, Next.js, Linux environments, and API integration
Experience building tools or solutions that automate workflows and improve operational efficiency
Background in data analytics, software development, or web development
Familiarity with applying AI or intelligent automation to solve business problems
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business needs into technical solutions
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work cross-functionally and influence without direct authority


At Walmart, we offer competitive pay as well as performance-based bonus awards and other great benefits for a happier mind, body, and wallet. Health benefits include medical, vision and dental coverage. Financial benefits include 401(k), stock purchase and company-paid life insurance. Paid time off benefits include PTO (including sick leave), parental leave, family care leave, bereavement, jury duty, and voting. Other benefits include short-term and long-term disability, company discounts, Military Leave Pay, adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, and more. You will also receive PTO and/or PPTO that can be used for vacation, sick leave, holidays, or other purposes. The amount you receive depends on your job classification and length of employment. It will meet or exceed the requirements of paid sick leave laws, where applicable. For information about PTO, see https://one.walmart.com/notices. Live Better U is a Walmart-paid education benefit program for full-time and part-time associates in Walmart and Sam's Club facilities. Programs range from high school completion to bachelor's degrees, including English Language Learning and short-form certificates. Tuition, books, and fees are completely paid for by Walmart.

Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment. Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to a specific plan or program terms.

For Information About Benefits And Eligibility, See One.Walmart.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
PMA’s Risk Control Department is seeking a self-motivated, solution-oriented Data Analyst who enjoys building the data models, tools, systems, and processes necessary to enhance and streamline the way data is utilized and processed currently.

As a member of our Risk Control Department, you will have the opportunity to positively impact efficiency and profitability by designing, and re-designing the way data is extracted, transformed, loaded. You will perform assignments, tasks or activities that directly and indirectly contribute to the fulfillment of the corporation's and department's overall business objectives.

Responsibilities


Collect, evaluate, run, and design statistical data/reports on a monthly and ad hoc basis.
Prepare statistical and narrative reports and recommendations and participate in the analysis and interpretation of data as appropriate.
Support various project initiatives
Develop, maintain, and ensure quality control of data sources, queries, models, and results.
Run existing analytical reports and assist with defining and designing new reports to meet business needs.
Utilize data visualization tools to effectively illustrate data trends and patterns.
Participate in the development, implementation, and enhancement of data models and data warehouse tables to ensure that business needs are met.
Develop and implement data query and mining techniques that optimize statistical efficiency and data quality. This may include cleansing and normalizing of data or development of internal control reporting to ensure data validity going forward.
Assists in the application and implementation procedures of data standards and guidelines to ensure access to and integrity of data sets.
Identify, troubleshoot, and remediate issues that are produced from anomalies in the data.
Develop self-service reporting and informational tools for end users through defining business requirements, application and/or query design, testing, solutions implementation and end user training.
Assist in the testing and implementation of new or upgraded software.
Work closely with management and other department heads to prioritize business and information needs.
Provide data consulting in support of business and information technology initiatives to stakeholders to improve database systems.
Assist in the development of a system by which other analysts may be effectively educated on the various data analyst techniques employed.
Collaborate with both internal and external stakeholders to clearly define and document information/data requirements.
Support compliance and audit requirements
Demonstrate commitment to Company's Code of Business Conduct and Ethics, and apply knowledge of compliance policies and procedures, standards and laws applicable to job responsibilities in the performance of work


Requirements


BS in Mathematics, Economics, Business, Risk Management, Computer Science, Information Management, Statistics or related fields
Experience as a data analyst in commercial casualty insurance industry, with emphasis in workers compensation preferred
Adept at query development, report writing and presenting findings
Understanding of data modeling and data cleansing techniques
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and communicate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Excellent visual design sense regarding clear and accurate presentation of data
Ability to interact with business and technology experts to help determine the technical feasibility of requirements
Ability to effectively communicate technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to organize and balance multiple concurrent timelines and tasks to meet deadlines
Superb business process analysis and interpersonal skills
Strong knowledge of tools such as Excel, Access, Power BI, Visual Basic, database development
Working familiarity with tools such as QlikSense, Tableau, Hyperion, Access, SharePoint, MicroStrategy, SQL, R and Python preferred

Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

ButcherBox is a certified B Corp headquartered in Boston, and honored to be celebrating our fourth year in a row on Inc.'s list of Best Workplaces. At ButcherBox, we believe in better. That’s why we deliver 100% grass-fed, grass-finished beef, free-range organic chicken, pork raised crate-free, and wild-caught seafood directly to our members’ doors. All of our products are humanely raised or wild-caught and never given antibiotics or added hormones ever.

We’re working to build a world that’s better for all, and we’re inviting everyone to come along. For us, better means treating our planet with respect. It means improving the lives of animals and the livelihoods of farmers. It means never cutting corners when it comes to doing business. Ultimately, it means better meals, enjoyed together. Our team is made up of people who collaborate and support one another. We’re always looking for outstanding people to join our mission!

About The Role

We are seeking a Procurement Analyst to help us deliver the best quality meat and seafood to our customers and to create data assets and insights that make that possible at scale. This role requires someone with strong analytical skills who can work closely with stakeholders and vendors to build action-oriented reporting, deliver rigorous ad-hoc analyses, and meaningfully improve visibility into our protein supply chains.

An ideal candidate is deeply curious, a systems thinker, and takes ownership over the analytical needs of the teams they support. This is a high-impact, high-visibility role for someone who wants to see their work drive critical business decisions.

Responsibilities


Build and maintain actuals vs. forecast reporting across key protein categories, highlighting variances, root causes, and emerging trends to support proactive decision-making.
Develop data models and reporting to monitor the cost and quality of proteins throughout the supply chain.
Partner with finance and commercial teams to reconcile assumptions across forecasts, budgets, and actual performance.
Support yield and conversion reporting (e.g., raw to sellable yield, trim loss, waste) to improve transparency into cutter performance and efficiency.
Track key product performance metrics—such as repurchase rate, cannibalization, and seasonality—and deliver in‑depth analysis that enables teams to understand SKU‑level performance.
Support scenario analysis and “what‑if” modeling (e.g., demand changes, yield adjustments, cost inflation) to help the team assess risk and opportunity before issues materialize.
Build and continuously improve dashboards that track core procurement metrics (including inventory weeks on hand, product weights, and unit cost) to enable fast, confident decision-making.


Qualifications


3+ years of experience in an analytical role. Experience in procurement/supply chain/demand planning preferred.
Strong technical skills across analytics and data tooling (Preferred: familiarity with Databricks SQL, Developing Power BI, or similar cloud-native analytics environments).
Ability to synthesize complex information into clear, actionable insights and influence key decision makers.
Deep curiosity and desire to learn.
Bias toward action – strong visualizations are important, but analysis needs to drive change.
Ability and willingness to commute to our Watertown, MA office 2-3 times per week.


What Success Looks Like

In your first year, you’ll own our core procurement dashboards, have helped build at least one net-new data model that improves supply chain visibility and purchasing decisions, and be the go-to analytical partner for the procurement team.

Salary - $103,000-127,000

What We Offer

Our Values

We are a mission-driven company and strive to embody our values in every step of the process. ButcherBox is driven by relentless improvement, accountability, humility, and customer obsession. We want our workplace to be one of growth and learning where everyone can be safe/comfortable to show up as their authentic self to work. Anyone who loves our products and goals is welcome here! We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer aspiring to incite positive change in the pastures, the fulfillment centers, the office, and the world.

Benefits


Health: medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, an employer-funded HSA, and short & long-term disability benefits
Financial: 401(k) with generous employer match
Annual bonus based on company performance
Time off: unlimited paid time off policy, 20 weeks parental leave, an executive team that encourages a good work/life balance
Personal growth: Annual Learning & Development stipend, and access to a range of personal and professional coaching solutions through BetterUp
Food: a free Custom Classic ButcherBox each month and weekly lunch reimbursement
Others: reimbursement for wellness activities, an exceptional EAP, fertility benefits, life insurance and more


ButcherBox is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive environment at all steps in the hiring process. Every qualified applicant will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. We are happy provide reasonable accommodations during our hiring process. Please click here if you need to request an accommodation for your interview.

ButcherBox does not use artificial intelligence (AI) tools in any part of our hiring process. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview:

The Master Data Analyst is a member of the Master Data (MD) Operations team and is responsible for ensuring Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, maintained, and governed in accordance with company data standards, internal control requirements, and defined Service Level Agreements (SLAs). This role supports accurate and timely data to enable efficient operations, reliable reporting, and sound business decision-making across the enterprise.

This position reports to the Master Data Manager and Master Data Supervisor and operates in a hybrid work environment in our Burlington, MA headquarters.

Position Responsibilities


Ensure Customer, Vendor, and Finance master data is created, validated, maintained, and governed per company data standards, governance policies, internal controls, and SLAs.
Process new requests and change requests for master data objects including customers, vendors, and finance data (e.g., GL, Cost Centers, Profit Centers), ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and required approvals.
Maintain master data hierarchies, mappings, and reference data to support operational, financial, and reporting needs.
Review and approve master data updates and perform periodic data cleansing and data quality reviews.
Support business projects, system enhancements, testing, and validation activities related to master data and Master Data Management (MDM) solutions.

Title: Data and Compliance Specialist (300195)
Description:
Bedford Public Schools

Data and Compliance Specialist

Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Education, Public Administration, or a related field.
Three to five years of experience administering student information systems, educational databases, or district data systems.
Experience with Aspen, PowerSchool, SchoolBrains, Infinite Campus, or comparable K12 SIS platforms.
Strong knowledge of SQL, database management, reporting tools, and advanced Excel functions.
Experience with educational data analysis and reporting.
Knowledge of FERPA and student data privacy requirements.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and meet strict reporting deadlines.
Successful completion of a Criminal Records Check (C.O.R.I.)


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Massachusetts DESE reporting processes and accountability systems.
Knowledge of educational technology integrations, APIs, and data exchange standards.
Project management experience.
Experience supporting K12 districts or charter school networks.


Reports to: Assistant Superintendent of Teaching and Learning

Job Goal: The Data and Compliance Specialist is responsible for student data management, state reporting, regulatory compliance, and administration of the Aspen student information system. This position ensures the integrity, security, accuracy, and accessibility of student, staff, and operational data while supporting district decision-making, accountability initiatives, and state and federal reporting requirements.

The role works collaboratively with district leadership, school administrators, information technology staff, special education teams, student services, human resources, and state education agencies to maintain high-quality data systems that support student success and organizational effectiveness.

Work Year & Day: 12 month position. This full time position may require occasional evening or weekend work during major reporting cycles, SIS rollovers, state submissions, or district technology implementations.

Salary: $90 -110k, commensurate with experience.

Benefits: Enjoy generous vacation, holiday, and sick leave, along with comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance. We offer long-term, short-term, and life insurance coverage, flexible health and dependent care spending accounts, and a municipal retirement plan, plus a variety of additional optional retirement savings options.

Performance Responsibilities

Student Information System (SIS) Administration


Serve as the district administrator for Aspen.
Configure, maintain, and troubleshoot SIS modules including enrollment, attendance, scheduling, grading, transcripts, discipline, and parent portals.
Manage user accounts, permissions, security roles, and system access.
Coordinate SIS upgrades, testing, and implementation of new features.
Provide technical support and training to school and district staff.
Develop and maintain system documentation, procedures, and user guides.


Data Management and Analytics


Ensure the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of student, staff, and program data.
Design, develop, and maintain reports, dashboards, and data visualizations using tools such as Excel, OpenArchitects, or other data visualization platforms.
Support district strategic planning and continuous improvement initiatives through data analysis and reporting.
Perform data validation, auditing, and quality-control processes.


State Reporting and Compliance


Coordinate all required state and federal reporting submissions.
Manage Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education (DESE) reporting requirements, including: SIMS, SCS, EPIMS, and SSDR.
Monitor changes in reporting requirements and ensure district compliance.
Conduct regular audits to identify and correct data discrepancies prior to submissions.
Support district preparation for state, federal, and program audits.


Data Governance and Security


Ensure compliance with: FERPA, IDEA, Section 504, State student privacy regulations, and District data governance policies, etc.
Maintain secure handling and transmission of sensitive student and employee information.
Develop and implement data governance standards and best practices.
Collaborate with IT staff on cybersecurity, backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity planning.


Collaboration and Training


Serve as the primary liaison between schools, district departments, technology teams, and external vendors regarding data systems.
Provide training and professional development related to data entry, reporting, compliance, and SIS functionality.
Support principals and district leaders in interpreting and utilizing data for instructional and operational decision-making.
Participate in district committees and initiatives related to accountability, technology, and data governance.


Essential Functions Of The Job

Must be Skilled In:


Aspen SIS
SQL Server
Microsoft Excel (Advanced)
Google Workspace
Data Warehousing
Data Governance
Reporting and Dashboard Development


Required Competencies


Data Integrity and Quality Assurance
Regulatory Compliance
Analytical Thinking
Attention to Detail
Problem Solving
Project Management
Communication and Training
Customer Service
Collaboration with a wide variety of faculty and staff.
Continuous Improvement


Physical and Environmental Demands: The physical and environmental demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.


Ability to work in a normal school building environment
Valid driver's license.
Ability to travel between school locations as needed.
Ability to sit and work at a computer for extended periods


Position Control: 300195

The Bedford Public Schools is committed to hiring, mentoring, and retaining a diverse, effective, and healthy workforce to support our students and families. We are relentless in our efforts to bolster positive experiences by fostering a culture of belonging and ensuring our employees, especially those from underrepresented backgrounds, have equitable access to the resources and opportunities needed to thrivepersonally, professionally, and academically.

Title: Data Visualization Analyst
Description:
Ocean Spray is hiring for a(n) Data Visualization Analyst! We’re a team of farmers, thinkers, creators, and doers. Whatever your title, whatever your role — it always comes back to this: we’re a farmer-owned co-op where everyone rolls up their sleeves to get the job done. Three maverick farmers started it all — and we’ve been making our own way ever since.

Role

The Data Visualization Analyst role is designed to help the commercial teams extract, summarize and visualize relevant past performance metrics to drive future performance. It requires a creative thinker who has the ability to transform ideas into actionable tolls that help teams deliver their objectives. This role will:


Work across multiple business teams learning how they operate, understanding insights they need to make decisions
Analyze information from multiple data sources, external and internal to identify business drivers that affect growth and efficiency
Design reports, tools and tracking mechanisms
Translate ideas into tangible output


A Day in the Life...


Delivering insights using compelling Power BI or SAC visualization techniques for business team.
Ability to translate business needs into actionable reporting and visualization thru rapid prototyping and storytelling.
Building models and designing experiments that seek to uncover insights that drive performance.
Analyzing complex data made of unstructured, semi-structured and structured datasets to drive insights.
Active participant in designing creative new ways to use data to help commercial team drive growth.
Work in an environment with internal IT, business teams and external vendors to design and scale work-flows, templates and dashboards .


What We Are Looking For


BA/BS in computer science or related field or equivalent relevant experience, preferably with a focus on data visualization
Minimum 5 years of related experience
Extensive experience with Data Visualization using tools like PowerBI (preferred) and Tableau
Demonstrate ability to work effectively with others within a complex, cross-functional and fast paced organization
Excellent communication. Ability to present ideas and insights to business stakeholders
CPG industry knowledge


Education

Bachelor's or University Degree (Required)

Work Experience

At least 3 Years of Experience

Annual Salary

$87,400 - $120,230

The base salary range information above serves as a guideline of the position’s typical hiring range. We value and appreciate what makes you unique and will consider a variety of factors when determining an offer. These factors include, but are not limited to, your skills and experience, external and internal benchmarks, as well as overall company considerations. Certain positions may be eligible for short-term and long-term incentive rewards. We also offer a competitive and comprehensive benefits program that supports all aspects of your health and well-being.

Benefits


Complete insurance package on Day-1 that includes a plethora of health and wellness programs
Health, Dental, and Vision insurance
Health savings account
Flexible spending account
Life and accident insurance
Employee assistance program
Telehealth services
1:1 health coaching
Supportive benefits for all the stages of your life
401(k) with up to 6% Company matching; additional potential discretionary match at year-end
Short-Term Incentive/Performance bonuses
Flexible scheduling options
Vacation pay, up to three weeks of time (pro-rated for your first year of employment)
Holiday pay for 12 holidays
Career development and growth opportunities
Tuition/Education assistance programs
Access to LinkedIn Learning
Scholarship programs for children of employees
Parental leave
Bright Horizons Family Solutions – Back-up care, tutoring, etc.
Adoption assistance
Bereavement leave
Up to $300 fitness reimbursement
Up to $300 massage reimbursement
Employee appreciation events
Employee discounts
Charitable giving

Who We Are

You might have our iconic cranberry juice in your fridge or have gotten into heated holiday debate about what’s better - canned or fresh cranberry sauce. But did you know that the hardworking people growing the superfruit in our products are 700 family farmers that own our cooperative? They entrust us with what is most precious to them to create new and innovative products that will delight consumers and grow this beloved brand today and into the future.

Team Members, Farmers, Consumers And Communities Alike--we Value What Makes Us Unique And Strive To Connect Our Farms To Families For a Better Life By Living Our Values


Grower Mindset – We embrace our grower-owners innovative spirit and heritage through confidence, learning and focus on the future.
Sustainable Results – Guided by purpose, we are focused on delivering results for our grower-owners.
Integrity Above All – We are ethical, doing the right thing for our grower-owners, customers, consumers and each other
Inclusive Teamwork – We build diverse and inclusive teams that strengthen our cooperative.


All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

For MA Applicants – It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. Any employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives.

WHOOP is hiring Product Analysts to embed across the product organization and serve as strategic analytics partners to teams across Healthcare, Algorithms, AI, Hardware, and other product areas. In this role, you will help define how WHOOP measures product success, uncover insights that shape roadmap decisions, and translate complex data into clear recommendations that improve the member experience. You will bring strong analytical rigor, product intuition, and intellectual curiosity to help teams make faster, smarter, and more impactful decisions with data.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product, Engineering, Research, Design, and cross-functional leadership to inform strategy and roadmap decisions with data.
Own the analytical narrative for your product area, illustrated by defining KPIs, refining measurement frameworks, and surfacing insights that drive action.
Support experimentation and feature launches end-to-end: from defining success metrics to post-launch analysis and iteration.
Build scalable, self-serve reporting & tooling that lets your stakeholders make decisions without waiting on you.
Develop deep context on your product domain and use it to ask sharper questions, challenge assumptions, and push the product forward.
Proactively surface insights and recommendations that go beyond the ask; identify opportunities others haven't seen yet.
Collaborate with a high-performance analytics team in a fast-paced environment where intellectual curiosity is the baseline expectation.


Qualifications


You've done this before: 2-6+ years in an analytical role where you owned business-critical metrics or initiatives. (We're hiring across levels; we'll figure out the right fit together.)
You use AI tools proactively and hold AI-assisted work to the same bar as your own.
You can take a vague question, wrangle the right data, isolate insights, and communicate actionable recommendations to stakeholders who aren't data people.
You're a strong storyteller: you know how to make a compelling, concise case for action.
You're technically sharp: advanced SQL is table stakes. Familiarity with modern data stacks (Snowflake, dbt, ELT workflows) and visualization tools (Hex, Sigma, Amplitude, Looker) is a plus.
You have product sense: you think about the member experience, not just the numbers.
You're curious about how things work. Algorithms, hardware, health science: you don't need to be an expert, but you should want to understand.
You're a self-starter who thrives in ambiguity and doesn't wait to be told what to work on.


TEAMS WE'RE HIRING FOR

We’re Looking For Multiple Analysts To Work Across Our Product Teams. During The Interview Process, We'll Work With You To Find The Right Team Fit Based On Your Interests And Strengths


AI Product: The future of intelligent coaching and personalized insights powered by AI/ML.
Algorithms: Where the science meets the product; sleep, recovery, strain, and how algorithmic outputs translate into member value.
Hardware Product: The physical device experience; sensors, wearability, and next-gen hardware.
Healthcare Product: Building WHOOP's Health Operating System; health outcomes, clinical features, personalized wellness.


This role is based in the WHOOP office located in Boston, MA. The successful candidate must be prepared to relocate if necessary to work out of the Boston, MA office.

Interested in the role, but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to still apply! At WHOOP, we believe there is much more to a candidate than what is written on paper, and we value character as much as experience. As we continue to build a diverse and inclusive environment, we encourage anyone who is interested in this role to apply.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-verify to determine employment eligibility

The WHOOP compensation philosophy is designed to attract, motivate, and retain exceptional talent by offering competitive base salaries, meaningful equity, and consistent pay practices that reflect our mission and core values.

At WHOOP, we view total compensation as the combination of base salary, equity, and benefits, with equity serving as a key differentiator that aligns our employees with the long-term success of the company and allows every member of our corporate team to own part of WHOOP and share in the company’s long-term growth and success.

The U.S. base salary range for this full-time position is $100,000 - $155,000. Salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within each range, individual pay is based on factors such as job-related skills, experience, performance, and relevant education or training.

In addition to the base salary, the successful candidate will also receive benefits and a generous equity package.

These ranges may be modified in the future to reflect evolving market conditions and organizational needs. While most offers will typically fall toward the starting point of the range, total compensation will depend on the candidate’s specific qualifications, expertise, and alignment with the role’s requirements.
